### [email redacted] — 2012-04-16T02:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nothing-succeeds-like-success.847/post-15434>

> **Jack Kruse;15474 wrote:** Docs are getting ready to be paid for success. This is one reason i am looking for better answers for certain diseases that we have now

I keep shopping for a doc that might be open to stuff. This last week I got everyone in the fam an appointment with a family doc that I knew socially. We had done athletic/bootcampy stuff together and worked out at the same time/place for a long time. He too wasn't any thinner than the previous two years he'd been busting ass following his own advice. My thought was that since he too was stuck on the weight roller coaster, he might be open to at least listening to what we have been doing.

Boy was I wrong. It was the most depressing thing. Hubby went in for his private appt (probe anyone?) and he wouldn't order any of the tests I knew were needed to evaluate. Wouldn't order expanded cholesterol and when my husband told him what his previous labs were on that front - he got a big statin and "healthy whole grains" speech.

I am trying to figure out a way to assess them a little better before wasting everyone's time and money. Anybody got any ideas on how to filter a little?

Fwiw, I'm looking for someone in our insurance group - so the pay-only docs are going to be a last resort. If I'm paying

out of pocket, I'd rather pay Jack!

I've looked at the paleo/low carb referral sites - nothing useful. There was one in Ft. Collins north of here - but he died last year!

We pay a very large amount of $ for good insurance, I am not settling for mediocre - I'm going to wrestle this damn system to work with me somehow

What kind of questions, what kind of appointments are you making? Should I go in with a litany of ailments so I can get relevant tests?

### chocolate — 2012-04-16T03:02:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nothing-succeeds-like-success.847/post-15444>

Cavemam, in CA, you can go to the pot clinics and get labs written up for you and get the results over the internet. Or the cash and carry clinics are pretty good. Insurance just seems to put a monkey wrench in everything. We learned in Canada to go to the heroin clinics for triage. It seemed to be the fastest and simplest, until we could get below the border. Somehow, it cut the red tape and it was so much faster then the emergency room. The doctors were styled like the patients, and very thrifty about razors. Very chill. lol. The patients were very chill also and you could play checkers and share smokes. Maybe a heroin clinic is in your network. Sort of a hybrid deal.

### Guest — 2012-04-16T14:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nothing-succeeds-like-success.847/post-15538>

> **Jack Kruse;15474 wrote:** Docs are getting ready to be paid for success. This is one reason i am looking for better answers for certain diseases that we have now

What has changed to bring this about?

I believe you will have many successes with people who follow your advice. In just a short time I have no swelling and no pain in my ankles with following your methods. It's a miracle to me because I have had constant swelling and pain in my ankles for over 20 years.

I haven't, as of yet, followed 100 % of the LR, but I am starting to understand the importance of eating 30 min upon waking. The rest of the protocol is quite easy for me. I think giving up coffee will help me eat at the right time of the morn.

I am all for doctors striving for excellence in patient care --with no drugs that is.

### caroline — 2012-04-17T13:36:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nothing-succeeds-like-success.847/post-15842>

I went to my female family Doc today to get some travel scripts. I thought I would just check with her about leptin - blank stare. I asked about cortisol saliva test - didn't know about it. I asked about bioidentical hormones and she said no one in the practice \[12docs\] knew much about that. She is a lovely woman and I think she would work with me - but I will have to come up with all the info and a plan to implement. She asked me how I knew this stuff! We really do have to be in charge of our own Ferrari don't we!

I went to an optometrist today. She was looking at pictures of my eyes. # Thread: Going Naked
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2012-04-13T01:09:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14466>

So, how many of you have changed your wardrobe? I am eying my sweater collection carefully, and frankly, the sweaters are getting nervous. OTOH, I have a new-found love for wool socks. I find I have been padding about with little clothing on, with the house at about 60-65 degrees, and I feel very comfortable all-over with wool socks. What changes have you made?

### AKMan — 2012-04-13T01:51:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14484>

We have a wood-burning stove. The room it's in has to be pretty hot for it to heat the rest of the house. So, as a compromise, I generally just wear a pair of shorts and no shirt or socks. My feet get pretty cold as the floor temp is usually around 50-60 degrees. I also walk around outside in the snow and cold barefoot every evening for a while.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-13T01:57:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14488>

I live in Texas, so definitely no more long sleeves for a very long time!! At work I have to wear pants, but as soon as I get home I put on a sleeveless T shirt and that is IT! I just parade around the house in my underwear. This also works wonders for my relationship! I sleep with no covers, windows open, and no A/C since its been so nice out. I don't know WHAT I'm gonna do when the temps get over 100 lol...

### ealachan — 2012-04-13T04:32:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14544>

I don't wear a coat outside anymore (but I'll wear a hat and gloves if it's really cold out), I dub around the house in a tank top or tshirt most of the time even though I keep the thermostat down around 62, and much to my husband's chagrin I've started opening the window at night when I go to bed...even when it's like 30 degrees out.

### shilohman — 2012-04-13T04:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14552>

> **lsutiger143;14557 wrote:** I live in Texas, so definitely no more long sleeves for a very long time!! At work I have to wear pants, but as soon as I get home I put on a sleeveless T shirt and that is IT! I just parade around the house in my underwear. This also works wonders for my relationship! I sleep with no covers, windows open, and no A/C since its been so nice out. I don't know WHAT I'm gonna do when the temps get over 100 lol...

I am from Texas also, guess a lot of cold baths with ice in them. Oh well.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-13T04:52:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14553>

been naked for 6.5 yrs.....welcome to the naked club

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-13T05:03:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14559>

> **Jack Kruse;14622 wrote:** been naked for 6.5 yrs.....welcome to the naked club

Even when drinking single vineyard reserve cab franc?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-13T05:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14560>

The wine is gone and I am right now in the man room......naked listening to pandora heading out to the pool. I take no prisoners and give no quarter. Just finishing an optimal bulletproof coffee though. Have two big bio hacks to do in AM.

### agatha — 2012-04-13T22:43:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14855>

I had to have an emergency dash to the shops a couple of weeks ago when I realised all my work clothes are too, too hot.

### Birdy — 2012-04-13T23:25:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-14866>

I am finding that many things are too heavy, but I really like the wool socks. I think it is weird for me to be developing a dislike for cotton. It seems to clammy in the cold, and almost drippy when I warm myself up. I prefer thin polyester/nylon or even rayon in the winter.

### jsrhoades — 2012-04-15T03:14:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-15187>

I get some strange looks from the morning walkers when doing circuits of the beautiful lake in my neighborhood. I'm wearing gym shorts, running shoes, and an iPod. It's 45-50 deg F. I'm wishing it were a bit cooler, but others are wearing sweaters, coats, and sometimes hats and gloves. The dogs are smarter. I took a pic of a dog swimming in the lake, chasing the Canadian geese and ducks. He was having a ball. He was in the water for at least of my two circuits, about 30-40 minutes.

### MonteD — 2012-04-15T18:18:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-15313>

Do you realize how much ice were going to need in three months when its 110 outside... lol.

### agatha — 2012-04-15T19:41:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-naked.849/post-15336>

Yes, my bull terrier used to always love splashing in icy water - it made him go completely mad with energy and exuberance. He didn't like a warm bath though!

# Thread: The Blood Type Diet.....Paleo Style!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-type-diet-paleo-style.857/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Momma28 — 2012-04-13T16:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-type-diet-paleo-style.857/post-14671>

Anyone doing the Blood Type Diet? I have been on it now for nearly 6 months and only backed my way into it by noticing how my face looked in the mornings. I am an A positive and we don't do well on red meat, dairy or grains. When I removed these....WOW! I looked ten years younger. Amazing! So I started eating only the foods for my blood type and have consistently gotten younger in appearance. I'll be fifty soon, so every year I can reverse makes me very happy considering I still have 6 children at home, the youngest two being 4 and 18 months. I don't want to look like their grandmother.

I'm now experimenting with chicken, fish and eggs to see how these affect me. Is anyone doing the Leptin reset vegan style? Blood type A is the only one that performs best on a vegetarian diet and I'm seeing it.

Christina

### janagram — 2012-04-13T19:11:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-type-diet-paleo-style.857/post-14753>

I'm a longtime blood type dieter! D'Adamo is great! if he and Kruse got together....it would be so intersting!

Before the bloodtype diet, I was a vegetarian (not vegan!) for 25 yrs and nearly did myself in, ( I'm an O), before waking up to reality!!! My body was crying out for meat!! I am so much healthier on Paleo eating! which is great for bloodtype O! I believe type A is good with grains, but I can see ditching them, even as an A. So, go paleo, type A style, lots of fish and chicken. And eggs!

### chocolate — 2012-04-15T17:39:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-type-diet-paleo-style.857/post-15302>

I am a+ and everything worked for me. I just didn't have a swamp to spear carp from nor anywhere to brouse berries. The goat and lamb is a little tough at times to get your hands on as well. I spent quite a lot of time not eating and just drinking water. I think I learned a lot from the book though. I tested allergic to beef years ago and the allergist was kind of upset. He was really concerned about how inconvenient it would be for me to get the nutrition I needed from red meat. He told me to wait a month and try a small portion of organic beef, I did and was fine. For years though, I just used that deck of card sized rule. The grain fed beef gave me the clue on the paleo. If I can't eat grain, I shouldn't eat something that eats grain. I eat small grain fed portions and large grass fed. feeling much better.

But, alas, my book is lent out the gal doesn't have the book with her. **I was wondering if any of you remember what it was about drinking alcohol that improved the a+ digestion? There is supposed to be something genetic and I was hoping to dig for it over the weekend.**

I can't remember how the diet worked so much though. You guys are going vegetarian and the combination wasn't there for the protein and calories, no matter how I mathed it out. The tempeh was full of garbage and the grain allotment was so minimal it was tough to stock. Plus the tempeh is more expensive than really great meat. For it to be pallatable, it has to be full of junk. The soy makes your gazongas so huge, you can't haul them. (I guess some people do the soy for enhancement) I just know everything it told me to avoid, worked. My dad had food allergies and the stuff he was allergic to was excluded on my A+diet. I wound up avoiding the same things I avoided as a kid, so of course I felt better. Just not good enough. Pork chops make the world beautiful.

### Momma28 — 2012-04-18T18:03:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-type-diet-paleo-style.857/post-16222>

> **Shijin13;14827 wrote:** 4yrs later I eliminated wheat/gluten - to begin my path toward health - but I didn't begin to feel like I didn't have anything wrong w/m until I went primal/paleo - and finally ditched all the grains, and sugar, and added fat and high quality meat back into my diet.
>
>
>
> If it works for you - great that's your N=1; my N=1 on this diet = BS

I have been gluten-free for nearly five years now and as an A+, this is a match. I came to the Blood Type Diet through the back door after keeping a journal of my responses to the foods I was eating. I have been organic for nearly 20 years now and have managed a local organics buying club for more than ten years. I have eaten grassfed beef for more than 10 years and pastured, locally grown chickens and eggs as well. We get local produce, raw honey, raw dairy, both goat and cow(no longer consuming)....you name it, I've done it.

The biggest jump in our health as a family, (Hubby is A+ as well which makes all 8 kids A+) was when we optimized our vitamin D levels and eliminated wheat and casein from our diet. We do not submit to being injected with foreign substances and drink purified water. The kids play outside daily, without shoes and we don't use antibacterial anything. The only illness we deal with is a cold or two per year. More than half of the kids have never been given, or needed, thank God, antibiotics. Hubby is 49 and consistently tests out as an 18 year old on his yearly physical. So, I have an enormous body of evidence that what we are doing works, I just needed to tweak what I was eating of late as I am nearly 50, too.

Age is catching up with my physical body, though I am told my outward looks 10 to 15 years younger. So I only looked at the Blood Type Diet when I began to notice how my face looked in the mirror the morning after consuming the grass-fed beef or dairy of any kind that I might have had out and about. I feel great on a meatless diet, but am wondering if that would just be a short term benefit. I am currently abstaining from red meat and dairy. Aside from the extra wrinkles and puffiness under eyes, I get bad body odor from it as well. It clearly doesn't agree with me chemically/metabolically. The whole family eats paleo style with an occasional rice pasta for an Italian dish. The kids will eat anything placed in front of them and they get two meals a day, with a fruit snack and all have optimal weight for the age and build.

My goal then is to marry the paleo with the blood type to optimize my health and also control blood sugar levels as my anti-aging strategy. I have slowly started adding in CT but very sloooowly. Oooooh, it's so cold.

### chocolate — 2012-04-22T22:48:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-type-diet-paleo-style.857/post-17877>

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Effects\_of\_high\_altitude\_on\_humans](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Effects_of_high_altitude_on_humans)

**The effects of high altitude on humans are considerable. The percentage saturation of hemoglobin with oxygen determines the content of oxygen in our blood. After the human body reaches around 2,100 m (7,000 feet) above sea level, the saturation of oxyhemoglobin begins to plummet.\[1\] However, the human body has both short-term and long-term adaptations to altitude that allow it to partially compensate for the lack of oxygen.** Athletes use these adaptations to help their performance. There is a limit to the level of adaptation: mountaineers refer to the altitudes above 8,000 metres (26,000 ft) as the "death zone", where no human body can acclimatize.

# Thread: Martin's Story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 531
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2012-04-13T21:49:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-14826>

I'm a male, 59, 138 lbs. and a retired (emphasis on "tired") old house restoration contractor.

I've had a long history of low-grade health problems since I can remember. Fevers, muscle aches (my Mom called them "growing pains"), cold-natured, lethargy, mental fogginess, coming alive as the day wore on, and always wanting to stay up late and then sleep late the next morning. I saw others play harder, learn faster, and have more friends than me and got picked on a little by others. Because they looked down on me for being "weak" and "stupid", I eventually blamed myself for just not being "good enough".

I had my moments of clarity and courage and went on to get married, help raise my stepson, and have a great career restoring old Craftsman bungalows in Atlanta for a number or years. It was rough work and it wore at my 140 lb. frame, but it was by sheer force of will and a love for those old houses that got me through. Joint pain and slow healing wounds, along with a lack of energy, drove me to do research on the net. It led me to look at my past history in a new way.

I remember my Mom talking about her sub-normal temps, aches, weakness, low energy level, and she was always struggling mentally. She started me on a path of natural healing that I continue to practice to this day.

I eat organically, fresh air, spring water, saunas, and relaxation strategies, and now, Paleo eating and eventually, Leptin Reset and Cold Thermogenesis.

I am through blaming myself and allowing others to blame me for being "weak minded", "not good enough", "it's all in your attitude", and other bull\*\*\*\*. There is a part of this that is spiritual, of course, and I'm addressing it. But there is a physical part of feeling lousy and that's why I am here. It's called epigenetics! I'm fixing the bio-mechanical machine that is our body through what I have learned here and other sites such as the Weston Price Foundation, the Doctor Whithin, Hair Analysis and Mineral Balancing - Dr. Larry Wilson, and others.

I am coming at the Reset, CT, et al, from a biotoxin illness place. Recently dx'ed with two genetic defects: mold exposure and low aMSH. These explains so much about my life.

I found Dr. Kruse's work through a chance question on a WPF forum and dove right in. I have always searched for a well-researched method of diagnosing imbalances and Kruse is the man. I feel so fortunate to learn from him and look forward to a life restored, just like my old houses.

Martin

### shilohman — 2012-04-13T21:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-14830>

Way to go Martin, welcome aboard.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-13T23:28:22+0300

### Martin — 2012-04-15T15:33:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-15279>

Mold doc visit went very well. The four-month wait for the appointment was finally over.

I had read up on the treatment in "Mold Warriors" by Dr Ritchie Shoemaker and knew what to expect and more importantly, what I wanted to take away from the first visit - a script for cholestyramine.

This ancient cholesterol drug is not absorbed into the body. It just absorbs fatty stuff and, along with it, the biotoxins that have been circulating in my body for a long time creating an unchecked feedback loop of cytokine release and the resulting misery.

I feel as if I've had the flu for three years. Painful joints, headaches, vicious leg cramps, unrelenting fatigue, and a gathering hopelessness. Like Dr. Kruse said, chronic pain will change your life.

Doc took a nasal swab to test for a nasty infection that is sometimes present in us Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (CIRS) sufferers. The tiny little Q-tip looked so small and innocuous until it was several inches back o' my nostril. Then it felt like a cotton-covered baseball bat. A quick dab or two and it was all over. At least no bending over this trip.

I learned from the doc that the VIP testing (vaso-intestinal peptide, a very important marker for mold illness) is currently going haywire among the testing labs out there and the test results cannot be relied upon. $75 wasted? It's all good. The way out is the way through. There are plenty of other tests to provide a path.

I had introduced the good doc to Dr. Kruse's work a few months ago and have mentioned how CT raises aMSH, another important (extremely!) hormone in everyone, let alone mold folks. Mine is

### freesia — 2012-04-16T00:43:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-15402>

Hi Martin,

Do you mean your saw Ritchie Shoemaker? Or another Mold doc who works with Dr Shoemaker's protocols?

I know a few people using CSM, they seem to think it's effect is potentiated by other measures, extreme mold avoidence in some cases or other detoxification support. I also think CT could work very well with CSM. I'm really interested to see how you do!

Did you get significant mold exposure while renovating? I guess with that genotype any exposure was going to make you sick?

I googled Atlanta craftsman bungalows because I was curious, and I love old houses. Nice! Not _too_ different from bungalows built here, in NZ, in the 20's.

Best to you!

### Martin — 2012-04-18T21:34:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-16318>

Hi freesia, I found a mold doc who follows Shoemaker's work. I started CSM two days ago and no changes yet. I am curious about using other clearing supplements like chlorella. Do you know if those people you mentioned used it?

I know I was exposed like crazy during the remodeling, but it wasn't anything I haven't been exposed to for 12 years. The house is not water-damaged, and we have a very dry basement, but the mold grew on the backsides of the rough-sawn pine interior boards that were on the wall. Humidity over the years and poor construction would let air thru from the outside and deposited mycotoxins on the inside. Ugh.

I theorize many would get respiratory issues if exposed, but would quickly clear once they left the area. But if you have haplotypes, watch out! I'm still wondering how I did as well as I did all the years up here. I actually got very healthy 3 years ago thru watching diet, etc., but then something really kicked my a\*\* - bad!!! I have not recovered from that episode and the search for healing brought me here. I thought it was Lyme's and that brought Shoemaker's work up, but I forgot it until Dr. Kruse referenced him. Bingo, I'd found my path thru that I could trust.

Got more blood tests back.

TGF-beta 10,820 pg/ml range: 344-2,382

VIP 42 pg/ml range: 0-58

ACTH 14 pg/ml range: 7.2-63.3

TGF-beta is in line with CIRS (chronic inflammatory response syndrome) range.

VIP is probably worthless due to current lab difficulties going on presently according to my doc in Atlanta.

ACTH ? not sure what to relate this to yet.

New Zealand! Always wanted to be a Kiwi, albeit an import!

### Martin — 2012-04-23T14:53:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-18028>

Ninth day in CSM and not doing well. More aching and discomfort than before. Constipation is a side affect of CSM and they ain't kiddin'. Doing steps to improve "flow".

Contacting mold doc to see if other tests are back yet and whether there is some strange thing going on.

I seem to respond very little to ANY therapy I've ever done thru my search for relief. Pills, tablets, tinctures, decoctions, all go down and nothing resembling a big change happens. I read success stories and about new (and old) therapies and get really good inspiration, but don't seem to fall in line with the healing crowd.

More testing will show me the path through this. The answer is there in my blood.

Good luck. I'll keep checking back to see how you're doing so don't leave. You have found a place with lots of good support.

### Martin — 2012-04-24T02:27:37+0300

Hi Darleen,

All grains? Only brown rice sometimes mixed into my veg/beef soup. And sometimes rice crackers with sesame seeds on them (makes them palatable!) just to have some sort of CHANGE from beef, broccoli, beets, and bacon.

I read "Dangerous Grains" and got tested, but results were not high. I went off wheat, rye, oats, and corn anyway after reading Dr. Kruse.

No gluten for six months. I could tighten it up and eliminate ALL grains, though.

Thank you for the book suggestion. That's what great about this forum. People do seem to care and want to spread the knowledge and experiences.

Speaking of that, you sang in a rock band! COOL! You are inspiring me with your life story.

I want to play in a band before I die. Country prolly, and sing harmony behind my quite talented wife. Too fatigued right now. Haven't even picked up my guitar in months. Time is slipping...

### Martin — 2012-04-25T19:06:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-18765>

I got some good news from my testing. No Marcons! It's a nasty nasal infection that constantly triggers cytokine releasing. You may not even have a runny nose!

And so far, I am not having to take Actos, an effective, but sometimes risky drug (when taken long-term) that does a lot of good when used off-label to treat moldy, lymie people.

Eating lots of prunes these days.

### Martin — 2012-04-28T17:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-19575>

I spent part of the day yesterday in the garden finally planting some raspberry and asparagus crowns I ordered. I almost decided not to get them, but if you don't act on it when spring comes, you waste an entire year. It's not like a tupperware bowl you can just go out and buy anytime you want one. Timing is everything.

Having said that, I left the poor things imprisoned in their little shipping box for over a week as I struggled to get more pressing things done with my limited energy. Rental house madness. Believe me, having to dig deep holes and putting in lots of plants is much easier than dealing with a socio-pathic tenant. People can suck.

While I was out digging my holes, I had a slight inkling of a lessened painful reaction to getting out and working. Just barely perceptible, but enough to get my attention.

When you feel crappy for so long, even the slightest improvement is noticed. In the past twenty years, I have called upon these relaxed, warm, comfortable, can't-feel-my-aching-ass-body, instances for solace and inspiration, and it spurs me on. Is this how normal people feel?! I can only barely remember what that's like and it has been getting fainter by the year.

I want to be feeling so good and free of pain that I am "outside" my body, ahead in my plans, and excited about my dreams - like I used to be as a child. All I think about these days is how I feel and what the hell I ought to be doing about it. I know I'm overstating it, but it's a large part of what occupies my mind.

This morning my arms and legs ache and I can feel the fatigue slipping in. Not to worry! I push through and do what I have to do, albeit at a minimum. Bills need paying? Write a check. Hair's on fire? Put it out. Then go back to resting and shoving my nose into research on what to do next to get well.

I got back my Comprehensive Metabolic Panel and all of the 20 or so items are in range. No major values high or low is a good thing, if the ranges are anything to trust. A few were on the low side, a couple on the high, but most are in the middle. I'm sorting and researching(!) to find out what they mean.

Most of my testing comes back "normal". My FBG is always good, no renal problems, gluten sensitivity

### Martin — 2012-04-29T17:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-19786>

Bad crash yesterday afternoon. I suspect it's the parasite cleanse (mimosa pudica) and the constipating effects of the cholestyramine. In bed for hours, never did recover before the night was over. Flu-like feeling I always have was heightened big-time.

I did not do night dose of CSM, doc said back off if it gets too rough.

Hammered today, but got to go plant my garden. The seasons wait for no one.

Doc said that was ok since I am very sensitive to anything. Go slow and be patient as my doc would say to me. I am axious for getting better, but need to listen to his advice. I hope today is better.

### Martin — 2012-05-04T15:12:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-21291>

Day 18 on Cholostyramine and a slight improvement?

Less ache and my attitude a tad better?

Lasted nearly all day in various degrees. Man! I'll take it!

Almost reached for the face-dunking bowl, but I have to be patient and follow the protocol out of this mess.

Next day the chronic ache returned, but I can see the gift in it by the contrast and how I feel. Movement in a chronic pain condition is a good thing.

It's been three years since no pain and I'm getting excited about returning to a better life. Dr Kruse's work on raising aMSH and other hormones through CT has given me a effective pathway to get to optimal and live out my life like a child again.

My niece has gotten interested in Paleo and is starting to embrace the ancestral ways! It is a good feeling to see someone get on board and change their lives. She has severe digestive issues and the docs say it's "stress" and "psychosomatic". I'm having her send me all her testing to see what I can and encouraged her to join us here.

I am eyeing a stock tank from the local feed store and want to put it on the deck for CT-ing this spring and summer. I can drain it down to my garden or landscape when water needs changing. Plant CT!

Oops. I posted something on ASK JACK and realized afterwards that it was not the right forum. Sure enough, got a reply as such from the good doc, but he answered anyway. Thanks, Doc! I had wondered for over a month about my question and it's cleared up.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-05-08T00:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-22039>

Hey Martin, Just wanted to send some good vibes your way! Hope you're having some more good days.

### Martin — 2012-05-13T17:08:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-23657>

Thanks Riv, I can use the good thoughts.

After a month, I'm nearly out of CSM and no changes. Emailed doc for further details since next appt. isn't until second week in June.

Trying to not be disappointed and keep on plugging away. It's been a long painful slog through this crap. Decades.

Despite mixed reviews on efficacy, started taking chlorella for helping to clear biotoxins. Hell, I'll stand on my head if it helps.

When I get up in the morning, arms and legs ache first, then as the day wears on, my whole body. I go about doing what is in front of me that is most important and plug away. The really strong fatigue sets around 5 or 6, just in time for me to fix dinner. I push through, clean up the kitchen, then crash on the couch until bedtime.

Get up the next day and do it all again.

This is my life. No gardening, working in my orchard, building a project in the shops, or playing music. I do what absolutely has to be done and no more. My memory is getting worse and I'm forgetting things that I used to hold in my thoughts since childhood. They are going away.

My weight is own to 135 pounds from a record lifetime high of 150 lbs. I started the Leptin Reset about six months ago, lost the belly weight, and then saw a post about not doing the LR until biotoxins were cleared.

Great. More waiting. No LR or Ct.

Still eating paleo, paying attention to light, snacking, and other principals.

I turn 60 this summer. The run to the end needs to be free of pain and worry.

I'm doing all I can to get there.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-13T17:22:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-23664>

stay strong and never quit. Your journey is tougher because of the hand you were dealt so the conquest will be that much sweeter.

### Martin — 2012-05-13T22:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-23737>

Thanks for the encouragement, Doc. I agree about the sweetness of victory- it will be good to revel in it!!! You really show your good nature by responding to so many of us folks out here. It is not lost on us.

Hi freshveggies, no, I have not heard of nagalese. I have read betterhealthguy, but not lately. Thanks!

I did 24 hr adrenal saliva and it's low, but always in the bottom of the range. Thyroid is low, and I have tracked temps, symps, etc., before I came to see from Dr. Kruse's work the underlying inflammatory conditions are running the show. I've taken Armour and HC with little benefit, and moved on.

I really believe there is a workable remedy in there somewhere, and testing will reveal it. I have learned so much so far!

Got this from my mold doc:

"C4a is high as expected. This should decrease with remediation and CSM. (I have asked him for the number)

VEGF is good (More good news!)

ADH is on the low side. This could improve with remediation and CSM. Sometimes, we need to use ADH supplementation.

New tip!: You can run a 5 species ERMI for only $125 through Mycometrics. Make sure to write "PCR 10.5" on the test order form. It focuses on the important parts of the ERMI so that you save money."

The last bit is very cool. I have four mold strains that were high and this will help with the costs to see where they are. Time to research. Thanks again, River!

Martin

### Martin — 2012-05-16T17:33:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-24598>

Almost out of CSM. No improvement that I can hang a hat on yet.

I have a doc nearby that offers IV glutathione treatments and I'm considering giving it a try. She also offers HBOT at a reasonable price. I'm looking at whatever will give me a leg up on getting better, or at least relieve the chronic ache.

Headaches are back. Had an uncomfortable night with them, but I can usually turn over and get into another position and it will lessen.

Today I will work on my clearing out the rest of my studio to complete the mold remediation. It has been remodeled in the recent past and will only require a thorough cleaning vs. the total rebuilding like the front rooms did. Glad that's over!

One little win I've noticed is the fact that my middle fingernail is no longer splitting in the same damn place. It's been going on for a decade now and was a chronic signal that something was going on and drove me to get online and search the cause.

I know, it's ridiculous, but I'm glad for ANY movement in the right direction.

The splitting nail, and a few other annoying symptoms, led me to a succession of clues and remedies and finally, here to Dr. Kruses's work. Funny how little things can pull you along and then big things are revealed.

Added a cup of decaf coffee to breakfast this fine morn. Instead of cream, I tried coconut milk.

Blick.

Ain't the same as my beloved cream.

If I could easily get unpasteurized cream, I'd do it. I can, but it's a fair amount of trouble to drive to a local dairyman. Maybe when I get doctored up and feeling better, I'll splurge.

### Martin — 2012-05-23T18:18:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-26701>

Last week, I email doc with an update and mentioned I was getting low on CSM (cholestyramine). Didn't hear back, sent another email. Got a response to some other questions, but nothing on CSM. I saw where there is "No refills" on container so I figured the doc was in charge of continuing with treatment with drug. I am not a drug taker and have never refilled a prescription in my life so I'm naive when it comes to this drug stuff.

Ran out.

Called doc's office (shoulda done that first, instead of email) and asked if doc was refilling script. Turns out he forgot to check the "refill" box!

During the time I was without I seemed to get worse - more fatigue, more aches, and the resulting mental challenges from going backwards. Not a good sign. If one goes off the drug and is still in a moldy structure, the symptoms can amplify. I may have experienced this. This means I may eventually have to move from my beloved farm and all that I have done to this place - big garden, huge garden shed, an orchard that is just now coming into full bearing, remodeled house, tricked out workshops, landscaping, and the privacy of 35 acres of beautiful bliss. All because of friggin' mold.

Started back on CSM yesterday. I guess we'll see what's next.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-23T21:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-26794>

Martin if you have to move maybe a drier climate would suit you better. Just a thought.

and can you have butter? If so, then you might want to try the bullet proof coffee a lot of us are in love with. Easy recipe ... a blender works best I think. Put your butter into the blender ... adjust for taste. Me? I prefer 4TBS but I'm a pig. My hubby uses 2TBS. The guy who has popularized it uses more, as I understand it. Whatever floats yer boat. Add 2 cups of hot coffee and sweetener if you use it.

CAREFUL NOW. Hot liquids in a blender can and will blow the top off spewing hot liquid everywhere. I put a damp dishcloth over the lid, put the blender on PULSE and GENTLY tap the stir button a few times, then I press liquify and take it off pulse. Whir for a few seconds until well blended, pour and ENJOY. Best darned latte you will ever drink. And no casein. Butter is just cream with no liquid.

### Martin — 2012-05-23T22:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-26807>

Darleen, moving to a drier climate? it may come to that. I've thought about it a lot. I love where I am, but if it's killing me...

Oooh.... your recipe sounds good. Thanks!

Yes, pastured butter is back on the table as I follow the seasons. Four tablespoons don't sound like nearly enough. I'll try it soon.

Feeling the "flu" today as usual. Low aMSH really perks up the pain.

My cognitive abilities are eroding. I've never been a great typer, but it's now a challenge not to transpose letters and forget spelling. My wife has to "bullet point" all commands and instructions to me because I get lost with too much information. Or is because I'm a guy?! "Bar, nine o'clock, bring cash"."

Next doc appointment June 7. It will be interesting to see where my C4a is after six weeks of... make that five, CSM.

### Martin — 2012-05-25T16:07:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-27311>

My T is 420 in a range of about 300 to 800, but I'm guessing 'cause the file is back in my studio and I don't want to get up from my couch and leave my green tea. I'd like to see it higher, of course.

I am going to get another 24 hr cortisol test on the next appointment because I feel unusually tired. It's like I'm melting into the ground. The last time I felt this way I was on HC and when I needed dosing, I would get extremely tired.

That was over four years ago. I got thru it and went on to feel the best I have ever felt before the Great Sickness came upon me. I really wish I could figure out what that was. Slight sore throat, felling the flu-like ache, and I thought I would get over it because I was feeling the strongest I'd felt in ten years.

Boy, was I wrong. I got the sickest I've ever been in this life. Terrible. Ain't been right since. I got better-ish, but it took two weeks. Three years of this s\*\*t.

What's strange is that I was living in the same house all this time and if it is mold-related, and the testing shows that it is, why do I get so strong and then crash and burn? It's just a curiosity I have, not important other then to know. I like to "know" stuff.

Today, I'm going to do my second Visual Contrast Sensitivity test (VCS) that I bough online from Dr. Shoemaker's site. It's easy to do and I can compare it with my first one to see where I am. My mold doc says our goal is to improve that score and monitor the C4a blood test to see if it goes down.

Those tests are biggies.

From Shoemaker's site: "C4a has become the inflammatory marker of greatest significance looking at innate immune responses in those with exposure to Water Damaged Buildings.

### Martin — 2012-06-03T15:46:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-29195>

I took my second Visual Contrast Sensitivity test yesterday.... and passed! Mind you, it's only a screening test, but can help show what's happening. I compared the chart of the previous test I took many months ago before taking cholestyramine and saw where the scores were better. Progress.

I will take an advanced VCS test at the mold doc's and it will be interesting to compare results. We will also test C4a to see where it is.

My symptoms, however, have not improved much at all. I might have a stretch of hours where I don't feel terrible, but generally I feel terrible all the time. Even when I get up in the middle of the night to go to the bathroom (which happens a lot with my low Anti-Dieuretic Hormone - pee, pee, pee, all the time!), I feel the Ache.

I did have a good afternoon several days ago and I used it to go plant my garden and ended up working 'til dark, showering, and then off to bed.

Next day I crashed. That's what happens to biotoxin illness folks. Oxygen is not getting into the cells and glucose is not making energy very efficiently so we burn it up, crash, and it takes several days to get some energy back. Whoopee-effing-s\*\*t.

THAT is where the rubber meets the road. I can't make energy to work, heal, or have good cognitive abilities.

But I gotta tell ya, KNOWING what the pathways are - both what is going on and how to address it - give me a lot of solace. I'm not in the mystery of "why" any more. When my thoughts go dark, and then I can get out of them quickly because I have solid answers as to the questions I've had all my life as to why I am the way I am.

Doc visit in five days.

P.S. I feel a little awkward posting here because I can't do the leptin reset or CT while everyone else is charging on ahead with these breakthru therapies and getting great results. And discussing Factor X, epigenetics, and other stuff leaves my moldy brain behind because I just can't hold it in my head. I don't feel bad about it, but I seem to be a little out of place.

But I feel that this is my community. No other forum has attracted me in such a strong way. Finally, a competent doctor (an understatement, to be sure) sharing his findings, what tests to do to find out where you are, and a pathway not only out of the mess, but a way to OPTIMAL!

### Martin — 2012-06-03T21:26:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-29249>

Hi vkiernan. Thanks for the encouragement! I have to say I've been wanting to write you after I read that you have stage 7 AF. That hit me hard when I read it. I CANNOT imagine. No way. I am truly sorry that you are going thru that.

About five years ago, I figured I was stage 4 to 5. Did some mineral balancing therapy thru hair analyses and four years of organic living. Then the Great Crash 3 years ago.

I thought it was Lyme. But a few things did not fit and I never found a doc that could help (imagine that!) and kept looking.

Long story a little shorter, I found the Doc. And when he wrote the Lyme/Leptin blog, I remembered a forum friend who mentioned Dr Shoemaker. With Kruse's 30k view, I remember what that forum member had said she thought I had - mold illness.

I had dismissed it, thinking I would have really crashed long before now since I worked on old houses for so long. But I started thinking, just maybe...

Here's what turned my head around - the **HLA DRB DRBQ genetic test. Result: **susceptible to mold was one, and low aMSH** was another**. The low aMSH TOTALLY explains my life!!!!!!! WOW! So many things! My mom and I share so many traits and I'm certain she had that as well and passed it to me.

My C4a test is a great marker for Lyme and mold sufferers. It's supposed to be below about 2300, mine is over 10,000! Some people have tested 40,000!! There are more - VEGF, MMP9, VIP - that confirm my illness.

I finally found a mold doc near me. I was ready to travel to see Dr. Shoemaker and just spend my retirement until I was well. This doc is good and has mold illness as well.

I'm not sure about my adrenals. I've done two 24 hour saliva tests and they were about the same - start out on the low edge of the scale and then uptick in the evening, but still low-ish. Lately, I sometimes feel like I'm just melting into the ground. Not always, though.

I would rec testing HLA and aMSH right away. Best $450 I ever spent! I self-pay as I have no insurance and have a local lab that does it without a script.

If you can, get Shoemaker's book, "Surviving Mold" and you may see yourself in there. EVERYTHING I have going wrong is in that book. It is the Pathway for me. For the first time in decades, I have much more peace in my life.

### Martin — 2012-06-04T02:30:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-29271>

Last week I went to a local doc to try hyperbaric oxygen therapy and IV glutathione. I've been reading about HBOT for years and was excited to get the chance. Mold doc Shoemaker says it doesn't help, but I figured it may for other things other then biotoxin illness. But I was really interested in the IV glut so I committed to the nearly hour-long drive.

HBOT tank was interesting, and I'm lucky I'm not claustrophobic. After a few minutes, the air whistling in started to get annoying so I put a pillow between it and me. Then my ears began to pop and started to be a bit painful. I swallowed like I have done on airplane flights and that seemed to help.

Not for long.

The pain got a lot worse and I grabbed the pressure relief valve and cranked that sucker open which alerted the nurse who came in and adjusted the pressure down to a point where I could tolerate it.

I was determined to get thru the whole hour, and while I did, it was not a fun time.

IV glutathione was a breeze compared to the cramped little space capsule.

Had some great sushi on the way home.

**Two days later:**

The second HBOT session started out okay, but it wasn't long before my damn ear was lighting up. The nurse was checking on me regularly and ramped up the pressure slowly, but we never got to full steam.

I was uncomfortable, but determined to get my O2 fix.

Oh, let's not stop there. Despite going to the bathroom right before getting in, my microscopic bladder started talking to me after only ten minutes.

"I can do this, just relax into the pee, feel the yellow....", I thought.

Torture. My illness has my ADH - anti diuretic hormone - low so I didn't have a chance. I lasted 48 and a half minutes before frantically waving to the nurse thru the porthole with the _slashing-across-the-throat-let's-stop-this-madness hand signal.

De-pressurization took what seemed to be nine days and it was a good lesson in steeling one's will. Mind over bladder.

I calmly got out, comforted by the fact that I was not longer incarcerated (albeit willfully) and knowing that I could go to the bathroom anytime I wanted to. I tried to be cool, but I think my desperation showed as I sprinted for the restroom door only to see an elderly lady entering and slowly shutting the door.

"_God, take me now_.", I mumbled.

The doc asked how I was doing as I sat waiting to pay (not pee, this is later) and offered to have a look at my ear. She whipped out her trusty ear thingy and actually gasped when she peered in there. She put in some drops and handed me what looked like a obese tv remote and told me to hold it on my ear. It was lit up and flashing like a pinball machine and I complied dutifully. Some kind of light therapy, me thinks.

Not going to do the HBOT anymore. Can't take it. Bummer, I have wanted to do this for a long time and I'm just too wimpy. Canary, coal mine.

IV glut treatment is wednesday. That_ I can handle.

Then there's that sushi bar on the way home...

### Martin — 2012-06-15T15:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-31310>

Two months on cholestyramine so far. No major improvement, but the doc says his patients take 3 to 6 months to start showing improvement.

I did have a decent day yesterday, at least compared to the last year or so. I have learned (the hard way) to rest in the afternoon for an hour or two to help heal things up.

I'm taking vasopressin, a hormone drug that helps me retain water and plump up my cells so they will do their job of intake and exhaust better. I have to pee all the time and this will help me not have to get up 58 times a night.

When I feel good, my attitude gets better to the point where I can dream a little about life again. That's one of the things I miss.

We are considering selling this farm and down-sizing to something more manageable as we approach our later years. This is a beautiful, rolling hilled, forest and pasture, dotted with outbuildings, remodeled, private, 35 acre dreamscape. But the palette is too big for this guy to create on anymore. I need to take the best and leave the rest so I can focus on a few things rather than starting 97 projects and completing none.

I will miss the garden and orchard, but one can start another. Workshops? Have another built and it won't be a nasty, mold infested(?) old converted barn that I am getting more and more apprehensive to go into.

Dreams? We'll see.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-15T16:28:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-31321>

CT plus CSM is the ticket.....to fat liberation while doing HBOT. Keep going!

### Tizawee — 2012-06-15T17:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-31334>

Martin - Please don't feel bad about posting here. You are helping us as much as we are helping you. Who knows, someone here may see their struggle in your writings and find out that what they thought was, say, a hormone issue turns out to be a mold issue.

Your farm sounds lovely. A shame to give it up but, as a chronic pain sufferer, I \*do\* understand.

Welcome to Optimal Living!

Elin

Thanks to all. It's encouraging to hear from others. Wish we had a "Like" button!

I spoke with an old friend today who's wife has fibromyalgia and bad back and neck problems, and he is pre-diabetic, fat, addicted to carbs, and it was gratifying to be able to share a way through their difficulties by sending him a trusted, logical, and inspiring source of health - this site. At last, I can reccomend a way to heal! I have never done much suggesting when it comes to health because I didn't trust the info... like I do here.

### Martin — 2012-06-22T00:46:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-32829>

Went for follow-up blood tests - BMP, and, for Osmolality, a test that measures the concentration of all chemical particles found in the fluid part of the blood.

Ductwork gets cleaned next week and in about a month, the mold re-test for the house gets done. Make or break on that one. Do I stay or do I go?

I have put a tremendous amount of work and thought into this old 35 acre farm, and the thoughts of leaving are a challenge, but less so these days because I'd move to the backside of the moon if it meant not feeling like crap. NO place is worth this.

I had a decent day so far. It's early afternoon and I am feeling a bit less ache and more importantly, less distraught and depressed. I actually made some plans for the future!

### Martin — 2012-07-14T03:12:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-37651>

In my quest for better health in this life, I have longed for a trusted, accurate, and interesting medical source to inspire me to take action to get well.

Why did I suffer so much? Did I do something to "deserve" this? Many have told me that "we create our own reality", and I "chose" this body and this life. I wanted to believe that I could change my body with my thoughts... and a few organic veggies. And when I didn't thrive, I figured I hadn't "created" the right thoughts.

I've done this therapy, and tried that pill, but it never seem to make much of a difference. Others would get fantastic gains and tell glowing stories of the "new" person they've become, but I could rarely boast in such a way. I'd tried some local alternative docs, regular docs, and spent thousands. Quit them all.

Year three of my chronic pain/flu symptoms was telling me that I was not going to sleep it away, wish it away, heal it away by the good habits that I had adopted - WPF diet, organic foods, etc., or, the fact that I'd become more at peace with myself and others. All that good stuff was not going to change the fact that my tired ol' ass hurt twenty-four/seven. Like the good doc said, chronic pain will change your life.

Where do I go for help?

Who "sees" true health, and the pathway to get there, and, can I trust them?

What information is biologically accurate, and not politicized/bastardized/corporatized?

Dr. Jack Kruse is what.

The first blog entry I read of his was, "What is Peripheral Neuropathy", I was hooked. The man knows his s\*\*t.

Every post and tidbit is exciting because I trust the source. No more dogma-do. No corp/pol/profitized agenda.

I want to shout it from the treetops. And have, in my own little way, but few are interested. I find that amazing. If I heard that someone has a 30k view and can think like a three-demensional hormonal chess player, I'd effing pay attention and sit on their head until they gave it up. "Hungry" for the pathway to Optimal does not describe it eloquently enough.

I don't post much, and while the blog entries provoke many thoughts, I am so cognitively challenged right now I can't ask many "smart" questions. I like how others can dive deep into the science and pathways and ratios and I eventually get a sense of how it all works. I don't need to know all the details, even though I wish I was sharp enough to be able to! Soon!

But it's all good. I'm glad to be where I am, and the excitement for my journey is building.

I see now that the thoughts I need to "create" are ones that are informed, tested, measured, and with a dose of hard science, I then pull those together and decide on action steps.

_That_ is creating your own reality.

### Martin — 2012-07-14T15:36:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-37723>

Did my first consult with Dr Kruse recently and I still am flying high from it. I have wanted to get in front of him and show him my labs since I started reading his blog last year. Finally made it happen.

It went very well and so much was revealed. He is able to spot so much in those sometimes cryptic numbers and picks up on clues about your personal history and your family's, too. He offered valuable suggestions on helpful supps, as well.

He saw something and suggested a full pituitary work up with the following labs: Prolactin, GNRH, LH, ACTH, FSH, TSH, and the biggie is IGF-1. If these hormones are low, it is rotten news. It could be that the biotoxin illness I have has fried my brain and I would need many replacement hormones for the rest of my life. Expensive, and I have no insurance.

I found a good article on STTM on hypopit issues and added a couple more tests that it suggested for a work up: Renin Activity and Aldosterone, DHT, and DHEA-S. When I went back to my consult notes I found references to some of these. Doc is on it, I tell ya.

I really like his leaning heavily towards using foods as medicine and now I am slamming down oysters by the dozen. I do the cooking in the house now and last night I fixed oysters with chopped parsley, garlic, sea salt, lemon juice, butter, olive oil, and tobasco sauce. Sides of broccoli, and sweet potato. Didn't fix enough. I could do it all over three times. Shrimp is next!

Shellfish! Talk about "traditional" foods! It makes a lot of sense that people have eaten from the coast for so long. I want to move to the beach now and go out every day and forage.

The local seafood market will have to do. I live in the mountains. It is loaded with great quality US and Canadian seafood and none from China. This a great world we live in when I don't have to do my own clam hunting.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-14T15:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-37727>

Morning Sunshine! You sound so good. It's fun when we can be on a high. You and I both know it is usually short lived. I'm thinking one day we both will be there for a long time if we keep this up. How was your CT the other day? Are you getting those tests done? I don't see this new doc for 3 weeks so I have to wait for her to request them. I tried with the one I saw last and she basically told me she is deferring it to the experts cuz it's out of her league. I sure hope the new one isn't a bust but if she is I have another one in my back pocket and she talks about thinking outside the box. Only prob is she is out of network and there go the dollars. You know can relate for sure. I am still waiting for my acth and rt3 result. Been a week now. I know the rt3 takes a week, but I thought for sure it would be back by now. I am feeling better mood wise these days at least for now. Still yawn all day long. Not much I can do about that except keep CT'ing and doing everything else I am doing.

H - you do realize this is really not your fault. You did nothing to deserve this bullxxxx or at least not intentionally. Hang in there and keep smiling.

### Martin — 2012-07-15T00:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-37857>

Hey V, thanks for the good cheer!

I am doing a bit better. Yes, you hit the nail when you said the "high" comes and goes. When it's here, I burn thru it by working, when it's gone, I crash and rest .

Glad to see you are in a better mood. Me, too. But you noticed that already!

Waiting is part of it all, I guess. I take solace in the fact we are moving forward.

Yes, I daydream about feeling like I used to in better days. I really believe I will be there again someday. Perhaps soon.

Yes, I do realize it is not my "doing". I have an epigenetically weak body in this life. I will do whatever I can to feel better. Got to deal with the cards I was dealt. How I feel about it is the key. I'm at peace with all this, no matter what comes my way.

I went to the lab two days ago and had the Big Pituitary Draw. IGF-1 is the big gorilla in the room standing on my coat tail. My life pivots on that test result. Isn't that fascinating?! The GNRH was outrageous, but I didn't hesitate and plunked down the VISA. None of the others were too expensive. Had to be done, doc's orders. I shopped LEF.org and my self-pay local lab and actually came out about $100 bucks cheaper. LEF is great! I've learned so much from their site. If I didn't have this great lab nearby I would go with them. As it turns out, if I went with LEF, my lab is where I'd have to go anyway to get the draws!

My next consult will show me some HUGE things, Vickie. Is my pituitary fried? Is it still "massage-able" back to functioning? Doc says it's a good possibility. I feel it in my bones.

Maybe I have more energy than I think. Am just so used to whining that maybe it feels worse than it really is? I know my labs like T are low and not positive indicators, but I putt along in life perhaps better than I think. Hard to get an objective look at myself. Maybe I'm riding a wave of unbridled optimism right now. Nuttin' wrong wit dat!

### Martin — 2012-07-15T14:51:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-38023>

I am coming at all this from a different angle than weight loss. I don't need to lose _any. I'm 135 lbs, 5'11". I'm pretty much at my core fat after I did Leptin Reset protocol and have gone Paleo about eight months ago. I had gotten up to 150 and it was all belly fat. Not_ attractive nor healthy on a small guy. It took nine weeks to trim down that protruding belly, and, for the first time in decades I did a protocol that actually had a huge result! It works!

Started CT a couple of days ago at a friend's pool. The water was a balmy 78 degrees, but I need to start slow and work down. Felt good and stayed in an hour. Afterwards, I had a familiar warm, fuzzy feeling like I used to get when I went swimming years ago. The beach, public pool, a lake, they all were coolish and I felt less ache and slept like a rock those nights afterwards.

I got home and decided to run a tub of 78 degree water and stayed in for 45 minutes. Chilly, but I could stand it. It gave me needed confidence to keep going.

But since I am coming at all this from a biotoxin illness angle, I had to clear some biotoxins with cholestyramine before doing more Reset or CT because I would get hammered by the toxins liberated from the fat that was getting turned over.

After three months on cholestyramine, I'm ready for CT to get rid of more crap.

Not a moment too soon, either. I am stalled in my recovery out of this three-year bout with whatever hammered my a\*\* into the ground. I have been fundamentally changed since that illness. Everything is different. Hormones are powerful things, and cause mood changes that don't become mo' better. And we'll see what other hormones are trashed when all the pituitary tests come back. I already know I have poor cortisol rhythm, lousy thyroids, piss-poor vasopressin (pun intended), flaccid testosterone (again with the puns), and god-know's-what-else going on.

CT raises aMSH, and my labs with

Good question on the run-up to testing! I wonder if sour-looks-because-your-going-to-the-lab-and-spending-money-and-you-never-seem-to-meditate-to-solve-your-health-issues is affecting mine! LOL!

### Martin — 2012-07-17T16:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-38705>

Yes, ACTH is low. Still trying to research what a "good" number would be and what to do about it. CT, of course.

Speaking of which, another hop in my friends unheated, beautiful pool. Whimpy, I know, but 78 degrees still feels right on the edge of shivering for this skinny old guy with crappy thyroid numbers. Got to get the gas pedal fixed so I can rev.

I did notice that if I can swim around I can tolerate lower temps. It's hard to backstroke in a bathtub. I am looking a buying a stock tub but I have to watch buying \*\*\*\* that I use once or twice and then neglect. Still, I am intrigued about the size and how it would allow some underwater arm and leg waving to tolerate the bone-chilling cold.

I seem to feel a bit less ache when I dunk. I really need to get colder and bend things around in my little hormonal head. Rewire. I believe that that is the key to getting back to feeling good, and, more importantly, not take a whole \*\*\*\*load of bio-identical exogenous hormone supps. Not that I won't! Cram 'em in that cakehole!\* I'm ready to get tested and find out what and how much! I'm so ready, I'd gulp down a.... never mind, I should not really go there in public.

\*a nod to Quelsen, I stole this from one of his entries and still chuckle every time I see it. I was intrigued by him and his story, and his writing style. I wonder what happens to people when they drop off the radar.

### Martin — 2012-07-18T23:40:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-39161>

Reading up more on methylation.

Took laptop to lunch (seafood, of course) and read as much as my beany brain could take.

Cute waitress's station was right over my shoulder and she asked wtf was so interesting and what did all those weird charts and words like "heterozygous for MTRR A66G" mean.

Told her to give me a few months and I might choke up a coherent clue, and I was good on my unsweet tea.

I am really liking this direction I'm going in where I can get very specific answers and values to my body. I have long dreamt of it and I knew the answers were running around in my bodily fluids. 23andme could really pin some s\*\*t down and get me outs this constant ache and fatigue.

I have had a decent day so far, and my newly-aquired keto strips showed "negative". Another value that isn't in the sh\*\*ter. I'll take it.

No pituitary labs back yet. Daydreamed on the way home about them coming back in decent shape and had me a good little tear-up session. I am getting some answers! And some of them are already good! And I have protocols that will help me thru this! And a support group of like-minded folks that are willing to lend an ear and a kind word of support! They _get_ it!!!

### Martin — 2012-07-19T17:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-39357>

Mold doc tomorrow in Atlanta. Not sure what is going to happen, but I have mentioned starting CT and he seems to be interested.

I will get into methylation with him and see what he sees. I also told him that I consulted with Dr. Kruse about hypopit tests so we'll see what he says/knows/sees.

Those pituitary work-up tests were not cheap. I self-pay and still spent big (to me) money. But what else am I gonna do with it?! I'll spend it all to get right. You know the old saying, "can't take it with you."

I did get some of the labs back:

DHEA-S **123.7 ug/dl 38 - 318

Estradiol **20 pg/ml 1 - 47

PSA **1.91 ng/dl 0.00 - 4.00

If anyone sees anything interesting, please comment.

I've been testing BG upon rising/fasting and it's been 83, 87, 83, 84, over the last four days.

BP has been:

101/72/75

118/65/78

105/71/63

116/80/81

**

Read BG 5.

How boring.

Another mind-altering, breakthrough-creating, life-changing, life-affirming, direction-giving, load 'o info.

For breakfast, fixed eight ounces of lightly-cooked oysters topped with bacon, and a free-range egg from my chickies out on my pasture. I wanted to have a cup of my newly-aquired Bulletproof coffee, but I had to be content with just smelling the fresh grounds as I fixed it for my wife. When I ordered it, she was agast at the price, but the aroma brought her down off the ceiling. Killer stuff. Doc says to limit coffee due to my low-ish iron. Didn't feel like dumping it on top of my nutrient-dense, iron-rich, primordial bee-fast.

Nights are the worst for the Ache**. It seems to be better in the morning, but builds during the day. I push thru preparing dinner (I am still loving cooking!) every night and then crash on the couch and watch me some Roku. I don't like to nap because it tends to screw up my ability to go to sleep quickly. It seems that sleep is the only freedom I have from the Ache**. But if I awaken, buddy, it's there. Ugh. I really believe it wakes me up because laying in one position for a long stretch stiffens me up to the point where I awaken. A quick (and quiet - don't want to wake the woman out of her much-needed sleep) roll-over and _usually can nod off quickly. Usually._

I didn't make time to do CT yesterday. Rental house responsibilities and running into town for ersters and Keto strips. Craving the cold water - not! My skinny ass doesn't do well with cold and Doc says to go slow with my thyroid numbers. I'm all about it.

### Martin — 2012-07-23T15:51:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-40385>

Mold Doc visit went very well. Passed VCS test again. Good progress! I just wish my tired a\*\* would catch up to those good results. No tests were scheduled, just continued CSM, and, I've got to get my home re-checked for the top-five mold species. It's kinda of a pain to do, but necessary. But the thought of having to move from a place where I've put my heart and soul into (and tons of money and sweat) for 12 years is daunting. I love my house. Slap-dab in the middle of thirty-five acres in the beautiful mountains.

But I digress. Spoke to moldy doc about methylation and he was only slightly familiar. Bummer. I want to know more about zeroing in on what my body is screwing up. I brought up the 23andme test and he was again, only slightly familiar, and again, bummer. I think these are important issues and strategies to go for and some expert guidance would be a welcome change from my stumbling through every path I come across. That's why Dr. Kruse is so crucial. His offering up consults was a major turning point in my care!

Again with the digressing. I asked my doc if I could transition off CSM because I passed VCS and he reminded me it depends on my house and how effective the mold remediation was. Can't guess, test. So another $200 for CSM (Im almost out) and another $150 for mold test. Ku-ching, d\*\*m it.

Speaking of $80 for 30 pills, I mentioned the vasopressin drug I'm taking is really helping me get thru the night without nine trips to the johnny. "Sleep, perchance to pee." "To pee or not to pee." Okay enough cutesy, bastardized Shakespeare lines, but it's so fun when something I shove down my gullet actually gives me the intended bennies. I mentioned to the mold doc that it does wear off by the next morning, so he upped my dose, but told me to carefully monitor the fluid retention. Drugs, ya know, even though it's hormone replacement.

I tweaked my back playing with the twins when we went to visit our son after the doc appointment. Can't help myself! They are so much fun! All that enthusiasm in a seven-year old package. Picked them both up at the same time, spun them around, and when I got home snatched up a fifty-pound sacko' birdseed to finally move it from beside the front door. The next day, muscle misery on top of constant flu-symp misery. Yay me.

Went to friend's pool the next day and did some light (79 degrees, I know, I'm an effin' wimp, but I get what I get) CTing and felt better. Less ache and a "good" tired. Fixed grassy burgers on the grill along with home-grown veggies. Ate a tomato slice. Couldn't/didn't help myself. Nightshades are borderline with my lipid labs.

Got home and took Advil for my d\*\*m back pain. Tired of suffering and went for the drugs like a cocaine junkie at a a party. It worked well. Good sleep. Today, less pain.

What will today bring?

### Martin — 2012-07-25T18:22:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-41018>

Mold doc visit in Atlanta went well. Passed VCS test again. No labs done, just continuing to do same strategies. That shows promise, but that brings into the light another deeper, more disconcerting condition that I'm am the process of confronting.

I got my pituitary work-up back on Monday.

Oh boy.

I haven't seem to be able to write much about it yet. It's huge, deeply personal, and difficult to bring up, even in my house.

I strained my back muscles playing with the twins in my visit. And that 50 pound sack of birdseed I finally moved from the foyer didn't help. Back crap, _and_ brain crap. Yay.

The webinar was inspiring and I will listen to it again to try and figure out what was said because the audio would drop out, often just as an important thing was said! But the info is/will be offered up again and I just did it to join in my first webinar ever.

### Martin — 2012-07-28T17:44:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-41667>

Continuing to get inspired by the webinar. Nothing like a direction to go in that you trust. Nothing. I have searched for it all my life. Pain and fatigue have driven me to find a way out of it. I'm on it for the first time, I believe.

I turn 60 sunday and have been batting back thoughts that I may not ever get relief. They are old thoughts that are pre-Optimal. It may be true, or maybe not, but I will not stop seeking/trying/eating like a OPTIMAL HUMAN should/can/did.

Bought a fish head yesterday to try and do a smoothie and perhaps fish stock. I am really enjoying embracing seafood! Always loved the stuff and can buy less grass-fed beef to compensate for the added costs. And cut down on buying supplements, as well. The sea veggie info is killer. I have kelp and dulse in powdered form, kombu, wakame, and nori already in the pantry, and plan on looking into bladderwrack, irish moss, and others. Thanks, Doc, for the trusty direction.

Still haven't talked much about my pituitary labs I got back last monday. Difficult enough to organize a response to myself, let alone someone else. I guess I am waiting for my consultation coming up next week. Biggie, to say the least.

I can't be around mold. I have done tons of testing and history-gazing and many things seem to fit my illness. I really thought that remediation of my home and CSM would open the door to a new day and I would come out of this misery I have been overtaken by. Not happening much... yet.

So it is perhaps ironic that in one of out two rental homes has become water-damaged by the idiot tenant who didn't check his online auto-pay water bill/power bill statements for over a year and didn't realize the water heater had been leaking hot water into the crawlspace for ten months. Perfect conditions for growing, dangerous, illness-causing, mind-altering, soul-killing, effing fungi. The house is uninhabitable and has to be torn apart and remediated by professionals. I am a retired old home restorer and could do all the work myself, but no more. Too lethargic and sick, let alone having a positive haplotype for susceptibility to mold exposure.

Called our insurance agent, and long story a little shorter, probably not covered due to having gone on so damn long. I never saw any evidence in my walk-thoughs because it was dripping down into a isolated part of the old section of the house way under the house.

What is the purpose of insurance if s\*\*t like this is not covered?!!!!! It is HALF of our income in our retired life and now we are going to have to come out-of-pocket to get it back?!!! Remediation is BIG money due to the fear factor (much of it justified) and C-Y-ASS attitude that prevails. I'm not trying to get something for nothing, I just want to get our rental back in good shape and going again.

Oysters for breakfast, raw. Covered with chopped parsley, bacon and grease, butter poured on top, salt and pepper. Side of sausage, and three brazil nuts. Had cuppa Bullet-Proof with heavy cream before I remembered that coffee inhibits absorption of iron.

Doh. Will remember from now on.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-28T17:57:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-41670>

Martin, just wanted to say I feel for you about the mold. I lived in a mold-infested apartment for a year or so, it was hell...... I had no idea what was happening to me. I just constantly felt like I had a head cold. My nose was always stuffy, I'd wake up in the mornings with swollen eyes, things like that. I discovered little mosquito like bugs one day, and the day I moved out their presence exploded.... everywhere.

I had a friend help me move who works in construction and the moment she and her friends entered my place they told me that it was mold. And that I needed to get the hell out of there. Three nights in my new place, and all my symptoms had resolved. I felt like a new person!! I'm suspecting that the place I live in right now has some mold issues, and we've had two water leaks in a few years. This is an old building. I don't like it here, so my goal is to move. Unfortunately, not happening right now

### Martin — 2012-07-28T18:23:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-41672>

Thanks, Souldancer. It's not fun. I read your posts often and wish you some healing relief.

Are your symptoms in any way mold-related? I do not know your story well, sorry. I try and keep up with people but get too tired to sit in front of my computer. I forget a lot and mix up stories, too.

Thanks again for the encouragement.

M

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-28T18:43:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-41675>

I mix up people and problems, too  I'm allergic to mold, as my skin prick allergy testing says as well. I'm not sure I have the sensitivities you have, though. My symptoms appear to resolve as soon as I leave the mold exposure, pretty immediately. I have other problems now... recovering from AN, multiple brain injuries, and recently found out I have a congenital heart defect that affects blood flow to my brain. At some point in time I will need to do some of the testing you did, probably, to make sure. I see improvements in the stuffy nose when I stay with my parents who have a nice, clean, non-moldy, all wood-floored house. My other symptoms stay. So they're related to the other stuff I got going on.

I just wish you will be better soon. B/c I remember the sudden improvement I had when I got out of that moldy place (it was \*very\* moldy and I filed a complaint.... ). I felt like born again, and I know you can, too. I am keeping this problem in mind as I am creating my un-lived life.... it will be mold-free.... for sure!

Do you experience relief when you stay in non-moldy, organically built places?

### kathylu — 2012-07-28T21:29:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-41703>

Martin, file an appeal on your mold claim and find out what it takes to file a complaint at the state insurance commission. Then call the state Attorney General's office and talk to their consumer liason. They absolutely LOVE putting pressure on insurance companies!

Seems to me if you do annual inspections your mold coverage cannot be invalidated due to hidden water damage. Fight back! Mold claims are very expensive to remediate yourself.

### Martin — 2012-07-29T00:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-41721>

Great suggestions! Will do some checking! Thanks!!!

### nonchalant — 2012-07-29T01:21:07+0300
