### chocolate — 2012-05-26T00:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-27464>

loading dose, finally... there is supposed to be a detox... bromide, fluoride, I think

[http://naturalhealthsciencenews.org/David-Brownstein-MD-author-of-Iodine-Why-You-Need-It-Why-You-Cannot-Live-without-It](http://http://naturalhealthsciencenews.org/David-Brownstein-MD-author-of-Iodine-Why-You-Need-It-Why-You-Cannot-Live-without-It)

I was initially hesitant to use higher (>1mg) doses of iodine due to my concern about causing adverse effects. In reviewing much of the literature there was concern about larger doses of iodine causing hyperthyroid symptoms. However, a further, more exhaustive review of the literature failed to prove that iodine, in milligram doses ever was shown to cause hyperthyroid symptoms. In fact, as iodine levels have fallen over 50% in the last 30 years in the United States, autoimmune disorders and hyperthyroid symptoms have been increasing at near epidemic proportions.

After testing individuals and finding low iodine levels, I began to use smaller milligram amounts of iodine/iodide (6.25mg/day). Upon retesting these individuals 1-2 months later, little progress was made. I therefore began using higher milligram doses (6.25-50mg) to increase the serum levels of iodine. It was only with these higher doses that I began to see clinical improvement as well as positive changes in the laboratory tests.

Why would people need the larger doses of iodine? Why have iodine levels fallen 50% in the last 30 years? As I pondered these questions, I came to the conclusion that the toxicity of modern life must be impacting iodine levels. It is well known that the toxic halides, fluoride and bromide, having a similar structure as iodine, can competitively inhibit iodine absorption and binding in the body.

A study was performed in my office to look at the iodine levels as well as the toxic halogen levels bromide and fluoride in 8 random patients. None of the patients had been treated with iodine before the study. The patients were studied at baseline to look not only at their iodine levels but also their bromide and fluoride levels. Next, after taking a loading dose of iodine (50mg of iodide/iodine--IodoralÂ®) they were rechecked for their levels of iodine, bromide and fluoride. The patients then took a loading dose of iodine (50mg of iodide/iodine--IodoralÂ®) for 30 days and they repeated a 24-hour urine collection. The results are summarized in Table 1 below.

My dog has had a quarter sized lump on her back near her tail for a couple of years now. Seven weeks ago I put a generous amount of Lugol's on the bump once or twice then forgot about it. Last week I noticed the dog had some blood & pus on her fur. The lump (possibly a Lipoma or cyst) burst on its own after getting larger and softer the previous week. I didn't touch it other than to clean up the draining sore with some hydrogen peroxide and some more Lugols. So then I put some Lugols on the lump on my hubby's head which he has had for a few years too. A few days later he said that it was kinda sore. I looked at it and it now had a red spot the size of a pencil eraser and it was starting to ooze a tiny bit. So I put more Lugols on it again. I need to be more diligent about reapplying the Lugols. He goes to bed before me so I forget to apply the iodine. The dog has a indent where her lump was. It's all healed now and she's doing well. I'm still taking 100 mg's of Iodoral each day--50 in the morning and 50 before bed along with companion supplements per Brownstein's protocol which include ATP Cofactors, Magnesium, Vita C, Selenium & Celtic or Sea Salt.

### chocolate — 2012-06-04T22:54:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-29415>

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potassium\_iodide](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potassium_iodide)

Pharmaceutical applications

**Potassium iodide can be conveniently prepared as a saturated solution, abbreviated SSKI. This method of delivering potassium iodide does not require a method to weigh out the potassium iodide so it can be used in an emergency situation. KI crystals are simply added to water until no more KI will dissolve and instead sits at the bottom of the container. With pure water, the concentration of KI in the solution depends only on the temperature. Potassium iodide is highly soluble in water so SSKI is a concentrated source of KI. At 20 degrees Celsius the solubility of KI is 140-148 grams per 100 grams of water.\[15\] Because the volumes of KI and water are approximately additive, the resulting SSKI solution will contain about 1.40 gram (1400 mg) KI per milliliter (mL) of solution. This is 100% weight/volume (note units of mass concentration) of KI (one gram KI per mL solution), which is possible because SSKI is significantly more dense than pure water—about 1.72 g/mL.\[16\] Because KI is about 76.4% iodide by weight, SSKI contains about 764 mg iodide per mL. This concentration of iodide allows the calculation of the iodide dose per drop, if one knows the number of drops per milliliter. For SSKI, a solution more viscous than water, there are assumed to be 15 drops per mL; the iodide dose is therefore approximately 51 mg per drop, assuming 15 drops/mL. It is conventionally rounded to 50 mg per drop.

The term SSKI is also used, especially by pharmacists, to refer to a U.S.P. pre-prepared solution formula, made by adding exactly KI to water to prepare a solution containing of 1000 mg KI per mL solution (100% wt/volume KI solution), to closely approximate the concentration of SSKI made by saturation. This is essentially interchangeable with SSKI made by saturation, and also contains about 50 mg iodide per drop.

Saturated solutions of potassium iodide can be an emergency treatment for hyperthyroidism (so-called thyroid storm), as high amounts of iodide temporarily suppress secretion of thyroxine from the thyroid gland.\[17\] The dose typically begins with a loading dose, then 1/3 mL SSKI (5 drops or 250 mg iodine as iodide), three times per day.**

Iodide solutions made from a few drops of SSKI added to drinks have also been used as expectorants to increase the water content of respiratory secretions and encourage effective coughing.\[citation needed\]

SSKI has been proposed as a topical treatment for sporotrichosis, but no trials have been conducted to determine the efficacy or side effects of such treatment.\[18\]

Potassium iodide has been used for symptomatic treatment of erythema nodosum patients for persistent lesions whose cause remains unknown. It has been used in cases of erythema nodosum associated with Crohn's disease.\[19\]

> maybe this is why chocolate puts knots in your boobs
>
> "Ted writes, "Iodine is taken when you suspect you have a deficiency. But it is not taken everyday at all and often it is taken once or twice a week. Only when you know you need it. Seaweed contains both iodine and bromine, which generally means you can't overdose on iodine. The reason is simple: bromine displaces iodine. But when bromine and iodine are present together, it makes for iodine bioavailability, while bakery products high in bromine will displace the iodine. The interesting thing is when bromine and iodine (found in nature) as in seaweed are found together, once the body doesn't need iodine it can easily be removed due to the presence of bromine. I have not seen any case of excess iodine consumption from taking of seaweed, but I have seen quite often excess consumption of iodine when they are added in the salt and taken too much. The reason is nature presents us with many minerals in their respective ratios and their antagonism preventing such an overdose and allowing the body to achieve greater equilibrium. This issue appears to be consuming foods from natural rather than chemical if you fear of any possible overdose. Ted"

### chocolate — 2012-06-07T02:39:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-29824>

[http://www.breastcancerchoices.org/iprotocol.html](http://http://www.breastcancerchoices.org/iprotocol.html)

About the Iodine Supplementation Protocol

\--- Not Just For Breast Cancer Patients

Physicians Guy Abraham, MD, David Brownstein, MD, and Jorge Flechas, MD, have treated more

than 4,000 patients with iodine supplementation. The protocol below is suggested by their

writings and lectures. We acknowledge with appreciation their pioneering research and

generous contributions to the field of Iodine Therapy. See references below the protocol.

Iodine and Companion Nutrients

From October 2007 Iodine Conference

50 mg Iodoral minimum for breast cancer (may start with 12.5 mg).

Some practitioners may recommend another form of iodine such as Lugol's solution.

Iodoral is the Lugol's formula in tablet form especially designed to avoid gastric irritation.

Vitamin C - 3,000 mg per day (more may be necessary to detox bromide).

300-600 mg magnesium oxide (Iodine Investigation Project participants prefer

magnesium glycinate or magnesium citrate.)

200 mcg selenium. The selenomethionine version is preferred by many.

500 mg niacin (B3) twice a day (NOT niacinamide). Start lower to avoid flush. \*See update

in left margin on the ATP Cofactors as an alternative.

100 mg Vitamin B2 three times a day. \*See update in left margin on the ATP Cofactor as

an alternative.

A comprehensive vitamin and nutrition program.

(Feb 2008) Dr. Guy Abraham cautions that "excess calcium supplementation (2,000-

3,000 md/day) has been the most common cause of poor response to iodine

supplementation." Vitamin Research News Vol. 22. Number 2.

24 Hour Iodine Loading Test Now Essential

The Iodine specialists have recently discovered it is essential to have the 24 Hour Iodine

Loading Test before implementing iodine supplementation in order to investigate the presence

of a possible iodine absorption defect. If a patient has a normal or high Loading Test score in the

presence of known iodine deficiency condition such as breast or thyroid disease, a defect in the

Sodium Iodide Symporter (NIS) mechanism is suspected. The iodine taken during the loading

test may pass into the urine unabsorbed giving a false normal reading. Follow your physician's

iodine dosing carefully to see how the iodine test result changes.

The doctors find that the most common side effects are usually resolved with 3 grams of

vitamin C and/or the salt loading protocol.

Be aware of iodine-related bromide detox symptoms. Symptoms of "bromism" may

occur in some people. Click here for a list of recorded bromide symptoms. Patients are

usually advised to cut back on iodine, \*pulse-dose (see below) and salt-load more

frequently.

Important: The following protocol should be implemented only under your doctor's supervision

and monitored with the appropriate laboratory work and thyroid studies. Report any side effects

to your physician. Do not take iodine if you are allergic to iodized salt.

Salt Loading Protocol for Possible Bromide Side Effects

Orthoiodosupplementation induced and increased mobilization of bromine from storage sites,

with increased urinary excretion of bromide. --Guy Abraham, MD

Certain iodine-literate doctors feel the bromide excreted by iodine therapy can be eliminated

more efficiently by the chloride present in mineral-rich unprocessed sea salt. The salt is added

to water in a specific way. Eating salty food is not effective. Table salt is only used in an

emergency.

Many iodine takers found the Salt Loading Protocol devised by Dr. William Shevin (and

presented at the February '07 Iodine Conference) to be effective in eliminating the side effects

such as bromide sedation, acne, brain fog, brassy taste, mouth sores, frontal headache or other

symptoms which occur in a small percentage of patients.

1/4 teaspoon salt dissolved in 1/2 cup warm water. Follow immediately with 12-16 oz

pure water.

Repeat in 30-45 minutes if needed. May repeat again until copious urination begins.

Observe subjective response (usually within several hours).

Iodine Investigation Project participants have found 1/2 teaspoon salt dissolved in the

recommended amount of water works faster than the 1/4 teaspoon dosage.

\* Pulse dosing of iodine has often helped our participants with detox symptoms: taking a 48

hour break from iodine supplementation as needed seems to enhance the kidneys' ability to

excrete bromide. These are only our observations as educated patients. Consult your own

physician about your particular iodine regimen.

No consensus exists on how important the salt loading may be to an iodine

therapy strategy if the patient does not have adverse symptoms.

Be sure to ask your doctor before implementing salt loading or any medical

strategy.

### Grizz — 2012-06-11T22:00:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-30584>

Ruth,

It's very concerning that you have breast pain because iodine deficiency has been scientifically studied as the main underlying cause. Once you have been diagnosed with breast cancer, having breast pain is a sign that you haven't addressed that underlying cause.

As you know, fibrocystic breast disease can progress to breast cancer. The objective is to arrest the process.

Also, all kelp supplements are now considered toxic with arsenic, pesticide run-off, heavy metals, oil slick dispersants, etc. Even if they weren't they don't contain nearly enough iodine to clean correct the anatomy and physiology of the breasts. Iodine leaves seaweed by evaporation (sublimation) soon after the seaweeds are harvested. By the time they are processed into tablets, there is very little left.

Keep in mind "kelp" is just a generic word for seaweed and that some seaweeds contain very little iodine.

I hope this information helps. Please see [http://breastcancerchoices.org/iodine.html](http://http://breastcancerchoices.org/iodine.html)

Lynne

Director of [BreastCancerChoices](http://http://www.breastcancerchoices.org/iprotocol.html)

[http://health.groups.yahoo.com/group/breastcancerthinktank/message/12822](http://http://health.groups.yahoo.com/group/breastcancerthinktank/message/12822)

You should take more than 2 nuts. 4 small nuts or 3 large nuts are what I take. When the Brazil Nuts are gone it is best to switch to selenium tablets to get a more reliable dosage.

Yes, we must take enough iodine supplements to offset the Halogen Chemicals that surrounds us for the rest of our lives. Dr. Brownstein recommends 50mg daily. Most report that if they drop dosage too much, they don't feel good. So how you feel at a dosage is most important. I am feeling FANTASTIC at 150mg a day, so I may not drop it down by much. Jack,

My eyes are wide open. Help me to understand why so many doctors claim to be ignorant of iodine. Why is there a National Epidemic of iodine deficiency? Why do so many doctors tell women to cut their breasts off for Fibrocystic Breast Disease instead of prescribing iodine.

This report is very typical of what I am finding:

Originally Posted by Zophie

I know I am chiming in on this post super late but I just want to thank everyone who has shared information here. Grizz, If I ever meet you in person I'm going to kiss you on the lips!

After reading everything and thinking about it I decided to give iodine a try. I have had fiber cystic breasts since I was about 11 years old and I can't even tell you how uncomfortable exercising, hugging and even bras have been my whole life. **I too have had 4 different doctors tell me to cut off my breasts. (and I have even thought about it) Also they told me to get a hysterectomy as soon as I was done having children to deal with the uterine fibroids.

I have been taking iodine with the support supplements for just over a month. I dove right in and started with 1 drop. (now up to 4 drops) The pain in my breasts went away within 24 hours!!!! I literally did a little dance in the shower when I realized it. I still have some lumps but the smaller ones are gone. I also am getting a lot of relief from the fibroid pain.

Other things I have observed is my appetite is almost completely gone, my hands don't break out from the chemicals anymore, my fingernails are hard and growing for the first time in my life, I haven't even had one migraine since starting and I am happy and have so much energy. I have suffered from depression for so long that it is amazing to feel normal again.

So, again, thank you thank you!!

[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-317.html#post889724](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-317.html#post889724)

Zophie Continues:

\=============

Thank you both! To me this is nothing short of a real miracle. Grizz, I have read everything in your file at least 5 times, I have sent it on to my mom and sister and I tell everyone in the salon who will listen. Thank you for putting it together. **The information is life changing for me.

I know 11 is young... and I could have been spared 25 years of pain. I don't know if this is a common thing for girls with FB but I was that freak of nature kid who was as tall as the teacher at 9 and had a full-grown woman's body at 12. My breasts always hurt and always felt like bean bags. (and got worse over the years) I remember complaining about it when I was young **and being told that it was normal** for developing girls. People just thought I was complaining because I wasn't yet mature enough for the body I had. I didn't know it was FB untill I was 19 or 20 when I went in to get birth control for the first time. So I learned it was not normal but there was nothing anyone could do.**

Grizz- Iodine has only been in use for 150 years and no one knows??

I never got as far as price with the doctors so I don't know what that would cost but I was told that they could do some nice reconstructive implants at the same time. (I'm sure that doubles the price) When I chose not to do it my last doctor wanted to get me on antidepressants to "help" me deal with it. I am sure doctors just don't know about iodine.** I really feel that western medicine is great at cutting stitching and fixing broken bones. They fail when it comes to restoring health to people with chronic problems. **They just want to cut it out or give you a pill to mask the symptoms. No one, it seems, asks the question of what is causing the problem. That is why I am so grateful for the information you have put out there.

I have two small victories to share. First, I went out yesterday to do a short run. About half way in there is a hill thats really steep that I use for my sprints. (maybe 150 yards long) I always run up but never down because of my chest and my uterus. Yesterday I flew down it with no pain at all and I was laughing so hard when I got to the bottom because I was so happy. Second, I woke up this morning and realized that I had slept on my stomach! **Both of these are small things... to me, they represent the impossible.

[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-317.html#post889847](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-317.html#post889847)

Multiple doctors in my neighborhood in Tennessee are also telling women to learn to live with their FBS breast pain or cut off their breasts, when the cure is a simple bottle of iodine. [Dr. Derry says 90% of American Women suffer fibrocystic breasts,](http://http://iodine4health.com/body/breast/derry_breast.htm) and so many doctors know NOTHING??? Something is VERY VERY VERY wrong.

Jack, I just can't believe all the facts that I am uncovering. Halogen chemicals in city water, bread & food are stripping what little iodine people have and replacing iodine with fluorides, bromides, chlorines. **There seems to be a National Conspiracy to make everyone iodine deficient. Why?** Boost revenues for the cancer industry? Many of those iodine deficiency diseases end up as cancer cases. Endometriosis, PCOS, Fibrocystic Breast Disease, Cysts, Fibroids, Prostate Problems. I cured my Prostate BPH problems with 50mg iodoral iodine. Women on our Message Board are curing their FBS woth iodine (in spite of their ignorant doctors)

I want to thank you for sending me off on this journey. It was your story of your training on fibrocystic breasts in med school, and your recommendation to eat seafood for iodine that got me started.

Look at this report, again typical, where doctor refuses to discuss iodine.

Went to a doctor that school health center referred. I wanted to hear how he interpret the ultrasound. He said mine nodules are very likely benign but he suggested biopsy to confirm - I asked if the only reason for the suggestion of biopsy was the size of my nodules, he said yes. I told him I'm moving to PA soon, he said this is not an emergency, I can deal with this after I settle down in my new place.

I told him about iodine supplement and what I read online, he is totally against it. Told me iodine deficiency is history... we don't have this problem in America now. I told him my nodules shrunk, I feel better, ect... he didn't want to hear about it. He said there's no need to supplement iodine... T**OO BAD, I wasn't able to educate him. But I'm glad that my ultrasound looks good, that's actually all I wanted to hear from him. I was trying understand my ultrasound result, and needed some help there.

I asked him to give me an iodien deficiency test, he said there's no need for that.** Asked for another ultrasound to see if my nodules shrunk, he said you can ask the doctor who does your biopsy to do it... So I guess I'll find a doctor who knows about iodine next time. And use ultrasound to monitor my thyroid.

Actually took 37.5mg iodoral this morning. Will take 25mg in the early afternoon. Feeling good.br />

> ne clinical finding I always ask about is the presence of fibrocystic breast disease. When it is present I immediately think the woman is likely suffering from an undiagnosed iodine deficiency. I distinctly remember in medical school seeing a woman’s fibrocystic disease cured in less than an hour by an ancient physician who taught us how to examine a women’s breasts. She had volunteered to be a guinea pig for us during our medical education, and on this day we could not examine her without making her scream and cry because her breasts were so tender to palpation. Our MD proctor was about 80 years old and he too was a volunteer helping us learn our craft. He stopped me from examining her breasts and asked her a few questions and then he asked us to leave the room. He applied Lugol’s \[iodine\] solution to her vulva and her nipples and we were able to examine her without any pain a half hour later. It made a huge impression on me. He asked her if she was on any thyroid meds and if she had bad energy or weight gain. A lack of iodine was the source of her painful breasts. Her labs did not reveal any major issues when we saw her. I never forgot this lesson on the thyroid.

Sounds a bit like a fish in a different package to me

Grizz, I appreciate you trying to help and I've probably read most of the information you've read. A woman does not take a lump in her breast lightly, and we don't go have surgery for the fun of it. I had a naturopath help me with this who did the testing and dosing for me. Was I taking the entire list of supplements? Probably not at the time I was trying to avoid surgery. Was I taking these supplements at any time in the last 3 years it's been since I was diagnosed - yes, probably at some point in time in combination with Dr K's recommended supplements I was taking a mix that came close. It didn't work for me and I have not found the reason why, and believe me I'm not happy about it. I am scared of getting new cysts and I know I am more likely to get breast cancer.

My symptoms started many years ago while I was re-feeding from AN and escalated when I moved back into town from living in a minimalist cabin in the Rockies at 8,000ft. I truly believe that cabin, the light cycles, the mineralized well-water, the cold showers (lol) and the reduced exposure to pollutants gave me another few years to figure things out. I just didn't know it at the time. I did see a doctor when I was first having symptoms and she told me all I needed was a bra that fit well and a couple of Ibuprofen. I'll admit I didn't allow her to examine me or do anything to me other than talk b/c I was so scared of doctors at the time and touching me was out of question. That probably didn't help. She was also my eating disorder specialist at the time and told me I was going through puberty b/c I had been too sick before (age 27). What later turned out to be Fibrocystic Breast Disease was one of the biggest challenges in my recovery. I was in pain and super swollen for 2 weeks of the month and after moving back into town it escalated, I was constantly crying, the pain never went away, I was so swollen I had to sleep with a bra and couldn't exercise, I spend a lot of days laying down with ice packs on my chest (that should have fixed it in retrospect ) My symptoms stopped within a month of moving into the cabin. Entirely. Gone. Including all PMS. I was eating a lot of fish oil and hemp at the time.

Within a few months of moving back into town my FBD was a major factor in causing a huge relapse with AN. I lost so much weight again that I ended up in intensive treatment including IP again. I freely stated that it was b/c of the pain and swelling I was experiencing constantly and I still wasn't offered treatment for it other than bras and pain medicine. Losing weight to the degree of emaciation is a \*very\* effective treatment for FBD btw. No hormones no cysts no pain no swelling. But all I got was crap for the choices I was making when I was saying so clearly what was causing my pain. I seriously considered a breast reduction at the time just to deal with symptoms (which I entirely do not need in a healthy state).

Eventually I found a tiny lump one day that was painful to the touch and witnessed it get bigger and more painful every month according to my cycle. I finally went to see an OB/GYN about it who said I was making it up as she couldn't palpate it (it was tiny at that point, a few millimeters) so did a pregnancy test on me. It was negative, she told me the usual (wear a bra and take Ibuprofen). I broke down in her office, couldn't stop crying, said what was happening to me was more than I could handle. I left her office with sleeping meds and anti-depressants that day. The lump kept getting bigger but was in an unusual spot (mammary fold) and eventually got big enough to even be palpated by my OB/GYN. Off to the surgeon I went. I did all sorts of testing but successfully refused the mammogram. I got a number of opinions and all of them said no idea if cancer or not. It had undefined edges, unusual for a cyst, but was hormone sensitive. I found out about iodine treatments and decided to take my chances for a month or two while watching. The lump kept getting bigger and at 1.7cm or so I decided to have surgery minimal lumpectomy only no matter what the outcome of pathology. I also needed that to stay sane at that point. Pathology report dxed FBD and they cut out a 1in square guessing the location b/c they couldn't find an actual tumor or cyst. The surgeon did this by palpation info only.

I've kept up the iodine ever since, sometimes more, sometimes less. I still get swelling and pain every month and I have some spots I'm watching closely. I have tried the topical application and it doesn't appear to make a difference. My max dose was 50mg but that has been for years now. I was only mildly deficient in iodine upon iodine loading test when I started my iodine journey.

More info than you cared for but boy does it feel good to get it out of my mind and onto "paper" instead. I'm not so crazy after all despite the fact that I am female and have an ED.

**Souldanzer;38267 wrote:** Sounds a bit like a fish in a different package to me

Grizz, I appreciate you trying to help and I've probably read most of the information you've read. A woman does not take a lump in her breast lightly, and we don't go have surgery for the fun of it. I had a naturopath help me with this who did the testing and dosing for me. Was I taking the entire list of supplements? Probably not at the time I was trying to avoid surgery. Was I taking these supplements at any time in the last 3 years it's been since I was diagnosed - yes, probably at some point in time in combination with Dr K's recommended supplements I was taking a mix that came close. It didn't work for me and I have not found the reason why, and believe me I'm not happy about it. I am scared of getting new cysts and I know I am more likely to get breast cancer.

My symptoms started many years ago while I was re-feeding from AN and escalated when I moved back into town from living in a minimalist cabin in the Rockies at 8,000ft. I truly believe that cabin, the light cycles, the mineralized well-water, the cold showers (lol) and the reduced exposure to pollutants gave me another few years to figure things out. I just didn't know it at the time. I did see a doctor when I was first having symptoms and she told me all I needed was a bra that fit well and a couple of Ibuprofen. I'll admit I didn't allow her to examine me or do anything to me other than talk b/c I was so scared of doctors at the time and touching me was out of question. That probably didn't help. She was also my eating disorder specialist at the time and told me I was going through puberty b/c I had been too sick before (age 27). What later turned out to be Fibrocystic Breast Disease was one of the biggest challenges in my recovery. I was in pain and super swollen for 2 weeks of the month and after moving back into town it escalated, I was constantly crying, the pain never went away, I was so swollen I had to sleep with a bra and couldn't exercise, I spend a lot of days laying down with ice packs on my chest (that should have fixed it in retrospect ) My symptoms stopped within a month of moving into the cabin. Entirely. Gone. Including all PMS. I was eating a lot of fish oil and hemp at the time.

Within a few months of moving back into town my FBD was a major factor in causing a huge relapse with AN. I lost so much weight again that I ended up in intensive treatment including IP again. I freely stated that it was b/c of the pain and swelling I was experiencing constantly and I still wasn't offered treatment for it other than bras and pain medicine. Losing weight to the degree of emaciation is a \*very\* effective treatment for FBD btw. No hormones no cysts no pain no swelling. But all I got was crap for the choices I was making when I was saying so clearly what was causing my pain. I seriously considered a breast reduction at the time just to deal with symptoms (which I entirely do not need in a healthy state).

Eventually I found a tiny lump one day that was painful to the touch and witnessed it get bigger and more painful every month according to my cycle. I finally went to see an OB/GYN about it who said I was making it up as she couldn't palpate it (it was tiny at that point, a few millimeters) so did a pregnancy test on me. It was negative, she told me the usual (wear a bra and take Ibuprofen). I broke down in her office, couldn't stop crying, said what was happening to me was more than I could handle. I left her office with sleeping meds and anti-depressants that day. The lump kept getting bigger but was in an unusual spot (mammary fold) and eventually got big enough to even be palpated by my OB/GYN. Off to the surgeon I went. I've kept up the iodine ever since, sometimes more, sometimes less. I still get swelling and pain every month and I have some spots I'm watching closely. ### kathylu — 2012-07-18T19:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-39079>

Ok folks...let's talk about poop and iodine! I upped my intake to 15 mg per day from Lugol's 2%. I was severely constipated within 2 days. I know that I have gut dysbiosis and I have been pounding the fermented veggies for years because of it. I eat a home fermented veggie with every meal and drink home made kevita twice per day. That gave me great bowel movements...until I increased the iodine. It's potently bacteriocidal, so I assume I'm killing my gut microflora. My stool size has reduced to next to nothing and I'm suddenly at a "1" on the Bristol stool chart. Now I know there are folks on the internet that say that iodine only kills "bad" bacteria, but it ain't so!

So, i guess my question is has anyone else experienced this? What did you do to minimize the problem? Take your iodine with food only? Put Lugol's on your skin instead of drinking it, thus bypassing the gut? If I can't solve this, the iodine has to go....

You are having detox symptoms. Iodine does NOT kill the good gut flora. Go back to your old dose. The jump from 2.5mg a day to 15mg a day released more toxins into your blood stream than your liver & kidneys could handle. Titrate up very sloowly.

Here are the instructions that we use in the file, ["Iodine References."](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

Start Slow, Low Dose Procedure to detox slowly:

\- - - Mix 1 drop 2% Lugols into eleven-8 oz bottles of water, put the caps back on.

\- - - Week 1 drink 1 oz (~30ml) daily (about 380mcg iodone)

\- - - Week 2 drink 2 oz (~60ml) daily (about 775mcg iodine)

\- - - Week 3 drink 4 oz (~120ml) daily ( about 1.5mg iodine)

\- - - Week 4 drink 6 oz (~180ml) daily ( about 2.3mg iodine)

\- - - Week 5 drink 8 oz (~220ml) daily ( about 3.1 mg iodine or about 1 drop per day),

\- - - Week 6 drink 2 drops per day in a glass of water

\- - - Week 7 drink 3 drops per day in a glass of water

\- - - Week 8 drink 4 drops per day (about 12.5mg = to 1 iodoral)

\* Next, Titrate up with iodoral 12.5mg iodine tablets available at Amazon.Com

\- - - add 1/2 tablet each month to eventually reach 50mg daily-refer to Dr. Brownstein’s Book

\- - - 50mg Daily is the dosage target recommended by Dr. Brownstein to overcome toxins

Are you taking the required co-supplements?

\- - Â½ tsp Natural Celtic Salt, Himalayan Pink Salt, Redmonds Salt or Hawaiin black salt

\- - - If the sea salt is pure white, it is unacceptable (minerals have been stripped out )

\- - 200 mcg selenium (L-selenomethionine preferred)

\- - 400 mg Magnesium - Glycinate preferred

\- - 2,000mg Vitamin C - Ester-C preferred - do not take with iodine

\- - ATP Cofactors caplet contains both 100mg B2 & 500mg B3 non-flushing in the correct ratio. Take 1/2 caplet with iodine up to 25mg, then one caplet for each 50 mg iodine,

I certainly agree that I could be having some detox symptoms. However, I think saying that iodine doesn't kill "good" bacteria is just wishful thinking. Iodine is an efficient broad spectrum microbicide. I can't find a single bacteria, yeast or virus it won't kill at sufficient concentration (and I searched PubMed). That's why it's used to sterilize skin (and surgeon's hands, they wash with it) before surgery. It kills effectively 95 % of bacteria in 90 seconds and 100% within 5 minutes. Of course, at lower concentration, some gut bacteria will survive, but it simply has to have an effect. Stool is about 75% bacteria by weight and since my stool volume decreased so radically, I think it's a reasonable assumption that the iodine was killing my gut flora. I certainly think there is merit to trying to correct iodine deficiency and displace other halogens (detoxing especially bromine), but I gotta show my gut flora some love. That's more critical for me at this point.

\* Iodine and Breast Cancer Prevention

\* Iodine and Fibrocystic Breast Prevention

\* Iodine Prevents Needless Mastectomies

Doctors offer women needless mastectomies for painful Fibrocystic Breasts (FBD) when the proven cure is simple iodine supplements.

My first evidence is a woman and her friends in my own neighborhood who were told to "Learn to live with their breast pain or cut them off." I could not believe that their doctors could be so CRUEL & INDIFFERENT to exploit frightened women for expensive Mastectomies when a $15 bottle of iodine is a proven, time tested CURE.

Then additional evidence began coming into our message thread with heart warming testimonials from women who testified that their doctors had recommended barbaric mastectomies to end their breast pain & suffering. See complete details in Iodine References, chapter Testimonials - - Fibrocystic Breasts: http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references

Dr. Derry explains why Fibrocystic Breasts occur and how iodine cures the condition & also how iodine prevents Breast Cancers:

[Derry. Iodine and the Breast -- Research](http://http://iodine4health.com/body/breast/derry_breast.htm)

> **DrDerry wrote: On Breast Cancer
>
> Once the cell has become a cancer cell then it can take two different turns. It can multiply and spread or it can multiply and just stay where it is. The second is called carcinoma in situ. (cancer at the site) The second phase(cancer multiplying and spreading) is the part we are all familiar with. **Since on average breast cancer cells double every 100 days, it takes 9 years before mammograms can pick it up and around 11 years before we find it ourselves. (15)
>
>
>
> This second clinical part of the cancer phase (the spreading) seems to be arrested by adequate levels of thyroid hormone in all tissues. Thyroid hormone completely controls the connective tissue which forms a strong sieve-like barrier to the passage of cancer cells trying to spread.(16-18) Low levels of thyroid hormone in the tissues (especially connective tissues) promotes the spread of cancer cells.** So the body cancer defense system has two parts iodine for the first pre-cancer phase and thyroid hormone and iodine together for the second clinical phase. There is some overlapping of these two defense systems. The excess iodine flows out in the urine. Of course, because the iodine flows out in the urine it is preventing the development of abnormal cells in the bladder and kidney system at the same time. This then prevents cancers developing there.**
>
> Complete article here:
>
> [Dr. David Derry Answers Reader Questions -- Breast Cancer, Tamoxifen, Thyroid & Weight Loss](http://http://thyroid.about.com/library/derry/bl1a.htm)

Just wondering if anyone has any comments about this recent case study that just came out suggesting iodine supplements caused congenital hypothyroidism in the 3 cases. My first thought is that it is probably related to not enough co-factors being consumed at the same time. In any case, I think we need to be careful about recommending iodine supplements to pregnant women unless they have documented deficiency -- or at least until we understand it all a bit better. Personally I would be cautious in young children too and focus on eating seafood instead.

[http://www.jpeds.com/webfiles/images/journals/ympd/JPEDSConnelly.pdf](http://http://www.jpeds.com/webfiles/images/journals/ympd/JPEDSConnelly.pdf)

### Grizz — 2012-07-29T00:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-41722>

> **Nourish;41937 wrote:** Just wondering if anyone has any comments about this recent case study that just came out suggesting iodine supplements caused congenital hypothyroidism in the 3 cases. My first thought is that it is probably related to not enough co-factors being consumed at the same time. In any case, I think we need to be careful about recommending iodine supplements to pregnant women unless they have documented deficiency -- or at least until we understand it all a bit better. Personally I would be cautious in young children too and focus on eating seafood instead.
>
> [http://www.jpeds.com/webfiles/images/journals/ympd/JPEDSConnelly.pdf](http://http://www.jpeds.com/webfiles/images/journals/ympd/JPEDSConnelly.pdf)

Any woman who is pregnant or planning to get pregnant should NOT increase the amount of iodine dosage. Each increase in iodine consumption kicks out more toxic bromides, fluorides & chlorines. It is also essential to take the required co-supplements. Pregnant women should lock in whatever doasge they were on before pregnancy to avoid any additional detox that could harm the fetus. Ditto while breast feeding, do not increase iodine dosage to keep detox toxins out of the breast milk.

Grizz

Souldanzer,

Since you are now at 150mg, keep it up and your FBD should start melting away. Here are my thoughts:

Are you taking ALL of the co-supplements?

\- - Â½ tsp Natural Celtic Salt, Himalayan Pink Salt, Redmonds Salt or Hawaiin black salt

\- - - If the sea salt is pure white, it is unacceptable (minerals have been stripped out )

\- - 200 mcg selenium (L-selenomethionine preferred)

\- - 400 mg Magnesium - Glycinate preferred

\- - 2,000mg Vitamin C - Ester-C preferred - do not take with iodine

\- - ATP Cofactors caplet contains both 100mg B2 & 500mg B3 non-flushing in the correct ratio. Take 1/2 caplet with iodine up to 25mg, then one caplet for each 50 mg iodine,

ATP Cofactors & the special salt are especially important to get iodine into the cells and toxins out of the cells. Without the ATP Cofactors, we are wasting money on iodine,

Another factor is the bromides & Fluorides coming into your body should be reduced as much as possible. Stop drinking city tap water if it is fluoridated, read labels. Install a reverse osmosis water filter. Most brands of bottled water even contain fluoride. Shockingly, even bottled Baby Water has added fluoride! For most folks, the first 25+mg of iodine is washed out by the toxins coming back in every day. We live in a sea of Halide Toxins that are driving iodine out of our bodies. Beer made with city water contains fluoride. Teas are LOADED with fluoride.

I have never heard of a case of FBD that did not respond to iodine with the required supplements. Has your pain gone away? The pain is the 1st to go, and usually within a few days. It typically takes 3 to 4 months for all the lumps to melt away.

### Grizz — 2012-07-29T17:12:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-41793>

> **Anna K.;42014 wrote:** dr Kruse, I've read and reread Brain Gut 5 and 6 and my understanding is that iodine is very important and we need to get lots of it from the seafood diet.
>
>
>
> but what I don't understand is why do you oppose this iodine supplementation protocol that Grizz recommends?
>
> Is there a harm to do it temporarily to raise your iodine levels while still eating seafood and seaweed to maintain this level?
>
>
>
> I do eat lots of seafood and some seaweed. I'm not sure I can eat enough seaweed everyday to raise my levels as fast as with supplementation?
>
>
>
> thanks.

Dr. Brownstein and the other "Iodine Doctors" have problems with the seafood approach:

1) Most important is that it is not possible to eat enough seafood to detox all of the Bromides, Fluorides & Chlorine that has invaded our cells and kicked out the iodine. After we have detoxed our bodies, the iodine doctors don't think we can get enough iodine from seafood to offset the Halogen Chemicals that come pouring into our bodies. We live in a sea of halide chemicals that are attempting to remove what little iodine we have.

[http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine%20update.htm#section18](http://http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine%20update.htm#section18)

2) They have concerns about toxins in the seafood itself, things like mercury, & other nasty chemicals from our polluted seas. Where Lugols & Iodoral are pure and toxin free. Paleo people were not living in a sea of toxins & an ocean filled with toxins, so seafood provided all the iodine needed. Also soils were not depleted and most lived near the sea.

3) You can't get enough iodine from seafood to cure FBS, PCOS, Endo, Fibroids & Cysts & prostate problems.

Dr. Brownstein says that 50mg of iodine is required to offset the sea of toxins that we live in. Obviously that advice may not apply to everyone. Those of us on a strict Paleo Diet who use fluoride free well water and read labels to avoid halide chemicals can get by with far less iodine. I am close to finishing my detox with 150mg iodine daily and after detox, I plan to drop down to 12.5mg daily for maintenance, which is said to be what the Japanese get in their seafood. I started in January at 1/2mg daily and have very slowly titrated up to 150mg with Lugols and then iodoral from Amazon.Com.

There is also a practical factor. We live in rural West Central Tennessee, and seafoods are just not available without long trips or expensive internet orders. The nearest big cities where seafoods are available are located 70 & 200 miles round trip. Not practical.

Bottom line-the MAIN GOAL for supplementing with iodine is to detox the body, and this just can't be done with seafood. However, after detox is completed, seafood is certainly an option if you can avoid most of the halogen toxins. Halogen chemicals are everywhere & in all the foods we eat, the water we drink, toothpaste, hair dye, cosmetics, drugs, vaccines, inhalers . . . It is impossible to eliminate them. So we need a lot MORE iodine to offset the toxins coming into our bodies every day.

BTW: Here is the ULTIMATE iodine message board with a 5 year history.

[http://curezone.com/forums/f.asp?f=815](http://http://curezone.com/forums/f.asp?f=815)

You can get some great research here by just searching through their HUGE database.

Grizz

Souldanzer,

1st of all, you are asking for thyroid problems by taking too much selenium. 400mcg is the max recommended amount. Iodine & Selenium are co-dependent - you should not take one without the other.

So your first iodine doctor gave up on you? I suggest you seek another opinion from this list of iodine doctors:

[http://breastcancerchoices.org/ipractitioners.html](http://http://breastcancerchoices.org/ipractitioners.html)

It is just unheard of for Fibrocystic Breasts to fail to respond to iodine painting & iodine supplements. You need to find a good iodine doctor to help you. Something is blocking your iodide pathways (Sodium/Iodide Symporter (NIS)) and you need an expert iodine doctor to help you figure out what is wrong. Usually Vitamin B2 & B3 resolves this problem, but you can't take these because of allergies. Do a Google on Goitrogens for a known list of things that can block the iodine pathways. Are you taking any drugs that might be blocking your Sodium/Iodide Symporter? Google Sodium/Iodide Symporter and maybe you can figure out why you can't absorb iodine.

Do a google on **Can't Absorb Iodine.** There are 600,000 hits on this search so you are likely to find your problem. If iodine painting doesn't work for you, then seaweed is not likely to help, either. You need to find out what is blocking your iodine pathways ASAP.

Let us know what you find. We don't want you to end up with a double mastectomy.

Bless you and best of luck,

Grizz

Anna K,

I am officially promoting the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol. I am not a doctor, so I cannot promote iodine in any way without a doctor's blessing. I am familiar with 3 iodine protocols.

\* Dr. Kruse recommends natural seafoods - this is where my wife & I started in Jan 2012.

\* Dr. Davis recommends the kelp seaweed capsules, 1/2 mg daily

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-14T23:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-44949>

> **Glen PDQ;44668 wrote:** Is there a genetic condition that causes the gut to be non-leaky which might hinder absorption of iodine? Are there humans whose zonulin production is impaired?
>
>
>
> Looking around on the net I found a blurb that zonulin was upregulated in people with diabetes 1. I wonder if it is upregulated in people with Goiter. Is upregulated zonulin a survival trait to try and get more iodine (among other things) into the body?

I am sure there is.......but Iodine in my view is best absorbed when it comes in its evolutionary package......seafood. There appears to be many issues with iodine in its raw form and it is not well tolerated by many folks. Zonulin is up regulated on Moby and LADA. I am not aware of being up on T1D. It is up-regulated in Goiters too for obvious reasons. It is possible that this is why it does happen. I think Iodine is a paracellular pathway ion but no one really knows how it is truly absorbed according to the literature.

SEAWEED IODINE CONTENT

Icelandic kelp, 8000ppm, Norwegian kelp 4000ppm, Atlantic kelp 1500-2000ppm, Pacific kelps 500-1200ppm, Fucus spp. 200-500ppm, Wakame 50-150ppm, Sargassum 35ppm, and Nori 15 ppm. These are all approximate and will vary considerably by season, location, age, and harvest practices.

The Japanese and other Asians apparently usually soak their Kombu and other seaweeds in freshwater for 10-30 minutes prior to using in miso broth and other cooking , which removes about 60% of the iodine (Hazutosi). Curiously, I was told by Japanese nationals that the kombu was left in the miso broth for 10-20 minutes and then discarded.

The soaking or prolonged rinsing of high-iodine content seaweeds may reduce the risks for excess iodine-induced disease.

### Grizz — 2012-09-03T23:30:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-49241>

**Iodine Deficiency - An Under-Recognized Epidemic**

Dr. David Brownstein MD

I have been involved in the iodine project for seven years. During this time, iodine has consistently provided the most satisfying clinical results as compared to any other nutrient. To date, my partners and I have tested nearly 5,000 patients for iodine levels. Our results show that more than 96 percent of patients tested have low iodine levels with the vast majority being severely iodine deficient. I believe iodine deficiency is occurring at an epidemic rate across the United States. The consequences of iodine deficiency are severe: mental retardation, lowered IQ, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), infertility, thyroid problems, and cancer of the breast, prostate, ovaries, thyroid and uterus.

Unfortunately, a lot of misinformation exists about iodine. People are afraid to use iodine because of unsubstantiated rumors about this nutrient. This article, excerpted in part from my book, Iodine Why You Need It, Why You Can’t Live Without It, is written to provide the reader with the correct information about iodine so that the reader can make an educated decision about whether to supplement with this important nutrient.

For more than 100 years, iodine has been known as the element that is necessary for thyroid hormone production. However, it is rare to see any further mention of iodine’s other effects. Iodine is found in each of the trillions of cells in the body. Without adequate iodine levels, life itself is not possible.

Iodine is not only necessary for the production of thyroid hormone, it is also responsible for the production of all of the other hormones of the body. Adequate iodine levels are necessary for proper immune system function. Iodine contains potent antibacterial, antiparasitic, antiviral, and anticancer properties. Iodine is also effective for treating fibrocystic breasts and ovarian cysts. Table 1 lists some of the many benefits of iodine and some of the conditions that would benefit from adequate iodine supplementation.

Rest of the story:

[http://www.oasisadvancedwellness.com/learning/iodine-deficiency-under-recognized-epidemic.html](http://http://www.oasisadvancedwellness.com/learning/iodine-deficiency-under-recognized-epidemic.html)

Grizz

### Grizz — 2012-09-04T11:24:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-49322>

**

Dr. Daniels Speaks on iodine,**

I have been trying to get to a post about iodine for awhile. Besides Vitamin D, I think it is the most exciting essential element that I never learned much about in medical school, other than that the thyroid uses iodine to make thyroid hormone. Thanks to some wonderful physicians like Dr. David Brownstein, Dr. Guy Abraham, and Dr. Bernard Eskin, I have learned how vital iodine is to so many areas of the body. I am still upset about the fact that Dr. Eskin, one of the world’s foremost researchers on iodine and the breast, was actually a faculty member at Drexel University in Philadelphia, where I did my training. However, as a resident, I never even met this great man, let alone was exposed to his groundbreaking work. His research in rats established the iodine-dependent functioning of breast tissue, which is greatly increased by pregnancy and lactation. He further showed that when a chemical agent like percholorate was introduced to the breast, it blocked iodine functioning leading to development of atypia in the breast tissue. Molecular iodine actually has been shown to alter gene expression in breast cancer cells. There is also a clear association between fibrocystic breast and iodine deficiency. I have been amazed at what a difference iodine has made in my most severe fibrocystic patients. Ob/Gyns are taught to tell people to get rid of caffeine (good advice for this particular problem), use evening primrose oil and Vitamin E (also good advice), and that fibrocystic breast disease, now so common the term was changed to fibrocystic “changes”, are not pathologic but a “variation of normal.” Very little attention is paid to any hormonal component. To which I say regarding the latter, hogwash!!! Lumpy, knotty breasts, which in some, are so sensitive that even bras cause pain, are not normal! Yet, we have women taking synthetic estrogens and progestins (known to increase risk for breast cancer) for years on end, being iodine deficient as even iodized salt consumption (a devitalized product anyway) declines, Vitamin D deficiency being rampant which we know supports breast health, late or no pregnancies, and poor breastfeeding rates….and we wonder why the rate of breast cancer in 40 years has gone from 1 in 20 to 1 in 8. Add in exposure to xenoestrogens through hormones in meat and plastics, toxins like percholorate and bromide, insulin resistance causing estrogen dominance, lack of estrogen binders like fiber, flaxseed and cruciferous vegetables, and you have the perfect storm for breast disease, with the end of the line being a huge increase in breast cancer rates.

We know the thyroid is dependent upon iodine to make thyroid hormone, so it stands to reason that the many people with hypothyroidism are likely to be iodine deficient. And if you add in all the people with fibrocystic breast (many have both), you again have an epidemic deficiency of an essential element in the human body. We believe this is why women have more thyroid problems than men…their breasts take iodine away from their thyroid. You don’t have to have a thyroid problem or fibrocystic breast to be iodine deficient, however.

...... Where else is iodine important? The ovaries, the prostate, the skin, and the neurologic system. My husband and I have both observed in our practices an improvement in restless leg syndrome and leg cramps using iodine. Remember when you were a kid and scraped your knee? You got it painted with iodine, because of the great antimicrobrial properties, which is why it is used in operating rooms every day. Just like with breast cancer, we have a near epidemic of prostate cancer, but is it any wonder when men are exposed to the same excess estrogens in the environment and suffer the same iodine and Vitamin D deficiency that women do?

In summary, who should be tested for iodine deficiency? Everyone, but especially if you have thyroid problems, nodules, goiter, “benign” breast disorders, family history of breast cancer, restless leg, or prostate cancer.

Rest of the report:

[http://drpoppy.com/wordpress/?p=40](http://http://drpoppy.com/wordpress/?p=40)

More on iodine:

[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

Grizz

Kiwi,

Our members that were told to get mastectomies did not have breast cancer. They had fibrocystic breast disease which is lumpy bumpy breasts that eventually get very painful. One of our members described her breasts as feeling like bean bags, and she was told by 4 different doctors to get mastectomies! Dr. Derry says that most cases of breast cancer begin as Fibrocystic Breast Disease \[FBD\]. FBD is easily & quickly cured by iodine as proven by our neighbor. Iodine also prevents breast cancers as detailed by Dr. Derry.Our membership has not had a breast cancer patient yet. If we did, I would send her to the [BreastCancerChoices Group](http://http://breastcancerchoices.org/iprotocol.html)

It is also true that iodine kills cancer cells at the earliest stage by a process called apoptosis. Make sure your [Vitamin D3 level](http://http://tinyurl.com/Vitamin-D-Report) is at Dr. Kruse recommended level of 80 ng/ml to boost your immune system. Iodine does more than kill cancer cells. It also kills germs & virus \[if we are not iodine deficient\]. Iodine is supposed to be in the mucous that lines our nasal passages, lungs, stomach & intestines. The mucous then kills incoming virus germs as they enter our body. **Iodine is our own natural anti-biotic that never gets resistant to disease.**

If not taken care of, FBD can become cancerous and then it gets tough to cure. Dr. Fletchas has photos of breast cancers dramatically shrinking from iodine supplementation alone. ### Grizz — 2012-09-06T21:08:08+0300

> **Big Bobcat;50345 wrote:** Grizz,

>
> Also, does it make a difference what type of liquid you take it with. If I took it with coffee would it be better/worse or doesn't matter?
>

Bobcat,

It just doesn't matter which liquid that you use or how much. Your iodine will be absorbed into your system. What matters most of all is the co-supplements that you take and that you titrate slooowly from a small dose of iodine.

The big problem that we have that is tough to understand is actually getting iodine into our cells and the toxins out of our cells. Some people who have been severely deficient for so long have damaged their Iodide Symporters. The iodine goes in, and it goes out into the urine with little to no absorption into the cells. This is the reason for the range of co-supplement dosages.

The smaller dosage is for the majority of people who are able to get iodine into their cells (Iodide Symporters are working normally). The larger dosage and the optional co-supplements are for the minority who cannot get iodine into their cells.

[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements](http://http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements)

I am collecting more information on this subject - see Recommended Reading, chapter "Iodine - Absorption Problems-Iodide Symporter Problems"

[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

I am still not satisfied with the information I have on this problem, and am looking for better answers.

Fortunately, most of us do well with the minimal dosage of co-supplements.

Grizz

If you take iodine you MUST also take selenium and other [co-supplements](http://http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements). Selenium is required to prevent thyroid damage. See "Selenium" in Iodine References.

[http://goo.gl/G4dLP](http://http://goo.gl/G4dLP)

Both hypo & Hyper result from Toxic Halogen chemicals displacing iodine in your thyroid & other organs. [90% of Americans suffer](http://http://www.oasisadvancedwellness.com/learning/iodine-deficiency-under-recognized-epidemic.html) from severe iodine deficiency leading to thyroid problems, prostate, fibrocystic breasts, PCOS, Cysts, Uterus problems. This is because [halogen chemicals displace](http://http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine%20update.htm#section18) what little iodine we do have.

I don't have any experience with Tri-Iodine, but it is on Dr. Buist's recommended list:

[http://steppingstonesliving.com/resources/iodine/](http://http://steppingstonesliving.com/resources/iodine/)

Since she approves of it, then it is OK with me. I can't do or say anything without a doctor's blessing.

Lugols liquid & Iodoral caps are identical. Iodoral may be preferable for those who get an upset stomach from Lugols. Iodoral does not dissolve until it is well into the intestines, and so avoids upset stomach. Lugols is better to start out with because you can start with a MUCH smaller dose. It is also less expensive on a Per/mg basis. There is only 3.1 mg of iodine in one drop of 2% Lugols iodine. You might consider making your own Lugols for 1/10 the cost of Lugols. [http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions)

### Grizz — 2012-11-01T08:57:12+0200

> **AbetterME;62893 wrote:** Thank's Grizz!

>

abetter,

B2 & B3 is OK. What kind of iodine are you taking? Lugols is the recommended iodine, and it always comes with an eyedropper to meter out dosage. 2 types of Lugols are available, the 2% and the 5% . Lugols is required because it contains both iodine and iodide, both of which are required by our body. You can buy an eyedropper bottle at the drugstore if you need one. You can also buy Lugols Iodine that includes the eyedropper at Amazon.Com.

Lugol's 5% = Iodine 2.5 mg, Iodide 3.75 mg = 6.25 mgs total per drop

Lugol's 2% = Iodine 1.0 mg, Iodide 1. 5 mg = 2.5 mgs total per drop

Iodoral 50 mg = Iodine 20 mg, Iodide 30 mg

Iodoral 12.5 mg = Iodine 5 mg, Iodide 7.5 mg

\[USA Standards - Europe is different\]

There are 20 drops per ML so you have been taking about 19 drops per day or about 119mg daily. I'm surprised you didn't come down with more severe detox symptoms. You are very lucky. So yes, I think it is a good idea to take a break from the iodine until your sleep returns, then start on the new conservative schedule using a squeeze eyedropper to measure dosage.

Best to you,

Grizz

Product Description

Our full strength Lugols iodine solution contains 5% iodine and 10% potassium iodide in purified water according to the original formula. It may be used as a iodine supplement or to purify drinking water. Two drops contains 12.5mg of iodine.

If you are iodine deficient the dose recommendation is 6 drops a day for three months as a loading dose which is the amount of time required to restore healthy levels. After this a maintenance dose of 2 drops daily will surfice. Iodine levels must be monitored if high doses are being used for longer periods as elevated iodine levels can be harmful.

Glen,

I would avoid any product that contains Bromide, Fluoride, or chlorine. They all displace iodine in your body, including your thyroid. Your thyroid, testes & prostate demand iodine. IMO, there is no substitute for Lugols/iodoral which has the right amount of iodide and iodine in the correct ratio.

\* Our body requires both iodine AND iodide (lugols iodine & iodoral capsules recommended)

\- - - Iodine is used by the prostate + Stomach

\- - - Iodide (SSKI) is used by the thyroid + skin + Salivary

\- - - kidneys, spleen, liver, blood, breasts & intestines can use either form

Thyroid needs 3% prefers iodide (50mg iodine)

Skin needs 20% and prefers iodide

Salivary Glands prefer iodide

Muscles (650mg) and fat needs(700mg iodine) 70%

Breast tissue needs 3-5 mg / day minimum and prefers iodine

Prostate prefers iodine

Stomach prefers iodine

Lungs prefer iodide

Entire body stores 1500 mg 2gm total

\* Dr Brownstein Recommended supplements = Lugols Liquid and iodoral 12mg caps

\* The following are recommended for use in the iodine protocol. See Note below.

\* 2% Lugols Iodine = 2.5mg per drop ( Iodine 1.0 mg + Iodide 1. 5 mg )

\- - - Best for beginners just getting started in detox - Amazon Price

\* 5% Lugols Iodine = 6.25mg per drop ( Iodine 2.5 mg + Iodide 3.75 mg )

\- - - Best for users in advanced detox > 12mg a day - Amazon Price

\* Iodoral Tablet = 12.5mg per tablet ( Iodine 5 mg, Iodide 7.5 mg )

\- - - Best for users in advanced detox > 12mg a day - Amazon Price

\* Iodoral Tablet = 50 mg per tablet (Iodine 20 mg, Iodide 30 mg )

\- - - Best for users doing VERY Advanced Detox > 50mg a day - Amazon Price

\- - - or for serious disease under a Dr. care such as FBD or Cancers

The problem is primarily with liquid Lugols mixing with Vitamin C in the stomach. Iodoral is designed to be absorbed deep in the intestines, so can be taken with Vitamin C without a problem.

You can prove this by dropping a vitamin C tablet into water with Lugols. Note the brown color turns to clear as the iodine is converted into iodide.

My own take on this subject - IMO after people who have titrated up to 50mg of iodine, have reported that their hormone levels have been restored. There is a direct link between iodine sufficiency and our body hormone health. This is why our sex organs have their own iodine symporters. The uterus, ovaries, breasts, prostate & thyroid absorb iodine like a sponge. Iodine deficiency results in swelling of these organs as they attempt to grab iodine. My own sex life has been renewed by painting the scrotum with a watered down iodine solution. There is also a direct connection between the vulva & the ovaries/breast. Painting the vulva with iodine speeds recovery to the ovaries & breast area. Caution - when painting the skin with iodine, be sure to use an oil carrier such as coconut oil or grapeseed oil to avoid dermatitus. Either mix the oil & iodine in your palm or apply the oil right after the iodine. Dr. Kruse mentioned this topic once:

[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-285.html#post860414](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-285.html#post860414)

This short paragraph is what sent me off on the iodine trail. We can all thank Dr. Kruse for everything seen in this thread. Without his little paragraph above, I would not be here today, and I would still be suffering with prostate & erectile problems.

Best to you, and please keep us informed of your progress,

Carol,

You are doing good for getting started, and this is a complicated subject, so cheer up. Lugols is the best way to go because it is pure and contains both potassium iodide and iodine in the right ratio for our body.

\* Our body requires both iodine AND iodide (lugols iodine & iodoral capsules recommended)

\- - - Iodine is used by the prostate + Stomach

\- - - Iodide (SSKI) is used by the thyroid + skin + Salivary

\- - - kidneys, spleen, liver, blood, breasts & intestines can use either form

Thyroid needs 3% prefers iodide (50mg iodine)

Skin needs 20% and prefers iodide

Salivary Glands prefer iodide

Muscles (650mg) and fat needs(700mg iodine) 70%

Breast tissue needs 3-5 mg / day minimum and prefers iodine

Prostate prefers iodine

Stomach prefers iodine

Lungs prefer iodide

Entire body stores 1500 mg 2gm total

\* Dr Brownstein Recommended supplements = Lugols Liquid or iodoral 12mg caps

\* The following are recommended for use in the iodine protocol. See Note below.

\* 2% Lugols Iodine = 2.5mg per drop ( Iodine 1.0 mg + Iodide 1. 5 mg )

\- - - Best for beginners just getting started in detox - Amazon Price

\* 5% Lugols Iodine = 6.25mg per drop ( Iodine 2.5 mg + Iodide 3.75 mg )

\- - - Best for users in advanced detox > 12mg a day - Amazon Price

\* Iodoral Tablet = 12.5mg per tablet ( Iodine 5 mg, Iodide 7.5 mg )

\- - - Best for users in advanced detox > 12mg a day - Amazon Price

\* Iodoral Tablet = 50 mg per tablet (Iodine 20 mg, Iodide 30 mg )

\- - - Best for users doing VERY Advanced Detox > 50mg a day - Amazon Price

\- - - or for serious disease under a Dr. care such as FBD or Cancers

\*\*Note - Some European formulations measure the opposite where USA 5%=Euro 10%

How much iodine is in a drop? Depends on which country you live in. What counts is mg per drop.

\===========================================

\* Ideas for Starting really slow using a bottle of 2% Lugols iodine (3.1mg per drop) available at Amazon.Com

This procedure minimizes any chances for detox symptoms.

\- - - Mix 1 drop 2% Lugols into each of many 8 oz bottles of water, put the caps back on.

\- - - Week 1 drink 1 oz daily (about 380mcg iodone)

\- - - Week 2 drink 2 oz daily (about 775mcg iodine)

\- - - Week 3 drink 4 oz daily ( about 1.5mg iodine)

\- - - Week 4 drink 6 oz daily ( about 2.3mg iodine)

\- - - Week 5 drink 8 oz daily ( about 3.1 mg iodine or about 1 drop per day),

\- - - Week 6 drink 2 drops per day in a glass of water

\- - - Week 7 drink 3 drops per day in a glass of water

\- - - Week 8 drink 4 drops per day (about 12.5mg = to 1 iodoral)

\* Next, Titrate up with iodoral 12.5mg iodine tablets available at Amazon.Com

\- - - add 1/2 tablet each month to eventually reach 25mg daily-refer to Dr. Brownstein’s Book

\- - - - Iodine - Why you need it - Why you can’t Live Without it

\- - - 25-50mg Daily is the dosage target recommended by Dr. Brownstein to overcome toxins

\- - - 6 to 50mg Daily is his latest recommendation for maintenance (07/27/12)

\* If any detox symptoms appear do the salt flush and pulse dose. (all listed below)

\* \* Complete Details at [http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions)

You didn't say if you are taking the required co-supplements. These are required to protect your thyroid from damage and to get iodine into your cells and nasty chemicals out of your cells. They greatly help prevent detox symptoms.

Recommended minimum daily dosage for those in good health with minimal detox.

> If you decide to supplement with iodine, you should take these co-supplements:
>
> \- - Â½ tsp Natural Celtic Salt, Himalayan Pink Salt, Redmonds Salt or Hawaiin black salt
>
> \- - - If the sea salt is pure white, it is unacceptable (minerals have been stripped out )
>
> \- - 200mcg selenium (L-selenomethionine or Methylselenocystine preferred)
>
> \- - 400 mg Magnesium - Glycinate preferred
>
> \- - 2,000mg Vitamin C - Ester-C preferred -
>
> \- - ATP Cofactors caplet contains both 100mg B2 & 500mg B3 (Niacin) non-flushing in the correct ratio. Take 1/2 caplet with iodine up to 25mg, then one caplet for each 50 mg iodine,
>
> \- - - Don’t drink Fluoridated water !!
>
> Complete details here: [http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements](http://http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements)

Complete instructions at Iodine References:

[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

Hope this helps,

Grizz

### Grizz — 2012-11-09T21:44:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-63196>

@@ Andres,

I am here to promote Dr. Brownstein's proven & time tested iodine protocol. He has proven his protocol with many thousands of patients and over a period of years. Anyone can do anything they want. However I cannot recommend anything without proven doctors orders. If you have a complete doctor written protocol for what you are doing, then you should be fine. The iodine protocol is complicated enough without experimenting with unproven ideas. I follow doctors orders as closely as possible in everything I say.G

@@Carol,

\*\* do you think that means i have alot of the halides in my system?

Yes. Reduce your iodine dosage, do the salt flush and pulse dosing. I took 6 months to titrate up to 50mg

\*\* The bloating has gone away and I dropped an additional 1 1/2 lbs.

Yay!

\*\* Am I correct that I should use the salt daily OR the vitamin c?

Use both every day. Be sure it is natural salt with color. Pure white salt is not recommended, all the nutrients have been removed.

\*\* Also, did I read that the iodoral is easier on the gut?

YES and it is mainly easier on your stomach than liquid Lugols. It dissolves deep into the intestines for minimal reactions. Also, ATP Cofactors greatly helps get iodine into your cells and toxins out of your cells. It contains the required non-flushing B3 and has the right ratio of B2 to B3. Take 1/2 of an ATP for up to 25mg iodine and 1 ATP for each 50mg of iodine

@@ MsYo

> Started Protocol yesterday when I finally received my Lugols. I started with 6 drops of the 2% solution in water. I did fine no detox reactions. Today I did 12 drops twice. I am taking all the cosupplements except the ATP cofactor which I have ordered. My b vitamins in my blood are high due to other B supplementation. >

If not, then slow down and remember that haste makes waste when we are detoxing halide chemicals out of our body.

>
>

>

>

What counts is the mg per drop. Ask your druggist what the dosage is per drop (for both iodine & iodide). Then the problem is that you took too much iodine too soon. Both of your suffered detox symptoms. Start with 1 drop each into a bunch of 8 ounce water bottles. Then start by drinking 1 ounce daily of the mix week 1, 2 ounces week 2, 4 ounces week 3, 6 ounces week 4, and 8 ounces week 5. Then start titrating up by 1 more drop per week until you get to 50mg.

For magnesium, try Natural Calm powder that you mix into a liquid,. It helps you to sleep, so take it before bed.,

> \* Magnesium 400mg best=glycinate, citrate or malate
>
> \- - - Up to 600+mg may be required in cases of disease
>
> \- - - Why Magnesium, helps to prevent strokes
>
> \- - - Chelated minerals are widely regarded as the most assimilable
>

1) Get the J. Crows brand of Lugols.

2) Iodoral is just easier to use than counting a large number of drops. They are identical otherwise.

3) 2% is best to start with because we can start on a lower dose by mixing with water.

4) Place a patch of 2% lugols on your wrist and see how long it takes for the stain to disappear. It should last 24 hours or you are deficient. When I first started, the stain disappeared in only 3 minutes ! Now after 10 months, it lasts 8 hours, so I am still deficient.

You are missing some supplements:

Recommended minimum daily dosage for those in good health with minimal detox.

If you decide to supplement with iodine, you should take these co-supplements:

\- - Â½ tsp Natural Celtic Salt, Himalayan Pink Salt, Redmonds Salt or Hawaiin black salt

\- - - If the sea salt is pure white, it is unacceptable (minerals have been stripped out )

\- - 200mcg selenium (L-selenomethionine or Methylselenocystine preferred)

\- - 400 mg Magnesium - Glycinate preferred

\- - 2,000mg Vitamin C - Ester-C preferred -

\- - ATP Cofactors caplet contains both 100mg B2 & 500mg B3 (Niacin) non-flushing in the correct ratio. Take 1/2 caplet with iodine up to 25mg, then one caplet for each 50 mg iodine,

\- - - Don’t drink Fluoridated water !!

[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements](http://http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements)

Grizz

> PotentSea Organic Sea vegetable contain red brown and green marine algae. The five species that we use, kombu, bladderwrack, rockweed, sea lettuce and dulse are sustainably hand harvested and sun dried. ### Glen PDQ — 2012-11-14T19:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-64288>

There are companies selling Liposomal Iodine now. Does this sound like a generally good idea or are there bodily tissues where we don't want to force iodine into? The cells see these liposomal phosphatides (lecithin) and gobble them up. Once inside the cell the lecithin shell is digested and the substances within are released directly into the cell.

OTOH, introducing liposomal PQQ, ALA, and ALC sounds like a good idea to me. I saw a non-liposomal study summary that said feeding ALA and ALC together allowed the effective dose to be 100 to 1000 times less than giving ALA and ALC separately. Or in other words the researchers feeding the substances separately had to give 100 to 1000 times more to get the same effect.

### Grizz — 2012-11-14T19:59:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-64300>

MsYo,

Count your blessings. You have a great plan for your condition. However, be aware that detox symptoms may not show up right away. Some of our members have reported delayed detox months after starting on the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol. If and when detox symptoms appear, simply do the salt flush and the Pulse Dosing and if necessary drop back to a lower dosage that was problem free.

We have success stories in the Testimonials section for fibroids & Adrenal Issues:

[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

I suggest staying at 50mg for a month before moving higher. Our body does not usually respond as fast as we want. I know, I'm always saying SLOW DOWN, but only because I have too many bad reports from those who did too much too soon.

Please keep us informed on when your conditions clear up. I would love to add your story to our testimonials,

Grizz

### Grizz — 2012-11-14T20:07:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-64304>

> **Glen PDQ;66282 wrote: There are companies selling Liposomal Iodine now. Does this sound like a generally good idea or are there bodily tissues where we don't want to force iodine into? The cells see these liposomal phosphatides (lecithin) and gobble them up. Once inside the cell the lecithin shell is digested and the substances within are released directly into the cell.
>
>
>
> OTOH, introducing liposomal PQQ, ALA, and ALC sounds like a good idea to me. I saw a non-liposomal study summary that said feeding ALA and ALC together allowed the effective dose to be 100 to 1000 times less than giving ALA and ALC separately. Or in other words the researchers feeding the substances separately had to give 100 to 1000 times more to get the same effect.**

**

Glen,

I can't comment on your question. I think you want this question directed to Dr. Krus**?

Grizz

Grizz I can't wait to share my testimonial. I go for my ultrasound in December (followup) after refusing a hysterectomy that my OB/GYN strongly recommended. I can already tell the benefits of using Lugol's in the last week. I usually struggle with sluggishness upon waking but I have become more alert earlier and feel better. I am sleeping more deeply and count this a HUGE success considering 2 years ago I slept in 2 hour increments. I can't recall ever sleeping 8 hours in my entire adult life. ### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-16T02:35:58+0200

This thread is about team work in health. Even if you realize the fact that your health maybe in Permanent Beta testing, even if you develop a competitive advantage to ward off disease, even if you adapt your health trust plans to ever changing conditions...........even if you do these things but do so alone..........you’ll fall short. World-class professionals build networks to help them navigate the world. No matter how brilliant your mind or strategy is, if you’re playing a solo game in health, you’ll always lose out to a team approach. This thread shows how we all lend out hand to make starfish.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-11-17T00:17:02+0200
