Too many references to one drop....one drop from what lol....this isn't a very good measurement to use. How do you get one drop out of a bottle?....

I figured out the drop...I have a dropper in a bottle and I just let one drop out...but now I'm confused about the dosing..sometimes I feel really stupid...

This procedure minimizes any chances for detox symptoms.

\- - - Mix 1 drop 2% Lugols into each of many 8 oz bottles of water, put the caps back on.

\- - - Week 1 drink 1 oz daily (about 380mcg iodone)

\- - - Week 2 drink 2 oz daily (about 775mcg iodine)

\- - - Week 3 drink 4 oz daily ( about 1.5mg iodine)

\- - - Week 4 drink 6 oz daily ( about 2.3mg iodine)

\- - - Week 5 drink 8 oz daily ( about 3.1 mg iodine or about 1 drop per day),

\- - - Week 6 drink 2 drops per day in a glass of water

\- - - Week 7 drink 3 drops per day in a glass of water

\- - - Week 8 drink 4 drops per day (about 12.5mg = to 1 iodoral)

Week one drink one ounce of what daily? I don't think its 1 ounce of Iodine...is it one drop in 8 ounces of water? Followed....oh wait is it 1 drop in 8 ounces week one followed by 1 drop in 8 ounces of water twice week 2?

I'm so confused...I'm going to take one drop in 8 ounces of water..for now can't wait for explanation because my brain is dead obviously.

Think I figured it out....You drink one ounce of an 8 ounce glass that has 1 dropper in it. I would like to know how its determined that there is 380 mcg of Iodine in 1 oz. I suppose naturally the Iodine naturally balances out in the water and it ends up having 380 mcg per ounce? I guess this was tested somehow?

### nonchalant — 2012-11-17T00:59:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-64847>

One drop (not one dropper) of the 2% Lugols has about 3mg of iodine in it.

When you put one drop in 8 oz, and only drink one ounce at a time, you will be taking 1/8 of a drop of iodine, or about .380mg iodine.

When you put the one drop in, it disperses evenly.

BTW, Raiken, I cannot take this dose yet, .380mg iodine. I'm still working up to taking the lightest recommended dose, and it's been a couple months since I started.

### Glen PDQ — 2012-11-17T01:08:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-64850>

> **Raiken3712;66836 wrote:
>
>
>
> This procedure minimizes any chances for detox symptoms.
>
> \- - - Mix 1 drop 2% Lugols into each of many 8 oz bottles of water, put the caps back on.
>
> \- - - Week 1 drink 1 oz daily (about 380mcg iodone)
>
> \- - - Week 2 drink 2 oz daily (about 775mcg iodine)
>
> \- - - Week 3 drink 4 oz daily ( about 1.5mg iodine)
>
> \- - - Week 4 drink 6 oz daily ( about 2.3mg iodine)
>
> \- - - Week 5 drink 8 oz daily ( about 3.1 mg iodine or about 1 drop per day),
>
> \- - - Week 6 drink 2 drops per day in a glass of water
>
> \- - - Week 7 drink 3 drops per day in a glass of water
>
> \- - - Week 8 drink 4 drops per day (about 12.5mg = to 1 iodoral)
>
>
>
> Week one drink one ounce of what daily?**

**

During Week #1 drink 1 ounce/per day of water from the 8 ounce bottle of water which has **1 drop of iodine in it.

During Week #2 drink 2 ounces/per day of water from a bottle of water which has **1 drop of iodine in it.

During Week #3 drink 4 ounces/per day of water from a bottle of water that has 1 drop** of iodine in each bottle.

During Week #4 drink 6 ounces/per day of water from a bottle of water that has 1 drop** of iodine in each bottle.

During Week #5 drink 8 ounces/per day of water from a bottle of water that has 1 drop** of iodine in each bottle.

During Week #6 drink an entire 8 ounce glass of water every day with 2 drops iodine in it.

During Week #7 drink an entire 8 ounce glass of water every day with 3 drops iodine in it.

During Week #8 drink an entire 8 ounce glass of water every day with 4 drops iodine in it.

Hope this made your instructions a bit more meaningful. If you drank a whole glass of water on day 1 of week #1 with 1 drop of iodine then, according to your instructions, you drank a whole weeks worth of iodine. oO

The water bottles I use are 16 ounce bottles.

### Grizz — 2012-11-17T13:07:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-64915>

Raiken,

The 8 ounce bottle has 1 drop of iodine added, not a dropper. A dropper is about 10 drops. Then drink 1 ounce daily of this water/iodine mix each day for the 1st week. This gets you started on 1/8th of a drop per day. Trust me, this is a LOT of iodine to start because most people are SEVERELY toxified with halogen chemicals, and easily get detox symptoms. You divide the MG per drop of iodine by 8 to get the dosage you are taking.

You can be more aggressive if you want, but you risk getting nasty detox symptoms such as pimples, bloat, headaches, and more. You also risk taking sick days at work. Some have started at 100mg and have had minimal detox symptoms. Everyone is so different and there is no way to know in advance how bad your detox will be or even how it will present. I don't want to see anyone getting sick from detox, so I recommend everyone to SLOOOW DOWN and start SMALL. The 6 months will come one way or the other. My iodine stain disappeared in only 3 minutes when I started in Jan.

Iodine gets rid of Fluoride, Bromide, & chlorine that has displaced iodine in your thyroid & body cells. You will find a lot of conflicting & false information on iodine on the internet, so the only people you can trust on this subject are the iodine doctors. If you want accurate information on iodine, see my "Recommended Reading" here:

[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

You will also find files on hyper/hypo that explains why iodine deficiency causes both.

Grizz

### Grizz — 2012-11-17T21:39:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-64992>

Raiken,

@@ how long does detox usually take to show up?

Everyone is so different. Sometimes detox symptoms show up within just a few days and sometimes detox illness is delayed for several months. Sometimes detox won't even show up at all until you reach 100mg. Sometimes it strikes people with only 1mg of iodine. You will know when it comes, then just do the Salt Flush & the Pulse Dose.

Boron is one of the Optional Supplements. I don't take it, and it is not on the Dr. Brownstein List of required supplements. The experts on Boron are at the Curezone.

[http://curezone.com/forums/f.asp?f=815&p=265](http://http://curezone.com/forums/f.asp?f=815&p=265)

Do a search on their site for "Boron" for all of their reasons for recommending it. My advice is limited to what Dr. Brownstein says. IMO, Dr. Brownstein wrote the Bible on Iodine.

We can thank Dr. Kruse for all of my documents. He is my favorite doctor, and he inspired me to do all of that research on iodine. I could not stand to see how women were being so grossly abused by our medical system for their severe iodine deficiency symptoms. I decided to get the word out to as many people as possible. That is why those files are all public on the internet.

Especially when I learned my neighbor was suffering from painful fibrocystic breasts, and her doctor told her to learn to live with them or else get a double mastectomy! She started the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol and her pain was gone in less than 1 week, and her lumps/bumps were gone in less than 1 month. She has now started her neighbor on iodine, who is suffering with uterus fibroids AND PCOS. This woman's doctor said that hysterectomy was her only choice.

WHY aren't doctors recommending iodine????? THAT is the mystery, especially in view of our vast collection of testimonial success stories.

[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

Grizz

> I guess maybe I need an adrenal panel....have to wait on it though I'm not going in for awhile due to money constraints....sheesh this makes me really curious but I don't have the money to exercise my curiosity.Â  What besides Cycloset can I do to reverse the bad cortisol?Â  Leptin Reset...anything else?Â  I suppose I have never strictly followed the LR ...for a significant amount of time.Â  I need to try harder.Â  I'm guessing my cortisol is higher later and lower in the morning and it has something to do with my temperature being warmer later...but just guesses.

Most peoples temps aren't right at 98.6 upon waking.Â  Our temps go up as the day goes on.Â  Usually the highest is around 3-5pm, I believe, and then they head downward again.Â  Chart your temps throughout the day and if you aren't getting close to 98.4ish there may be other issues going on.Â  But everyone is different so keep that in mind too.Â

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-04T10:33:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-69287>

**chocolate said**

Ive just found this thread and the stuff about breast problems. Since being LCHF ,although my weight has stayed stable (no weight loss....boooo) I have noticed that my breasts have grown bigger. From being a well endowed 34G I am now an even better endowed 34H.I have noticed that when I lie on my belly there is a slight tenderness although nothing like I used to experience before menopause. I am 60 now and would have thought all this hormone stuff was behind me but am beginning to suspect eostrogen dominance. I have a history of fibroids .I live in the UK ,bromide has been banned since sometime in the 1990s but I do eat 20 gms daily of dark (85%) chocolate and usually 5gms organic powder in a drink daily., So I was surprised to see the post possibly connecting chocolate with this problem.
So, although our water is flouridated I tend to drink bottled spring water and filter the tap water.
I have Lugols 7% but have been hit and miss about supplementing due to conflicting advise omnipresent on these forums.I had begun to wonder if the increased fat content of the diet had contributed .So, I am very confused.
I am sure that soemone will say 'get tested' but here in the UK you cannot get tested unless there is reason to believe its necesary by your Doc. If I go and say I am eating a huge amount of sat fat and my breasts have got bigger !! what will he say ??
Barbara
sorry Ive made a hash of this !

[http://www.breastcancerchoices.org/iprotocol.html](http://http://www.breastcancerchoices.org/iprotocol.html)
About the Iodine Supplementation Protocol
\--- Not Just For Breast Cancer Patients
Physicians Guy Abraham, MD, David Brownstein, MD, and Jorge Flechas, MD, have treated more
than 4,000 patients with iodine supplementation. The protocol below is suggested by their
writings and lectures. We acknowledge with appreciation their pioneering research and
generous contributions to the field of Iodine Therapy. See references below the protocol.
Iodine and Companion Nutrients
From October 2007 Iodine Conference
50 mg Iodoral minimum for breast cancer (may start with 12.5 mg).
Some practitioners may recommend another form of iodine such as Lugol's solution.
Iodoral is the Lugol's formula in tablet form especially designed to avoid gastric irritation.
Vitamin C - 3,000 mg per day (more may be necessary to detox bromide).
300-600 mg magnesium oxide (Iodine Investigation Project participants prefer
magnesium glycinate or magnesium citrate.)
200 mcg selenium. The selenomethionine version is preferred by many.
500 mg niacin (B3) twice a day (NOT niacinamide). Start lower to avoid flush. \*See update
in left margin on the ATP Cofactors as an alternative.
100 mg Vitamin B2 three times a day. \*See update in left margin on the ATP Cofactor as
an alternative.
A comprehensive vitamin and nutrition program.
(Feb 2008) Dr. Guy Abraham cautions that "excess calcium supplementation (2,000-
3,000 md/day) has been the most common cause of poor response to iodine
supplementation." Vitamin Research News Vol. 22. Number 2.
24 Hour Iodine Loading Test Now Essential
The Iodine specialists have recently discovered it is essential to have the 24 Hour Iodine
Loading Test before implementing iodine supplementation in order to investigate the presence
of a possible iodine absorption defect. If a patient has a normal or high Loading Test score in the
presence of known iodine deficiency condition such as breast or thyroid disease, a defect in the
Sodium Iodide Symporter (NIS) mechanism is suspected. The iodine taken during the loading
test may pass into the urine unabsorbed giving a false normal reading. Follow your physician's
iodine dosing carefully to see how the iodine test result changes.
The doctors find that the most common side effects are usually resolved with 3 grams of
vitamin C and/or the salt loading protocol.
Be aware of iodine-related bromide detox symptoms. Symptoms of "bromism" may
occur in some people. Click here for a list of recorded bromide symptoms. Patients are
usually advised to cut back on iodine, \*pulse-dose (see below) and salt-load more
frequently.
Important: The following protocol should be implemented only under your doctor's supervision
and monitored with the appropriate laboratory work and thyroid studies. Report any side effects
to your physician. Do not take iodine if you are allergic to iodized salt.
Salt Loading Protocol for Possible Bromide Side Effects
Orthoiodosupplementation induced and increased mobilization of bromine from storage sites,
with increased urinary excretion of bromide. --Guy Abraham, MD
Certain iodine-literate doctors feel the bromide excreted by iodine therapy can be eliminated
more efficiently by the chloride present in mineral-rich unprocessed sea salt. The salt is added
to water in a specific way. Eating salty food is not effective. Table salt is only used in an
emergency.
Many iodine takers found the Salt Loading Protocol devised by Dr. William Shevin (and
presented at the February '07 Iodine Conference) to be effective in eliminating the side effects
such as bromide sedation, acne, brain fog, brassy taste, mouth sores, frontal headache or other
symptoms which occur in a small percentage of patients.
1/4 teaspoon salt dissolved in 1/2 cup warm water. Follow immediately with 12-16 oz
pure water.
Repeat in 30-45 minutes if needed. May repeat again until copious urination begins.
Observe subjective response (usually within several hours).
Iodine Investigation Project participants have found 1/2 teaspoon salt dissolved in the
recommended amount of water works faster than the 1/4 teaspoon dosage.
\* Pulse dosing of iodine has often helped our participants with detox symptoms: taking a 48
hour break from iodine supplementation as needed seems to enhance the kidneys' ability to
excrete bromide. These are only our observations as educated patients. Consult your own
physician about your particular iodine regimen.
No consensus exists on how important the salt loading may be to an iodine
therapy strategy if the patient does not have adverse symptoms.
Be sure to ask your doctor before implementing salt loading or any medical
strategy.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-19T01:21:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-81028>

SO BIZARRE - this morning I put my iodine in my glass, then added water, and _it disappeared_!!!! No color, no taste left!

So then I thought about what I had done just before that - taken vit C in that cup. I suspected a chemical reaction, googled it, and sure enough - ascorbic acid neutralizes iodine! I think I read that it changes into iodide. Now, I thought I had drunken (drank? drunk?) all that C, and can't imagine that what trace of it was left was enough to neutralize 1 3/4 dropperfuls of iodine, but appparently, yes!

So I'm thinking that you probably shouldn't take iodine & C at the same time, and maybe y'all already know that, but yes, I'm figuring that what happened in my cup would happen in the gut, and that if iodide was just as useful, it would be sold in that form, since it doesn't have the obvious taste.

MsYo - I have used NDT for T3CM, and it works. Seriously works. But I don't know what you mean by dosing iodine for that particular protocol. I only know what I learned of it on STTM.

I was so amazed by his little paragraph that I could not believe I knew nothing of iodine. How many MILLIONS of women were suffering like this???? Just the thought of all of these suffering women brought chills down my **spine. As it turned out, there is indeed a pandemic of iodine deficiency. Here is that little paragraph that started it all:**

> **_One clinical finding I always ask about is the presence of fibrocystic breast disease. When it is present I immediately think the woman is likely suffering from an undiagnosed iodine deficiency. I distinctly remember in medical school seeing a woman’s fibrocystic disease cured in less than an hour by an ancient physician who taught us how to examine a women’s breasts. She had volunteered to be a guinea pig for us during our medical education, and on this day we could not examine her without making her scream and cry because her breasts were so tender to palpation. Our MD proctor was about 80 years old and he too was a volunteer helping us learn our craft. He stopped me from examining her breasts and asked her a few questions and then he asked us to leave the room. He applied Lugol’s solution to her vulva and her nipples and we were able to examine her without any pain a half hour later. It made a huge impression on me. He asked her if she was on any thyroid meds and if she had bad energy or weight gain. A lack of iodine was the source of her painful breasts. Her labs did not reveal any major issues when we saw her. I never forgot this lesson on the thyroid._**
> [WHAT ARE THE TOP TEN PALEO SUPPLEMENTS?](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/) - Jack Kruse

A bra was too painful to wear at times, nipples untouchable, showering hurt...it was horrendous.

She kind of blew off my lifestyle change as the cause of the improvement.

Highly recommend these 2 supplements.....full of good stuff!!

I have recently added 25mg Iodoral and RO spring water and have noticed quite a boost in my mood, a tiny bit more energy, swelling of thyroid down, and candida improving...thanks to the wonderful knowledge shared on this thread.

I've often wondered if there is any correlation between adrenal health and breast health....as I have had such drastic improvements in both.

### Grizz — 2013-03-22T21:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-81477>

Destiny,

Yes, I do have some comments. Please beware of painting iodine to the skin. You can get serious dermatitis if you paint iodine directly on the same skin area every day. The proper way to do this is to mix the iodine with an oil in the palm of your hand before applying to the breast. An oil such as grapeseed oil, olive oil, coconut oil. Some suggest painting to the breast first then immediately apply the oil after. Yet another possibility is to paint one breast one day the other breast the next day, alternating breasts each day. Or alternate skin areas each day. This allows the skin time to recover from iodine treatments. I'm not certain which works the best, please give me some feedback. But yes, painting the breast is a good idea. Strangely, painting iodine to the vulva also speeds iodine to the breast. Use the same precautions to guard against dermatitis. You might want to check in with the [Yahoo Iodine Group](http://health.groups.yahoo.com/group/iodine/) to ask which method they prefer to avoid dermatitis. If you have already determined the best way to avoid dermatitis, please let me know what you have discovered and I'll pass it on.

6mg of iodine & 1 drop of iodine is not nearly enough for fighting cancers. The experienced members at the [CureZone Iodine Group](http://curezone.com/forums/f.asp?f=815) and the iodine doctors typically recommend titrating up to at least 100mg daily iodoral with all of the required co-supplements. Listing of [required co-supplements here](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references) You are certain to have detox symptoms while titrating up to 100mg. So practice the Salt Loading, and pulse dosing if you notice any severe detox symptoms. Detox is only temporary, and some have forged ahead toward 100mg in spite of detox to get to the resolution they are after asap.

Then please reduce or eliminate as much as possible your intake of bromide, fluoride & chlorine. These are goitrogens which kick iodine out of your system. Most breads contain bromide, so buy the "Bromide Free Bread" and don't eat anything made from flour. Find out if your water supply has fluoride or chlorine. If it does, then you will have to drink [distilled water](http://www.amazon.com/Water-Distiller-Countertop-Enamel-Collection/dp/B00026F9F8) or fluoride free spring water.

I also suggest a Paleo Diet rich in dark green veggies, natural foods and no sugars, no sugary drinks, no high fructose corn syrup, no fruit juices (too much sugar) . A low carb ketogenic diet helps. Cancers thrive on sugars and carbs are converted into sugars by our body. Buy some keto sticks from the drugstore ( they are behind the counter) to verify your ketogenic urine readings.

Cancers hate oxygen. So exercise can help to increase oxygen blood levels.

One last thing, take 20,000 IU daily of Vitamin D3 Gelltabs made with olive oil (not soy oil). Then in 3 months [get your blood tested](http://www.vitamindcouncil.org/about-vitamin-d/vitamin-d-deficiency/am-i-vitamin-d-deficient/) and adjust dosage to be between 80 & 100 ng/ml (on the high side of normal where normal = 60 to 70 ng/ml). Vitamin D3 is ESSENTIAL to boost your immune system to help fight off your cancers ! If your immune system is weak, it may not be able to properly use the iodine to kill off your cancer cells.

PS) My [Iodine References](https://docs.google.com/document/d/139Xa8u8kgNVT-K17TxYvsM7qsSfusxyvloIXWbU92rg/edit#) includes a list of iodine trained doctors. I suggest you consider seeing one of these iodine doctors to assist with your cancer issues.

ALSO please spend some quality time here at [BreastCancerChoices Group](http://breastcancerchoices.org/iprotocol.html). Lynne Farrow is an expert at curing cancers with iodine, as she cured her own FBS & breast cancer with iodine.

I hope these tips help, Please keep us informed of your progress,
Grizz

Thank you, Grizz, for a wealth of information. You saved me some grief with dermatitis that would come after extended application. I will keep going up with iodine. ### Grizz — 2013-03-23T10:45:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-81517>

> [Patty Cakes said:](/goto/post?id=81488)
>
> Thanks for the info you wrote to Destiny, Grizz! I had an aha moment reading it. I was painting iodine on my breasts every day and after about 7-10 days I got real itchy on my nipples and stopped the iodine. I've had previous problems, I think, with iodine before but I am going to try the oil/iodine combo now. Also DH broke out on his stomach/chest area where he was painting iodine which is a perfect example of dermititis. Your words put it all together for me.
>
> **What's you opinion on Nascent iodine versus Lugols?**

Patty Cakes,
**b. Not Recommended**
i. Nascent (aka Magnascent, Detoxified, or Atomidine) – Based on
Edgar Cayce’s “energized” iodine. The iodine is reduced to a 1% concentration
in 100% ethyl alcohol and electro-magnetically transmuted (while
being suspended in a wet bath containing a mild acid solution) into the
Atomic state. 1 drop = 150 microgram (mcg) or .150 mg of Iodine. This
form will not saturate body tissues and has not been proven to detox
halides such as bromide, fluoride, chloride, and mercury, as other forms
have. This form of iodine is not recommended on this protocol as the goal
is tissue saturation and halide detoxification / protection.

Download the **[The Guide to Supplementing with Iodine](http://steppingstonesliving.com/resources/iodine/)** for more. This excellent guide should be on everyone's desk.

There is an exception to painting the skin. In cases where you have a skin lesion, mole or a cancerous lesion, you want to carefully paint the lesion but NOT the surrounding skin 2 x daily. After a few weeks, the lesion will become itchy and fall off, leaving fresh new skin. This does work as I have used this procedure myself. I used the eraser tip of a pencil to carefully apply iodine to the lesion & not the good skin. Iodine does kill cancers & cancerous tissue. For more details on this see "[Recommended Reading References](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)," chapter Cancer
This [old iodine pencil would have been ideal](http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=2048106#i) for painting skin lesions.

Best to you,
Grizz

### Grizz — 2013-03-23T16:31:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-81534>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=81531)
>
> Hi Grizz. Will it work on warts as well?

Diane,
Yes it will remove the wart AND kill the virus that causes it so it never grows back again.

> **How to Remove Warts with Iodine**
>
> I am listing this method first, because it is one I have personally used many times on myself, and a couple of times on my kids. I learned this method many years ago from a coworker who grew up in a small, "countrified" Texas town. I had never had warts before, and one popped up on my hand. I went to the drugstore, got some Compound W, and applied it as directed. My wart turned into an ugly, peeling mess. It eventually came off but grew right back.
>
> My coworker told me that her family had always used iodine to get rid of warts. I went back to the drugstore and purchased a small, very inexpensive bottle labeled "Iodine Tincture." Although no prescription is needed to purchase iodine tincture, most pharmacies keep it behind the prescription counter, so you'll have to ask for it.
>
> Here's what to do:
>
> Small bottles of iodine tincture usually have a self-storing plastic dabber inside, attached to the inside of the lid. Several times throughout the day, use the dabber to place a drop of iodine onto your wart and let it sink in. Warts are porous, so the iodine will sink in quickly. It does not burn or hurt in any way, but iodine has that brownish-red color, and it will stain warts, which makes them more noticeable. If you're going somewhere special, covered it with a BandAid. Otherwise, keep it uncovered because that makes it easier to dab the iodine on. Also, seeing your little brownish-red bump serves as a reminder to put iodine on it now and then throughout the day.
> Eventually, the wart will fall off and never grew back -- at least that's how it has always gone for me. I have used this method many times, and sometimes it takes ten days or a couple of weeks, and sometimes it takes several weeks to a couple of months. Every time I have used the iodine method, the wart has never grown back. I am no doctor, but I think that might be because the iodine actually kills the human papilloma virus, whereas some other methods do not. I would love for someone knowledgeable on the virus to chime in in this theory in the comments section.
> [http://smartandfun.hubpages.com/hub...-and-Cheap-Remedies-You-May-Not-Have-Heard-Of](http://smartandfun.hubpages.com/hub/How-to-Remove-Warts-at-Home-Painless-Easy-and-Cheap-Remedies-You-May-Not-Have-Heard-Of)

I would use Lugols instead of tincture. Also be very careful to keep it off of your normal skin, so you don't get dermatitis.

[Here is a fascinating tip](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-249.html#post836849) for the man in your life, to boost his manliness. I now use Lugols watered down 25% Lugols & 75% water seems to work just as well.

Grizz

> [MamaGrok said:](/goto/post?id=81536)
>
> So I worked up to 1.5 dropperfuls of 2% Lugol's per day, and I think I saw some benefits. I'm still taking Se every day, as I did for at least a year before starting iodine.
> My dropper is never full, though; I can only get it about half way full.
> When do I stop? Do I keep increasing to 2 dropperfuls, or hold steady? What signs am I looking for? When I hit those signs, do I hold steady forever, or gradually back down?

You have a very good question. The goal for iodine per day depends on your circumstances, diet & health. If you eliminate most of the bromide, fluoride & chlorine in your life, I think you can get by with 12 mg daily, or 1-iodoral caplet. 2% iodine is about 3mg per drop, so 4 drops per day.

If you drink fluoridated water, eat bromide in bread, and get exposed in other ways, you will need more iodine. Dr. Brownstein suggests 50mg daily. If you have cancers, fibrocystic breasts, etc, they recommend 100mg per day.

I am taking 50mg of iodoral with [required co-supplements.](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1t_6oprpjmDCMqwCXP_6BMf0Mbr9DivmOSALeJTjReuY/edit#heading=h.kgxowshu3xh3)
I plan to drop down to 12mg daily of iodoral this fall, this is after 1 year at 50mg. We need iodine for the rest of our lives.
Especially women because their female organs require a lot of iodine to remain healthy.

Grizz

### Destiny — 2013-03-24T18:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-81626>

This might be a silly question. Does the Lugol solution have to be mixed with oil? Or is ok to put an emu oil on first, then to paint the iodine on top of that after? Does it make any difference?

### Grizz — 2013-03-24T20:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-81638>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=81626)
>
> This might be a silly question. Does the Lugol solution have to be mixed with oil? Or is ok to put an emu oil on first, then to paint the iodine on top of that after? Does it make any difference?

Destiny,

Oil does not need to be mixed with iodine to apply to our skin. The problem we run into is when iodine is applied to the same spot of skin every day, we will suffer dermatitis sooner or later. One approach people use is to apply iodine to a different patch of skin each day and allow the skin to rest for a few days. Or alternate breasts painting one breast one day and the other breast the next day. Or another approach is to mix the oil with iodine in the palm before applying. Another approach is to apply the oil after the iodine.

I don't think the iodine will be absorbed if you apply the oil first. Try it and let us know. Has anyone tried this? Comments?

One last thought. Lugols is extremely concentrated. An approach I use to paint the testicles is to water the Lugols down to 75% water & 25% Lugols. This concentration is too weak to cause dermatitis. However it still works to enhance libido and helps eliminate erectile dysfunctions.

Grizz

I guess using more than one method might work for different things? I'm sure some discussion of this is in the thread...but I'm sure its buried. Its not bad to drink is it? ### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-25T07:07:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-81675>

> [Grizz said:](/goto/post?id=81517)
>
> Patty Cakes,
> **b. Not Recommended**
> i. Nascent (aka Magnascent, Detoxified, or Atomidine) – Based on
> Edgar Cayce’s “energized” iodine. The iodine is reduced to a 1% concentration
> in 100% ethyl alcohol and electro-magnetically transmuted (while
> being suspended in a wet bath containing a mild acid solution) into the
> Atomic state. 1 drop = 150 microgram (mcg) or .150 mg of Iodine. This
> form will not saturate body tissues and has not been proven to detox
> halides such as bromide, fluoride, chloride, and mercury, as other forms
> have. This form of iodine is not recommended on this protocol as the goal
> is tissue saturation and halide detoxification / protection.
> Grizz

Apparently there are some rare cases where microgram doses as in Atomidine or similar formulation may be a good idea. Dr. Brownstein did mention it was rare, but describes someone in his practice that was sensitive to many different things and had problems with taking Lugol's/Iodoral. Its in Iodine Why You Need It Why You Can't Live Without It 4th Edition..probably in others but thats the edition I have.

He also mentioned some people who needed to take a break from supplementation and not do it 7 days a week. I'm not sure how you would determine if you needed to do that besides having a holistic practitioner helping you or perhaps some other on line resource.

In general you are probably right about Nascent etc...though. Lugol's/Iodoral seems to be the best as its mostly what Dr. Brownstein talks about in the book. He only has one mention about the Atomidine use.

Just figured I'd post about it since I just read it.

> One clinical finding I always ask about is the presence of fibrocystic breast disease. When it is present I immediately think the woman is likely suffering from an undiagnosed iodine deficiency. I distinctly remember in medical school seeing a woman’s fibrocystic disease cured in less than an hour by an ancient physician who taught us how to examine a women’s breasts. She had volunteered to be a guinea pig for us during our medical education, and on this day we could not examine her without making her scream and cry because her breasts were so tender to palpation. Our MD proctor was about 80 years old and he too was a volunteer helping us learn our craft. He stopped me from examining her breasts and asked her a few questions and then he asked us to leave the room. He applied Lugol’s solution to her vulva and her nipples and we were able to examine her without any pain a half hour later. It made a huge impression on me. He asked her if she was on any thyroid meds and if she had bad energy or weight gain. A lack of iodine was the source of her painful breasts. Her labs did not reveal any major issues when we saw her. I never forgot this lesson on the thyroid.

BILLIONS are being spent to advertise risky drugs with absurd side effects, yet the Mainstream Media is CENSORING iodine ? ?

### Grizz — 2013-03-29T00:12:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-82095>

> [MsYo said:](/goto/post?id=82088)
>
> Grizz---- I have been on iodine for about 5 months now. I'm up to 100mgs daily with no detox sypmptoms. I have low free T3 and hypothyroid symptoms (low body temp, low morning cortisol, etc). Should I up my dosage of iodine even higher? My fibroids have gotten smaller though I haven't had an ultrasound since starting iodine therapy. I did start dosing with Thyro-Gold within the last 2 weeks and have seen an improvement in my body temps. Any insights would be appreciated.

MsYo,
100mg of ioldine is more than enough so I don't recommend going any higher. Are you also taking the required co-supplements? [http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Supplements)
\- - Â½ tsp Natural Celtic Salt, Himalayan Pink Salt, Redmonds Salt or Hawaiin black salt
\- - - If the sea salt is pure white, it is unacceptable - minerals have been stripped out
\- - 200mcg selenium (L-selenomethionine or Methylselenocystine preferred)
\- - 400 mg Magnesium - Glycinate preferred
\- - 2,000mg Vitamin C - Ester-C preferred -
\- - ATP Cofactors caplet contains both 100mg B2 & 500mg B3 (Niacin) non-flushing in the correct ratio. Take 1/2 caplet with iodine up to 25mg, then one caplet for each 50 mg iodine, see notes below
\- - - Don’t drink Fluoridated water
\- - - Cost Per Day for required supplements = 75 cents/day, 12.5mg iodoral = 25 cents/day
\- - - All prices from Amazon.com ( Free shipping > $25 )

The thyroid requires both iodine AND selenium. You may also need thyroid meds if your thyroid doesn't respond to the iodine. Over many years of iodine deficiency, the thyroid could be permanently damaged and you could require thyroid meds for life. But you also need iodine. When your fibroids have been cured, you can reduce your iodine down to 25 to 50 mg. However, I have heard of cases where the thyroid has come back to life after 1 to 2 years of taking iodine, and they had to reduce and even eliminate the thyroid meds.

Read my [references on the thyroid.](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.lu403wxsogr2)

> For more than 100 years, iodine has been known as the element that is necessary for thyroid hormone production. However, it is rare to see any further mention of iodine’s other effects. Iodine is found in each of the trillions of cells in the body. Without adequate iodine levels, life itself is not possible.
>
> Iodine is not only necessary for the production of thyroid hormone, it is also responsible for the production of all of the other hormones of the body. Adequate iodine levels are necessary for proper immune system function. Iodine contains potent antibacterial, antiparasitic, antiviral, and anticancer properties. Iodine is also effective for treating fibrocystic breasts and ovarian cysts. >

Iodine deficiency disorder can result in mental retardation, goiter, increased child and infant mortality, infertility, and socioeconomic decline.1 Iodine deficiency disorder is the most common preventable form of mental retardation known. ### Grizz — 2013-04-01T18:37:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-82473>

I have been asked if ALL of the co-supplements are required for life. That was a great question, and I had to consult with Lynne Farrow, author of [the book](http://www.amazon.com/The-Iodine-Crisis-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X/ref=sr_1_cc_1?s=aps&ie=UTF8&qid=1364510821&sr=1-1-catcorr&keywords=the+iodine+crises), "The Iodine Crisis: What You Don't Know About Iodine Can Wreck Your Life."
Here is the co-supplements listing:
Recommended minimum daily dosage for those in good health with minimal detox.

> If you decide to supplement with iodine, you should take these co-supplements:
> \- - Â½ tsp Natural Celtic Salt, Himalayan Pink Salt, Redmonds Salt or Hawaiin black salt
> \- - - If the sea salt is pure white, it is unacceptable - minerals have been stripped out
> \- - 200mcg selenium (L-selenomethionine or Methylselenocystine preferred)
> \- - 400 mg Magnesium - Glycinate preferred
> \- - 2,000mg Vitamin C - Ester-C preferred -
> \- - ATP Cofactors caplet contains both 100mg B2 & 500mg B3 (Niacin) non-flushing in the correct ratio. Take 1/2 caplet with iodine up to 25mg, then one caplet for each 50 mg iodine, see notes below
> \- - - Don’t drink Fluoridated water

Lynne said, "I think if a person is iodine sufficient and has no symptoms after a year they could drop the ATP Cofactors companion nutrients. Selenium, Vitamin C and magnesium should be maintained. It's important not to supplement (excessive) \*\*calcium because that purges the magnesium."

I'll update [Iodine References](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references) with this guideline.

\*\* (Feb 2008) Dr. Guy Abraham cautions that "excess calcium supplementation (2,000-
3,000 md/day) has been the most common cause of poor response to iodine
supplementation." Vitamin Research News Vol. 22. Number 2."

Grizz

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=82479)

> Wow, Grizz, I never heard of that....calcium purges the magnesium. I wonder if this applies for food-calcium? I don't do much dairy lately but I do have some goat kefir at times....
> Also, in a consult with Dr Flechas he said to 2 caps of the ATP cofactors, even at 50mg iodine....

Here is the original source for the calcium statement:
Dr. Guy Abraham cautions that "excess calcium supplementation (2,000-
3,000 mg/day) has been the most common cause of poor response to iodine
supplementation." Vitamin Research News Vol. 22. Number 2.
The key here is EXCESS & SUPPLEMENTS which implies that food sources are OK.

ATP Cofactors is a subject with conflicting expert opinions. No two experts can agree on the exact same dosage. Dr. Buist at the [Yahoo Iodine Group](http://health.groups.yahoo.com/group/iodine/) says NONE. The heart of this issue is whether your body is absorbing iodine or not and if you have symptoms. In cases where the body is not absorbing iodine, or if you have deficiency symptoms, then the dosage of ATP Cofactors and the other co-supplement dosages need to be increased. An iodine doctor is required to determine how much to increase each supplement. For clarification you can read the Dr. Brownstein book, and see an iodine trained doctor. So I suggest you follow your own Dr. Flechas on all issues regarding iodine. You are very lucky to have him !

Also, Vitamin D3 does FAR MORE for bone problems than calcium supplements, per the Dr. Kruse fantastic [article on Vitamin D3.](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/)

So, sorry at this time there is just not a perfect answer for increasing dosages of each supplement. Perhaps after the the next Iodine Conference, we can get better resolutions. I have this covered by the statement,"Recommended minimum daily dosage for those in good health with minimal detox." Iodine Deficiency health problems & severe detox requires a trained iodine doctor to adjust dosages higher than minimum. Here is a listing of trained iodine doctors: [http://www.breastcancerchoices.org/ipractitioners.html](http://www.breastcancerchoices.org/ipractitioners.html)

Hope this helps,

### Grizz — 2013-04-01T20:39:27+0300

If you ever meet Dr. Kruse in person, tell him I love him. He is my HERO for getting me started on my iodine research. Progress just today, because of Dr. Kruse starting all of this, is that Lynn Farrow is mailing a dozen of her books on consignment to our neighborhood Amish Health Food Store, at my request. Amish women in my area are also suffering iodine deficiency symptoms. The Amish Heath Food Store plans to supply the books, the iodine & co-supplements to everyone in their community.

There was no other way for the Amish to find out about iodine. They don't have electricity, no phones and are very isolated. The only phone they have is a community phone booth ! Obviously no computers or TV. They get around by horse & buggy. His store has no electricity, skylights in the roof for lighting, and old fashioned gas refrigeration for his eggs, cheeses, etc. However, **SURPRISE** ! He does have a Solar Panel & a battery to supply power for his cash register ! They heat & cook with wood, and they do use diesel engines to power their wood working shops, some of the farm tools and old wringer washing machines. The engine drives shafts, belts & pulleys to transfer power all around. Fascinating. Oh, and they have a LOT of children that still go to a 1 room school house barefooted. The older girls are the teachers in between graduating 8th grade until they get married. They do not go to high school, and they do not use plumbing. Hand well pumps & out houses are still in use. They still live in the 18th century and they will have no trouble surviving a world wide financial crises or any end of civilization crises.

Grizz

### Grizz — 2013-04-03T08:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-82676>

**Antibiotics -vs- Iodine**

> According to several studies, obstetricians and gynecologists write 2,645,000 antibiotic prescriptions every week. Internists prescribe 1,416,000 per week. This works out to 211,172,000 prescriptions annually in the United States, just for these two specialties. Pediatricians prescribe over $500 million worth of antibiotics annually just for one condition, ear infections. Yet topical povidone iodine (PVP-I) is as effective as topical ciprofloxacin, with a superior advantage of having no in vitro drug resistance and the added benefit of reduced cost of treatment.\[2\]
> Many women find after taking antibiotics, they get vaginal yeast infections (because their normal bacterial balance has been lost). Antibiotics bring on fungal and yeast infections thus will eventually be seen as one additional cause of cancer since more and more oncologists are seeing yeast and fungal infections as an integral part of cancer and its cause. With upwards of 40 percent of all cancers thought to be involved with and caused by infections\[3\] the subject of antibiotics and the need for something safer, more effective and life serving is imperative.
> Every day, new strains of bacteria, fungi, and otherpathogenic microorganisms are becoming resistant to theantibiotics that once dispatched them with extreme prejudice
> Though it kills 90 percent of bacteria on the skin within 90 seconds its use as an antibiotic has been ignored. Iodine exhibits activity against bacteria, molds, yeasts, protozoa, and many viruses; indeed, of all antiseptic preparations suitable for direct use on humans and animals and upon tissues, only iodine is capable of killing all classes of pathogens: gram-positive and gram-negative bacteria, mycobacteria, fungi, yeasts, viruses and protozoa. Most bacteria are killed within 15 to 30 seconds of contact.
> Iodine is by far the best antibiotic,antiviral and antiseptic of all time.
> \- Dr. David Derry
> Dr. Derry says that iodine is effective “for standard pathogens such as Staphylococcus, but also iodine has the broadest range of action, fewest side effects and no development of bacterial resistance.” There is a world of difference between using an antibiotic – anti-life substance – and a antibiotic, antiviral and antifungal substance like iodine, which is life serving because it is a basic and most necessary nutritional substance.
> Iodine kills single celled organisms by combining with the amino acids tyrosine or histidine when they are exposed to the extra-cellular environment. All single cells showing tyrosine on their outer cell membranes are killed instantly by a simple chemical reaction with iodine that denatures proteins. Nature and evolution have given us an important mechanism to control pathogenic life forms and we should use it and trust it to protect us in ways that antibiotics can’t.
> [http://drsircus.com/medicine/cancer/the-end-of-antibiotics](http://drsircus.com/medicine/cancer/the-end-of-antibiotics)

Grizz

I too have had 4 different doctors tell me to cut off my breasts. (and I have even thought about it) Also they told me to get a hysterectomy as soon as I was done having children to deal with the uterine fibroids.

I have been taking iodine with the support supplements for just over a month. I dove right in and started with 1 drop. (now up to 4 drops) The pain in my breasts went away within 24 hours!!!! I literally did a little dance in the shower when I realized it. I still have some lumps but the smaller ones are gone. I also am getting a lot of relief from the fibroid pain.

Other things I have observed is my appetite is almost completely gone, my hands don't break out from the chemicals anymore, my fingernails are hard and growing for the first time in my life, I haven't even had one migraine since starting and I am happy and have so much energy. I have suffered from depression for so long that it is amazing to feel normal again.

So, again, thank you thank you!!
[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-317.html#post889724](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-317.html#post889724)

Thank you both! To me this is nothing short of a real miracle. Grizz, I have read everything in your file at least 5 times, I have sent it on to my mom and sister and I tell everyone in the salon who will listen. Thank you for putting it together. The information is life changing for me.

I know 11 is young... and I could have been spared 25 years of pain. I don't know if this is a common thing for girls with FB but I was that freak of nature kid who was as tall as the teacher at 9 and had a full-grown woman's body at 12. My breasts always hurt and always felt like bean bags. (and got worse over the years) I remember complaining about it when I was young and being told that it was normal for developing girls. People just thought I was complaining because I wasn't yet mature enough for the body I had. I didn't know it was FB untill I was 19 or 20 when I went in to get birth control for the first time. So I learned it was not normal but there was nothing anyone could do.

I never got as far as price with the doctors so I don't know what that would cost but I was told that they could do some nice reconstructive implants at the same time. (I'm sure that doubles the price) When I chose not to do it my last doctor wanted to get me on antidepressants to "help" me deal with it. I am sure doctors just don't know about iodine. I really feel that western medicine is great at cutting stitching and fixing broken bones. They fail when it comes to restoring health to people with chronic problems. They just want to cut it out or give you a pill to mask the symptoms. No one, it seems, asks the question of what is causing the problem. That is why I am so grateful for the information you have put out there.

I have two small victories to share. First, I went out yesterday to do a short run. About half way in there is a hill thats really steep that I use for my sprints. (maybe 150 yards long) I always run up but never down because of my chest and my uterus. Yesterday I flew down it with no pain at all and I was laughing so hard when I got to the bottom because I was so happy. Second, I woke up this morning and realized that I had slept on my stomach! Both of these are small things... to me, they represent the impossible.
[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-317.html#post889847](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-317.html#post889847)

@@Caroline,
There is a nationwide iodine deficiency crises. If you are not eating a lot of seaweed, & sea foods then you WILL suffer from one of the typical deficiency diseases sooner or later. Most commonly
\* Hypothyroid & related thyroid disease including goiter & nodules
\* Female organ problems, severe menstrual pain, female hormonal problems
\* Male prostate problems & male hormonal problems
\* Brain Fog, headaches
\* Cancers of all kinds especially related to the above
I knew about the nationwide deficiency crises, but didn't realize it would impact families in EVERY house in my rural neighborhood. Rural Tennessee folks just don't have easy access to seafoods or seaweed.
On the positive side, Lynne Farrow's [new book](http://www.amazon.com/The-Iodine-Crisis-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X) has made a huge difference between people being super skeptical of my messages and accepting the facts about iodine.

@@ GlenPDQ,
I cannot modify Doctors Orders. Dr. Brownstein spent YEARS of research & testing to develop his Iodine Protocol. The best information that I have obtained from the doctors is to take the ATP Cofactors for 1 year or as long as symptoms persist. Then take 1/2 of a tablet for the 1st 25mg of iodine, than 1 tablet per 50mg of iodine. Others have tried to modify the protocol by making changes where you can see these recommendations at the Curezone. As far as I am concerned, it works fine just as Dr. Brownstein prescribes. I will wait for Dr. Brownstein to make improvements to his own protocol. Everyone is certainly free to do iodine any way they want, whether eating sea foods or taking supplements.

> Krista said:
>
> Also, can someone point me to the dosage for 5% Lugol's? I saw the 1 drop per 8oz bottle regimen, but that was for the 2%, wasn't it? What should I do, just use a 16 oz bottle?

@@Krista,
It doesn't much matter - everyone is sooo different. Some can start right off with 100mg iodoral with minimal detox symptoms while others have had a VERY difficult time with just 1 drop a day. It all depends on your detox symptoms and how severe they are. I like to see everyone start small to avoid sick days from work. You might do well on 1 full drop a day to start, or you might have to cut it down by drinking 1 oz of water per day from the glass with 1 drop. Whether 2% or 5% matters little, make your judgement by detox symptoms and pulse dose/reduce dosage as required.
\* 2% Lugols Iodine = 2.5mg per drop ( Iodine 1.0 mg + Iodide 1. 5 mg )
\- - - Best for beginners just getting started in detox - Amazon Price
\* 5% Lugols Iodine = 6.25mg per drop ( Iodine 2.5 mg + Iodide 3.75 mg )
\- - - Best for users in advanced detox > 12mg a day - Amazon Price
\* Iodoral Tablet = 12.5mg per tablet ( Iodine 5 mg, Iodide 7.5 mg )
\- - - Best for users in advanced detox > 12mg a day - Amazon Price
\* Iodoral Tablet = 50 mg per tablet (Iodine 20 mg, Iodide 30 mg )
\- - - Best for users doing VERY Advanced Detox > 50mg a day - Amazon Price
\- - - or for serious disease under a Dr. care such as FBD or Cancers
\*\*Note - Some European formulations measure the opposite where USA 5%=Euro 10%
How much iodine is in a drop? Depends on which country you live in. What counts is mg per drop.
NOTE: A Potassium Free Modified Lugols Iodine is available from Dr. Clark for those who must be on a Potassium Free Diet. Uses Sodium Iodide instead of Potassium Iodide.
\- - - Cost = $36 for 180 days @ 12.5mg a day = 20 cents per day Amazon.Com
Details here: [http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions)

> Inger said:
>
> I ordered 7% Lugol's.. I thought it might last me longer? I ordered it from Amazon.co.uk. Is this way too strong to use pure on moles? Should I dilute it? Should I mix it with oil or use it pure?
> I have lots of moles too. I am going to try to paint them if they go away, they are normal moles but still.. would be fun to get them disappear!

@@Inger,
The higher concentration of iodine will just work faster. Again, be VERY careful to keep the iodine away from your normal skin. I know it is difficult, but can be done if you don't use the eyedropper. Also, you may have a European formulation which is different than the USA formulation, what counts is the mg per drop. USA of 5% = Euro 10% - Those Crazy Europeans even use Metric, and they have confused a comma for a period.

> Meghan said:
>
> This could be answered somewhere else but I'm still sorting through the 29+ pages of this post.... if you have a brita or Pur water filter is your water still floridated?.... and if your selenium level is in top range (143) 70-150ng/ml.... do you need to supplement with iodine and selenium?

Meghan,
YES ! There are only 2 ways to remove fluoride from your water. Water is typically a major source of fluorides and other toxic chemicals. Only a distiller or a reverse osmosis filter will remove it. [More details here.](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.4i2b8t4os7jd)
We use [this filter](http://www.amazon.com/Water-Distiller-Countertop-Enamel-Collection/dp/B00026F9F8) for all of our drinking water. You should see and SMELL the horrible sludge left behind in the distiller. Simply DISGUSTING that we had been drinking that horrid stuff for years. It is no wonder that Americans are such a sickly population!

Also, selenium is extremely important with the Dr. Brownstein Protocol. Yes, take the recommended selenium, or you can substitute 3-Brazil Nuts or even a can of sardines. Both are rich in selenium. [More on Selenium here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.q6cw9ojckwx1)

Best to all,
Grizz

### Grizz — 2013-04-09T12:55:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-83454>

**_Iodine Protects Against Breast Cancer_**

> The thyroid-breast cancer connection
>
> Scientists at the University of California have determined there is indeed a thyroid disease-breast cancer relationship. Both diseases are female predominant, with identifiable biological pathways, and genetic and environmental determinants. They believe that viewing thyroid disease and breast cancer together is necessary for understanding, treatment and prevention.
>
> These scientists determined that the low risk of breast cancer seen in women with optimal thyroid hormone and ample iodine results from the ability of these compounds to generate appropriate cell death (apoptosis). When cells die on cue, breast cancer is thwarted, and they suspect the reverse is also true. In women with low thyroid hormone production and iodine deficiency, the risk is increased that cancerous cells do not die but instead continue to grow and divide.
>
> **Iodine has many actions in the body**
>
> Iodine deficiency can cause changes to the thyroid gland that directly lead to poor functioning of metabolism and immunity. Iodine deficiency promotes free radical damage in the thyroid gland that puts the gland itself at risk. Iodine blocks various toxic compounds from binding to and accumulating in the thyroid gland, such as fluoride, perchlorate, and goitrogens. Environmental pollution significantly aggravates a lack of iodine.
>
> Iodine is in high concentration in the ovaries and breast tissue, acting as a buffer to the growth stimulating effects of estrogen and as a promoter of proper estrogen metabolism. Iodine assists the functioning of hormone receptors throughout the body, helping all hormones communicate more effectively. Thyroid hormone governs the rate of formation of steroid hormones, and thus governs the many functions of those hormones.
>
> Iodine is essential for proper brain development and functioning. There have been many studies showing the importance of iodine during gestation when cognitive potential is formed. A mother with adequate iodine levels will be more likely to produce a child with superior brain development.
>
> People who have difficulty losing weight even though they eat small portions of health promoting foods are probably short on iodine. Iodine deficiency can lead to dull brittle hair, balding, lack of skin tone, low energy levels, difficulty dealing with environmental temperature change, poor concentration, constipation, depression, and extreme fatigue. And there is one more manifestation of low iodine.
>
> **The World Health Organization has announced that iodine deficiency is a worldwide health problem. A considerable part of the population may actually be iodine deficient.\*\***
>
> More here [http://www.tbyil.com/Iodine\_Breast\_Cancer\_Protection\_Barbara\_Minton.htm](http://www.tbyil.com/Iodine_Breast_Cancer_Protection_Barbara_Minton.htm)

\*\* I have made it a point to discuss iodine deficiency symptoms with everyone I meet lately. **SHOCKING**, The World Health Organization has understated the severity of this problem. EVERYONE I meet either has severe deficiency symptoms or has someone in their family with severe deficiency symptoms.

We must all re-double our efforts to get the word out to every one. I have prepared this very simplified handout that you can send out to everyone on your list. I want ALL women to be aware of iodine before I die of old age. We are doing God's work by getting the word out there. Please help. Women around the world are depending on us.

Handout:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)
Iodine Museum
[http://iodinehistory.blogspot.com/](http://iodinehistory.blogspot.com/)

Grizz

Destiny,
Here are some thoughts . . . . Are you taking Vitamin C in the ascorbic acid variety? Stop taking ascorbic acid, that contributes to blood acidity which contributes to cold sores. Ester-C is much preferred. Cold sores can be contagious so no kissing & no sharing towells ! Also your cold sore could be a detox symptom if you are taking iodine. Are you taking the required iodine co-supplements of selenium, Vit C, Mag \* ATP Cofactors?

Iodine KILLS all virus, so since cold sores are caused by the Herpes Simplex Type 1 Virus, I would definitely apply Lugols or a Providone Iodine Cream to the cold sore if I had one. But it will very likely sting. But so what? Unfortunately this may not kill ALL of the virus, as it hides in the nerves.

Please let us know what works best for your cold sores.

The doctors involved in the Iodine Protocol have made these decisions for us. I agree it is all very confusing for us to decide for ourselves which supplement to take & why. To keep things simple, I just follow Doctors Orders:

> \- - - NOTE ON DOSAGES: The range of doses are intended for the amount of Detox problems people have. Small dosage are for those with minimal detox symptoms. Large dosages are for people with severe detox symptoms or who cannot absorb iodine.
> \- - Â½ tsp Natural Celtic Salt, Himalayan Pink Salt, Redmonds Salt or Hawaiin black salt
> \- - - If the sea salt is pure white, it is unacceptable (minerals have been stripped out )
> \- - 200+ mcg selenium (L-selenomethionine preferred)
> \- - 400 mg Magnesium - Glycinate preferred (calcium supplements will purge magnesium )
> \- - 2,000+ mg Vitamin C - Ester-C preferred (not absorbic acid) - do not take with liquid iodine
> \- - ATP Cofactors caplet contains both 100mg B2 & 500mg B3 non-flushing in the correct ratio. Take 1/2 caplet with iodine up to 25mg, then one caplet for each 50 mg iodine, if iodine sufficient & no symptoms after 1 yr, this supplement can be discontinued.
> Vitamin D3 Gelltabs 5,000 IU are recommended to boost the immune system to get the most out of iodine. Complete Details at [http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)
As Dr. Brownstein & his team makes changes, I update my files accordingly. The primary Team Members include Dr. Buist, Dr. Brownstein, Dr. Abraham, & Dr. Flechas, who have treated more than 4,000 patients with iodine supplementation. Author [Lynne Farrow](http://www.amazon.com/The-Iodine-Crisis-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X/ref=cm_cr_pr_product_top) also keeps her website [BreastCancerChoices](http://breastcancerchoices.org/iprotocol.html) updated with the Iodine Team's recommendations. You can also download Dr. Buists [Iodine Guide](http://steppingstonesliving.com/resources/iodine/).

I'm sure there are many other ways to do the iodine protocol, I just prefer picking one doctor to follow to help prevent brain fog. There is a wide variety of time tested recommendations to be found [at the Curezone.](http://curezone.com/forums/f.asp?f=815) Check them out. I would not know if they are better or not. Here is our best Q&A File that might help you decide for yourself:
[http://curezone.com/faq/c.asp?a=13,281,2962](http://curezone.com/faq/c.asp?a=13,281,2962)

Best to you, let us know what you decide
Grizz

endless,
**"Taking iodine for Hashi is like pouring gasoline on a fire = FALSE, DEAD WRONG"**
This is an old wives tale that just keeps going on & on & on. Iodine is the cure. However the reason for this old wives tale is that iodine by itself is bad whether Hashimotos or not. Iodine supplements absolutely require selenium. NEVER take iodine without selenium, and never take selenium without iodine.

....health sure is complicated.

I am happy to see everyone doing such a great job getting into iodine supplementation. The results can be dramatic as everyone can see in the Testimonials file that I have been keeping up:
[http://goo.gl/OCZHT](http://goo.gl/OCZHT)
The most surprising testimonials are about breast pain with the usual lumps & bumps and how quickly symptoms fade away. I've had reports of fibrocystic breast symptoms GONE in less than 1 week, where doctors had insisted that mastectomy was the ONLY solution, confirmed by cases in my own neighborhood. How many MILLIONS of women are suffering needlessly ? ? IMO, it is a TRAVESTY that iodine is censored by the drug companies. We must all re-double our efforts to get the word out to everyone.

In my experience with iodine, the most typical symptoms are bloat, skin breakeouts, rash pimples and headache. Also TSH can be expected to drop temporarily. There are many other [symptoms in the list](http://tinyurl.com/Detox-Help), but I haven't seen anyone reporting them.

I will be on vacation for most of the next 2 weeks, so will check back in when I return.
Grizz

### Grizz — 2013-04-17T01:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-84307>

CT,

Here are your simplified instruction for iodine.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

Every cell in the human body requires iodine, especially the thyroid, brain & the prostate in men.

The Adrenals are a mind numbing insanely brain blasting subject. Here are some links to help clear up the complexity.

Overlooked Connection to Iodine Deficiecy
\==========================================
There is a lack of knowledge about the critical connection between the adrenal glands and the thyroid and iodine deficiency.
When the cortisol supply is low in your system, the body is unable to utilize the thyroid hormone (T3) creating hypothyroid symptoms.
DESICCATED THYROID
If you are taking desiccated thyroid to rebuild the lack of iodine in your system, an increase in the dosage creates extremely unpleasant side effects. The rest of the GREAT article:
[http://www.iodine-resource.com/adrenal-fatigue.html](http://www.iodine-resource.com/adrenal-fatigue.html)

Adrenal Information - 1
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/adrenal-info/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/adrenal-info/)

How to treat Adrenals
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/adrenal-info/how-to-treat/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/adrenal-info/how-to-treat/)

About Adrenal Fatigue
[http://www.adrenalfatiguefocus.org/using-licorice-root.html](http://www.adrenalfatiguefocus.org/using-licorice-root.html)

Finally the index to thyroid & Adrenals
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/)

ust something to consider...

SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ADRENAL FATIGUE
[http://www.womentowomen.com/adrenalfatigue/symptoms.asp](http://www.womentowomen.com/adrenalfatigue/symptoms.asp)

Stresses like a demanding job, raising a family, relationship issues, lack of sleep, financial pressures, improper nutrition, dieting, and unresolved emotional distress cause the adrenal glands to produce cortisol. However, when we ask our adrenal glands to chronically sustain high cortisol levels, they eventually become fatigued. The resulting adrenal dysfunction not only affects cortisol production, but also impairs the adrenals’ ability to produce and balance hormones like DHEA, estrogen, progesterone, and testosterone.

SYMPTOMS OF ADRENAL FATIGUE
The following symptoms are your body’s way of telling you that it is not receiving the support it needs to maintain healthy adrenal function.
\* Fatigue
\* Feeling tired despite sufficient hours of sleep
\* Insomnia
\* Weight gain
\* Depression
\* HAIR LOSS
\* Acne
\* Reliance on stimulants like caffeine
\* Cravings for carbohydrates or sugars
\* Poor immune function
\* Intolerance to cold

RELATED CONDITIONS
Adrenal fatigue is a likely factor in several medical conditions such as the following:
\* Fibromyalgia
\* Hypothyroidism
\* Chronic fatigue syndrome
\* Arthritis
\* Premature menopause

Every challenge to the mind and body creates a demand on the adrenal glands. And the list of challenges is endless: lack of sleep, a demanding boss, the threat of losing your job, financial pressures, personality conflicts, yo-yo dieting, relationship turmoil, death or illness of a loved one, skipping meals, reliance on stimulants like CAFFEINE and carbs, digestive problems, over-exercise, illness or infection, unresolved emotional issues from our past or present and more. The result is adrenal glands that are constantly on high alert.

In our experience, women with mild to moderate cases of adrenal fatigue can see significant improvement through these steps:
\* Dietary changes to enrich your nutrition and reduce carbohydrates and stimulants. We also recommend the addition of high-quality nutritional supplements, including essential fatty acids from fish oil.
\* Stress reduction, including moderate exercise and taking more time for yourself. It’s helpful to make a list of your stressors, especially those that are constant.
\* Get more rest. Your body needs time to heal.

Hope this helps,
Grizz

### AndrÃ©s — 2013-04-26T11:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-84862>

Benign prostate hyperplasia update.

After a year taking iodine/iodide, my father's prostate seems (I don't know the error margin of the echography estimation) to have shrank from 170g to 164g, at the same time that the PSA has decreased (they didn't give us the numbers though). We are not following exactly Dr. Brownstein's protocol and he has been increasing doses something like 50% every two weeks most of the times. Nowadays he is at something like 7.2mg/day (4 Iosol drops). He will be increasing toward 21.5mg/day (1 Iodoral tablet and 5 Iosol drops).

He was taking 5000IU/day of vitamin D3 for a year till last fall. His 25-OH-vitamin-D level was 61ng/ml then. He is taking 9x5000UI/week since then. We are going to measure his level again next week.

He takes also tocotrienols, tocopherols, vitamin C, vitamin B complex, selenium...

He is still taking the standard medication though: combined alpha-blocker and 5-alpha-reductase inhibitor. He was already taking it at the same dose for several years while his prostate kept growing till they advice us to operate on him last year, hence he hearing my advice and starting with iodine. We will decide to lower or eliminate it depending on next year results and medical collaboration (I would like to check size in half a year or less if eliminating). We haven't talked about iodine with his urologist yet.

@ Raiken,
The dose of iodine required for good health is extremely dependent on how much we eat & drink of the Iodine Busters
\* Chlorine
\* Fluoride
\* Bromine

If we could eliminate all of these poisons then we could get by with only 6 to 12mg of iodine daily. Unfortunately, we live in a sea of these poisons, and Dr. Brownstein recommends 50mg daily to overcome these goitrogens:
[http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine update.htm#section18](http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine%20update.htm#section18)

We have taken extreme measures to remove these toxins from our food & water including distilling our water, and we are now taking just 12mg a day (1-iodoral) with required supplements.

@ Andres
I look forward to your update on your dad's BPH. I just don't have enough [testimonials](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1KQ8tCLrOoQQJ8h_gSRM1aZ1Ya-YqaN4B3i8NMXOm8TY/edit#) on that subject. I would like to add his BPH cure to the testimonials section.

@Wurst23,
Yes, liquid iodine can react with Vitamin C where iodoral tablets are not a problem. So take your Vitamin C in the evening & your iodine in the morning. Also, I suspect that your 12% iodine is a euro version which is equivalent to the USA 5% Lugols. Euro measure iodine ratios differently than we do. What counts is the mg of iodine per drop.

@nuttmegs17,

> What is the optimol dose for Hashi people?

Here are the references for your Hashimotos. As you can see, [selenium is essentia](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.8eltjvzb6ets)l:

This thread is becoming more professional with every message! While on vacation I managed to educate some people in our travel group to help us spread the word on iodine. One was the manager of a health care facility.

**The BOING Effect**

> In Lynne Farrow's new book, "The Iodine Crisis..." she describes the "boing effect". I remember that all to clearly. I started out with supplementation very slowly, basically because I did not have anyone to follow, we did not have the huge database of people that have jumped right in at 50mg or so... my point is, that I felt "the boing effect" with one drop of lugol's. I drank that lugol's sitting right here, where I am sitting now, in front of my computer. It was magic
>
> Transposed directly from the book:
>
> [www.amazon.com/Iodine-Crisis-What-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X/ref=sr\_1\_1](http://www.amazon.com/Iodine-Crisis-What-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X/ref=sr_1_1)
>
>
>
> "Within two hours of taking the 50mg of iodoral, I got a sensation some of us call "the boing". My brain cleared as if the dusty unused rooms of my brain suddenly got oxygen. My thoughts grew sharper edges. I felt a burst of energy, thought faster and felt smarter even doing everyday math. I remember getting impatient with a store clerk because he couldn't compute three times thirty two in his mind. After several tries, he pulled out a calculator. Before iodine I wouldn't have even noticed."
>
> :::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
>
> My experience with the boing effect is in the archives. The boing not only affected my mind, but my body as well. That increased clarity and energy persisted and it's been the norm for 6 years now:
>
> curezone.com/forums/am.asp

[http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=2061737#i](http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=2061737#i)

IMO, that "Boing Effect" is the brain fog lifting that many people who are severely iodine deficient discuss. I never noticed a "boing effect" when starting iodine last year. Things I did notice were my frequent urination problems faded away and my occasional erectile dysfunction began to fade away. I also noticed improved sleep and energy. Everyone is different. I may not have noticed this boing effect because I started iodine with such a very low dose of 1/2 mg and I very slowly titrated up to 50 mg over a 5 month or so period.

> I'll never forget either one:
>
> 1) I had a heart arrythmia for YEARS and YEARS; it almost killed me in late 1997. A year or two later I was diagnosed with what they called "Long QT Syndrome". It means that the hesitation between heartbeats can be dramatically too long - life threatening if TOO prolonged.
>
> On 02/Feb/2007 I had an especially-scary episode of it. (This was ONE week after learning about Iodine from Trapper on another health forum and I was Led to purchase "detoxified iodine" from iodinesource.com and had already received it and started taking it in very small doses.)
>
> Anyhoo, I had a very slow heartrate on 2/2/07, was cold to the core, and the heart arrythmia was dramatic. Still too scared to take more orally, I decided to drop 15 drops of iodine on my belly skin surrounding my naval. In less than 15 minutes I had the "boing" effect and my heartrate went from the 50's to the 80's and even higher. I believe what I felt was the iodine PUSHING the bromides out of my heart and taking it's rightful place in the valve! By bedtime my heart was back to its normal rate of the low-to-mid 70's if memory serves correctly.
>
> I STILL have NOT had any arrythmia since that scary (but WONDERFUL) day!!
>
> 2) I held a cat (my biggest alltime allergen) for a neighbor while she went scouting for her lost dog. I realized I wasn't iodinated enough when I got allergy symptoms: itchy ears, itchy nose, tightness of lungs, shortness of breath.
>
> I took ten drops of iodine orally with glass of water. Again, in less than TEN minutes this time) I had a "boing" effect, and literally felt ALL my allergy symptoms drain away - gone - finito - POOF!

> Nobody is EVER gonna take MY iodine away from ME!

>
> After reading everything and thinking about it I decided to give iodine a try. I have had fiber cystic breasts since I was about 11 years old and I can't even tell you how uncomfortable exercising, hugging and even bras have been my whole life. I too have had 4 different doctors tell me to cut off my breasts. (and I have even thought about it) Also they told me to get a hysterectomy as soon as I was done having children to deal with the uterine fibroids.
>
> I have been taking iodine with the support supplements for just over a month. I dove right in and started with 1 drop. (now up to 4 drops) The pain in my breasts went away within 24 hours!!!! I literally did a little dance in the shower when I realized it. I still have some lumps but the smaller ones are gone. I also am getting a lot of relief from the fibroid pain.
>
> Other things I have observed is my appetite is almost completely gone, my hands don't break out from the chemicals anymore, my fingernails are hard and growing for the first time in my life, I haven't even had one migraine since starting and I am happy and have so much energy. I have suffered from depression for so long that it is amazing to feel normal again.
> So, again, thank you thank you!!

> Zophie said:
>
> Thank you both! To me this is nothing short of a real miracle. Grizz, I have read everything in your file at least 5 times, I have sent it on to my mom and sister and I tell everyone in the salon who will listen. Thank you for putting it together. The information is life changing for me.
>
> I know 11 is young... and I could have been spared 25 years of pain. I don't know if this is a common thing for girls with FB but I was that freak of nature kid who was as tall as the teacher at 9 and had a full-grown woman's body at 12. My breasts always hurt and always felt like bean bags. (and got worse over the years) I remember complaining about it when I was young and being told that it was normal for developing girls. People just thought I was complaining because I wasn't yet mature enough for the body I had. I didn't know it was FB untill I was 19 or 20 when I went in to get birth control for the first time. So I learned it was not normal but there was nothing anyone could do.
>
> I never got as far as price with the doctors so I don't know what that would cost but I was told that they could do some nice reconstructive implants at the same time. (I'm sure that doubles the price) When I chose not to do it my last doctor wanted to get me on antidepressants to "help" me deal with it. I am sure doctors just don't know about iodine. I really feel that western medicine is great at cutting stitching and fixing broken bones. They fail when it comes to restoring health to people with chronic problems. They just want to cut it out or give you a pill to mask the symptoms. No one, it seems, asks the question of what is causing the problem. That is why I am so grateful for the information you have put out there.
>
> I have two small victories to share. First, I went out yesterday to do a short run. About half way in there is a hill thats really steep that I use for my sprints. (maybe 150 yards long) I always run up but never down because of my chest and my uterus. Yesterday I flew down it with no pain at all and I was laughing so hard when I got to the bottom because I was so happy. Second, I woke up this morning and realized that I had slept on my stomach! Both of these are small things... to me, they represent the impossible.

Jasmin,

It is not the iodine that causes detox. It is the toxins coming out of your cells being displaced by iodine that cause detox. Every cell in our body has iodine receptors that get filled with bromide, fluoride or chlorine. When iodine kicks these poisons out of our cells, we can suffer detox symptoms.

I know that bloat is on the list of detox symptoms, and is very common. However I have not yet heard of bloat in the ankles. Just to be on the safe side, go ahead and do the pulse dosing - I suggest cutting back to 1 dose per week then slowly come back to where you are now after the ankles get back to normal. All detox symptoms are temporary until the toxins have passed through the body.

Grizz
