# Thread: Stock tank - tips  tricks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 86
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-04-17T19:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-15920>

I'm thinking of setting up a stock tank outdoors to do CT. The biggest reason is so I can re-use the water from day-to-day instead of filling & draining the bathtub every day.

What are you all doing to keep the water clean, if anything?

How often do you have to empty the tank? Do you scrub it, clean it, etc. or just let it air dry?

How do you drain it? Sump pump, gravity drain, ...

What do you use to cover it?

What size tank are you using?

Do you wish you'd done something different with your set-up?

Any other tips?

### Qodesh — 2012-04-17T20:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-15934>

I'm picking up my 110 gal stock tank early next week.

I'll be keeping it indoors for now, so the air temp will be cooler (I'm in S. Florida)and thus hopefully not a bacteria breeding ground.

I plan to be draining it with a hose to the drain, or siphon, approx every 6 dips (every other week)

I plan on a wipe down with peroxide.

When I'm able to move it outside (I live in an apartment now), I plan on covering it with a breathable material with an elastic edging to keep down the dust and avoid closed space humidity.

I ultimately would like to get a chiller. I think 1/4 HP will do it for 110 gal tank. I'm still researching.

In S. Florida, I may need to have some heat reflective wrap on the outside to reflect the sun. I don't plan on sinking it in the ground because then I won't be able to reach the drain.

**I'm also very interested in how people are keeping the tank bacteria free non-chemically, between dips in the summer heat .**

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-04-17T20:27:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-15938>

I have been working on this for a couple of weeks now.... See my post in the [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?524-How-was-your-CT-today](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?524-How-was-your-CT-today)

run down... I have a 100 gallon tub, I am 5'8 173 so if you are smaller than that a 100 or less gallon tub will work. I could have got a 70 gallon and fit but it would have been tight. I plumbed in a gravity drain (tub came with a plug) it gets most of the water out and then I just flip out the rest. I keep it under a 10x10 sun shade and cover it with a tarp when not in use. I hung a mosquito net around it for bug free CTing. I planned on cleaning it once a week but after researching a GREAT tip by shilohman, I am going to get a filter pump and plumb it in. ( approx 35 dollars on ebay) so I will not need to change it so often. being in FL it is a temp issue being outside.

Yesterday it took 40 pounds of ice to drop the temp from approx 78 degrees to approx 62. And it is not even summer here yet.  I just need to insulate it some how and it still be water resistant. So far I have spent approx 100 dollars and will probably spend another 200 at most.

Still is a lot cheaper then a real cold tub made by coldtub.com I got a quote for a two person tub (smallest) used by universities $19,900. yes that is thousand.....

My advice so far is not to over buy the tub. The smaller the better. You will use less water and ice to lower the temp. Also remember your body weight will displace water and the ice ice will add water. I figure at my weight I am displacing 15 gallons and another 5 gallons for 40 pounds of ice. I estimate I only use 70 gallons of water.

good thread Lyndra

### quelsen — 2012-04-17T20:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-15941>

If i were going to set up a cold station i would set it up like a 150 gallon saltwater tank including filtration. then i would rinse off in my shower before each use and cover the tank when it was not in use.

so

150gal Rubbermaid tank

1/4 chiller

salt water filtration system

change water once a month or so.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-04-17T20:43:55+0300

The pre-rinse is a good suggestion. I didn't do it the first couple of times I went in, I started to the last couple of times and noticed a big difference in the water condition when done.

I looked into one of those chillers and the run 450 or more...plus I read they will only lower the temp down to 10 degrees lower than ambient temp. The guy at the fish store recommended a 1/2 hp chiller to get it colder but they were even more expensive.

### PaleoMom — 2012-04-17T23:39:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-15988>

I bought a 150 gallon Rubbermaid stock tank that has a drain in the side near the bottom. It is bigger than needed, but is so that DH and I can do it together. It has only been a few days, but I think that even if I have to dump it 2x a week it is less water than the tub. I'm not filling it to the brim. I put in like 50 gallons then it rained (and it's in a place that get roof run off- metal roof) and put about 60 more gallons in it. Water is still quite cold, 47 last night.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-04-21T23:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-17549>

on the advice of shilohman (thank you) I hooked up a pool pump filter on my 100 gallon tank today. Its like having a cold tub jacuzzi...LOL

I completely over bought what is really needed. I got a [1000 gph filter pump](http://http://www.amazon.com/Intex-56637EG-1000-Gallon-Filter-120-Volt/dp/B005QIYL7E/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1335041147&sr=8-1) but for the price and its ability to circulate the water, what the hell...I will post pics when the rain stops here.

also stopped by the pool store and picked up some water conditioner, so I should be able to go a while without changing he water.

### Birdy — 2012-04-22T00:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-17552>

We are still deciding what kind of tank to use. Currently I have a rubbermaid tub (not the fancy stock tank type) and it is difficult to use because the sides are too flimsy for me to get out easily. Also, dh can't fit in it very well. Currently, I have it in a shady area, and covered with an old window when not in use. That is pretty suboptimal since it stays warm. I am just starting with the cold soaks but will be covering it with something different soon. We are still in disagreement about the type of container, the necessity of a drain hole, and the chlorine issue. I used mine for only two days before emptying it out by hand to water the plants. Whatever system we end up with will probably be filled shallowly and then reusable water-bottle ice packs added, so the actual water in the tub will be low. Currently, I soak, and then dry off naturally while I am watering the plants.

### Birdy — 2012-04-22T00:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-17553>

I had the good fortune of running into an industrial air-conditioning guy on my last train trip. Interesting discussions! He says it is always cheaper to heat water than cool it, and suggested that getting an ice machine would be much cheaper than some automatic hot-tub-type system. He also said I could go for a week before changing the water with a simple filter and no chlorine.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-04-22T20:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-17809>

> **Cowboy;17865 wrote:** Did you drill into the tank to rund the filter hoses, or just drop the intake and return hoses into the tank?

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/17880=125-cold tub4.jpg>)

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/17880=126-cold tub3.jpg>)

The tank came with a pre drilled 1 1/4 in hole in the side near the bottom so I plumbed that to the intake. I wanted to get it up and running so I did not plumb the return yet and I may not. I took 1 1/4 in sink pipe, using a J bend and I just hook it over the edge. It works quite nicely. It also gives me the option to just pull it off and throw it in the grass and allow the tank to drain out for cleaning.

My last obstacle is insulating the tank. I would like to be able to keep the temp of the water at 70 degrees or lower when not in use so I do not need so much ice to cool it down.

Right now when the tank is 3/4 full it hovers around 73-74 degrees and it takes 40 pounds of ice to drop it into the low 60's (depending on ambient temp)

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-04-24T12:57:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-18314>

> **Birdy;18297 wrote: Great CF~! How long do you run the pump each time?
>
>
> so far I run the pump for 45 min - hour every other day when I am in it, I add a phosphate water conditioner to handle any algae.

Monte, is your tank inside or outside? What hp is your chiller? Is the chiller keeping the water temp in range? Do you continuously run the chiller and if not how long does it take to chill the water? How much has your electric bill gone up?

### Dali Dula — 2012-06-11T02:36:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-30464>

I have an old claw foot tub out behind the shed that I fill from my well 51f at night. If the temp drops at night that's what temp my water is in the am. Nights are now getting warmer so I drain and refill the tub from the well and throw in as many cubes as my fridge has made since last session. Today I got it down to 50. Not bad. Small ice works better. Like so many things in physics it's all about surface area.I looked into all the mechanical possibilities; ice machines, chillers. My latest thought is why not just buy a large chest freezer, fill it with water and either time it till it's good temp or hook up thermostat to on/off. Chest freezers have molded interiors, they are already insulated and they have a tight fitting lid. I don't know if they have drains or not. I'll check it out. Get back to you.

### RayB — 2012-06-22T00:53:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-32831>

Hi All,

I just got finished a setup that so far seems to be awesome. It's a rubbermaid 150 gallon stock tank (I wanted I big enough for 2 people). I built a frame and a floor and a deck and dropped it in. The frame is insulated with R-30 batts including the bottom. I made a chiller by cannibalizing a small dehumidifier. They can be found on craigslist for about 100 bucks. My particular unit is a 25 pint Fridgidaire. I removed most of the housing and the evaporator coil. I carefully bent the coil off to the side so that It would fit into a five gallon bucket. The bucket will be my heat exchanger. Inside of the bucket I coiled about 20-25' of 1/2" thin wall irrigation tubing and on each end of the tubing I put a barbed elbow fitting and drilled a 5/8" hole in the side of the bucket about 4 inches down from the top and another hole near the bottom. These fitting were fixed in place with PVC compatible epoxy putty. The bucket was then insulated with about 3/4 to 1" of insulation ( I could use more...'cuz it still sweats a little). A 350 GPH submersible pump was dropped into the stock tank and plumbed to the heat exchanger with the same irrigation tubing. In at the bottom fitting and back to the ST from the top fitting. The lines were insulated with neoprene pipe insulation. The evaporator coil was put back into the heat exchanger bucket and the bucket was filled with a mixture of propylene glycol (antifreeze) and water. I dropped a very small submersible pump into the bucket with the coils The pump is for circulating the antifreeze solution and it put out about 40 Gph.

The whole setup is outside on the north side of a building and under a tin roof shed. I am in a very hot and humid place, just outside of New Orleans. After running for about 12 hours the water in the stock tank was 50 degrees! I seems like I need to run the chiller about 6-8 hours a day to maintain 55 degrees. It's all about the insulation. The main pump draws about 50 watts. The little pump about 2.5 watts. And the chiller about 450 watts. @ $.12/KWH, it costs me about $.06/hour to run this. I keep the water chilled all the time and four people in my family are now using it. For safety reasons we always turn it off before using it. Optimal CT! Yeah!

### Cowboy — 2012-06-26T07:49:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-33614>

There are two brands of stock tanks that I could find in my area. Rubbermaid and another brand whose name escapes me now. both have a threaded plug that will unscrew. Unscrewing is counterclockwise. You will need either a "crescent wrench" or a set of channel locks or a large set of pliers. a wine cork will not work. You could also unscrew the plug, bring it to a plumbing shop and tell them that you want to put in a ball valve into the threaded hole, with the plug in hand they will know the proper size and be able to set you up with the pieces to screw into the threads that will allow you to add a valve to let the water out. Cost of the parts should be under $15, including a small roll of teflon tape to wrap the threads with to prevent leaking. Who ever is working at the plumbing shop will most likely help you and put the teflon tape on the threaded pieces. Hope this make sense.

### RayB — 2013-04-13T00:33:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-83939>

An update on my refrigerated stock tank project. This update is the 3rd generation of this project. It is completely self-contained and only requires an electrical connection.

Specs: 100 gallon rubbermaid stock tank undermounted beneath a fiberglass top.

Insulated with 6" of closed cell (2# density) foam , approximately an R Value of 36

Refrigerated with a 1/4 hp water/aquarium chiller

Heated with a 350 watt inline heater

Dual mode (chill/heat) thermostat set to a 1 degree differential

Pond filter with integral 700 gph pump and a 9 watt UV sterilizer

Automatic cabinet ventilator ... comes on with the chiller to remove excess heat

Approximately 70 gallons of water with 20#'s of magnesium sulfate (epsom) and 10#'s of magnesium chloride (dead sea salt) and a couple of tablespoons of potassium iodide (excellent germicide)

Custom paintjob

It’s been working excellently since the early fall. So convenient, no need to haul ice and always at the set temperature. Inexpensive to operate…so far throughout the winter and cooler months at the northern Gulf of Mexico is has cost an average of $3 per month to operate. Our electric rate is $.12/KWH and we keep the thermostat set at 45 degrees.
With the warm weather lately, early indications with my best guess is that we can expect the operating cost to run as high as $12/ mo. in the summer. But, it all depends on how many use the tub and what your preferred temperature is. Most efficiency is gained by running the tub at night when the ambient temp is low.

The heater is very seldom used. It is only used for prolonged temps below 45 or during hard freezes to prevent the plumbing from freezing up. The heater is nowhere near powerful enough to used the tub as a hot tub.

The water has never been changed in the last six months. It smells as fresh and it looks as clear as the day I first filled it up. I do brush the inside of the tank every week or two.

I use a foam lid to insulate the top when the tub is not in use.

### RayB — 2013-04-14T16:33:06+0300

Even though it is getting warmer in Wyoming, my tank was 47 degrees inside my garage, so I hopeI have a month or so before I worry about moving outside.

Hey Cowboy... I built a CT previously using batts of fiberglass insulation and down south with the high humidity the tank was always sweating and the insulation and wood framing would stay soggy. Although, batts are way less costly it may work in Wyoming. The cabinet has a partition between the equipment and the insulated tank side. Try to keep as much of the plumbing on the insulated side. This helps a lot in efficiency... so the plumbing doesn't gain too much heat.

### Cowboy — 2013-04-15T05:50:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-84125>

Even in Wyoming we would get some sweating and soggy insulation, so foam has to be the way to go. I messed with ice last summer and it was a pain trying to keep it cold enough. This winter with the tank in the garage the lowest I got was 39 or so and the highest I got was 60 degrees when it was 70 outside.

>
> Automatic cabinet ventilator ... comes on with the chiller to remove excess heat

>

### Q. C. — 2016-02-26T22:43:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-189838>

I like to just use the bath tub. I have a chest freezer and put frozen milk jugs into the bath. When I am done I do NOT drain the water. I just leave it for three or four days. I can add a bit more ice as needed but it stays pretty cold. I have a window in the bathroom that I keep closed. Reducing the sunlight into the bathroom slows down the chance of algae growth. I only drain the tub when someone else needs it or it starts to get a slight feel of slime or too dirty. Doing this makes it so that I am more inclined to use the tub several times a day or use it for short periods of time when I am short on time. I like to cool off before work, late at night, early in the morning, before and after exercise etc. I keep a washcloth in the tub to rinse cold water over my head and neck. Having it always ready makes this possible. Leaving the water in the tub keeps it always ready, saves money and makes it very available and convenient.
Q.C.

### Debntx — 2017-02-23T00:50:00+0200

Late... but what about one of those inflatable bath tubs on Amazon - happy life is the one I purchased- just have only used it for warm soaking so far but I think might work well for CT

### jwhb77 — 2017-03-12T19:39:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/post-210201>

This may be late-coming, but I figured is chip in here... I know inflatable options are often cheaper, but they are almost always a bad choice for our purposes... even the thicker-gauge plastics can be punctured easily, the seams on these things (even the nicer ones) always seem to develop air leaks before much use, and the cold water temps used for CT cause fully-inflated vessels to droop & sag (and often leak your water).

If skip them for a solid structure of some kind... the stock tubs aren't much more expensive than the higher-quality inflatables, will be less headache, & will outlast them many times over. Waiting if you have to is better than wasting your money IMHO.

### Debntx — 2017-03-12T20:34:35+0200

If your at a high latitude where it gets cold during the winter you could take advantage of the fournier effect by cooling one half in the freezing air and the other half in the hot water.

# Thread: How much Ice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-04-17T19:37:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-15921>

So I know you are supposed to reduce the temp to 50-55 degrees from whatever the tap water is with either bath or sink...well I put all the ice I had in the freezer in the sink and it wet to 32 degrees. I went ahead and used this water and it didn't take long to get to the 50 degree point. I was wondering how much it matters what the water is at as long as I don't let the skin temp go below 50 as well as how exactly I figure out how much ice to put to get to 50-55.

I know the amount can vary based on the starting temps but could anyone give me an idea about how many cubes they use to get to 50-55? I would like to use as little as possible because even though I don't buy the cubes I use tap water cubes made from ice trays it saved time to use as little as possible. It would help if someone could post their starting tap water temp and say how much it took them to make the water 50-55 degrees in a sink and how big the sink is and the same with the bath.

I should be able to estimate how much I need to use with other peoples numbers. I'll adjust for the difference in the size of my sink/bath and temp. Won't be terribly accurate as I don't know how much a each additional block reduces temp....anyway lots of variables but any information should be able to help. I really want to start trying this, but don't want to start with 32 degree water.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-17T20:15:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-15936>

Not sure how much this will help but I use five small blue ice packs, one ice cube sheet (approx 100 cubes), all the ice my ice maker makes in a day, and 60 degree tap water to get my water to 52-55. I also use one ice sheet below me that I lie on and one on top of my chest. My bathtub is filled to the bottom of the spill-over drain about 8" high. It's as high as I can get it so that is why I use the ice sheets to get the top of me as well as the upper shoulders and back.

### quelsen — 2012-04-17T20:58:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-15948>

Raiken3712 this is a math problem.

so.....

lets say you have a 100 gallon tub at 60F..

That would be 850 pounds of weight

British Thermal Unit is a unit of heat energy. One BTUis the amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of one pound of water 1Â°F

so to drop the 850 pounds of water at 60 degrees to 50 degrees would be a drop of 10 degrees for 850 pounds so you need -8500 BTU's

a pound of ice will "soak up" or remove 144 BTU's from it's surroundings ([http://www.wisedan.com/ice](http://http://www.wisedan.com/ice))

So to drop the water 10F you would need 7 pounds of Ice.

or

(8500\[BTU's in 100 gallons of water to be removed\] / 144 \[BTU's in a pound of Ice\] )/ 8.5\[number of gallons per pound of ice\] = 6.9444

to drop 20 degrees for that same 100 gallons it would be - (17000/144)/8.5 = 13.888

### Ray — 2012-04-17T23:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-15985>

8500 BTU / 144 BTU / pound = 59 pounds of ice

17000BTU / 144 BTU / pound = 118 pounds of ice

### nonchalant — 2012-04-18T01:29:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-16035>

Hi Raiken, I live near you. I draw my bath, and add a two-liter bottle of ice, plus about 15 half liter water bottles of ice. I let that sit for about 10 minutes, and check the temperature. If it's down to 60F, then a 20 pound bag of ice might do the trick. If not, then I have full ice cube trays to finish the job. If the water is down to 50F, I remove all the water bottles which are now about half frozen. I leave some in if the temp is only 55F.

If you just take it in steps, you can get it to 50 or 55F precisely. Be sure to stir the water around.

If you are just using ice cube trays... I have 14 trays, and that is not enough to get the bath temp low enough, now. It was fine earlier in the year.

For dunking in the sink, I used 4 trays of ice. But that was also earlier this year.

We've been having nice weather lately. It's going to be a struggle when normal temps return. I'm probably going to have to just let the water temp edge higher. And draw the bath hours ahead of time, to let it cool down to room temp. Sigh.

My cold tap water is 72-77 and have a deep tub. 60 pounds of ice will lower it to about 48.

### hellojtm — 2012-04-19T01:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-16378>

Once the ice soaks up the 144BTU then there is still the differential between the recently melted water and the always liquid water. That normalization will also occur and reduce the temp further. I.e. if you drop 59lbs of ice in you'll lower the water temperature to 50f until you have 100lbs of 50f water and 59lbs of 32f water. Then they would mix and end up around mid40s I would assume...

### jbond56 — 2012-04-20T03:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-16831>

All ice is not created equal. Block Ice has very little air bubles in it. The bags of ice from the gas station has lots of air bubbles in it. You have probably noticed this when cooling a beverage. The bagged ice will make it taste more watery. So the math is probably correct for block ice. I think my jacuzzi tub is also closer to 200 gallons. Hard to tell its a weird shape and i have a cover over the drain so its overfilled. I run the jets so the temp is pretty even.

### Ray — 2012-04-23T00:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-17899>

8500 BTU heat to be removed / 162 BTU/lb of ice = 52.5 lb of ice

There is no need to convert to gallons of ice or to divide by 2

### quelsen — 2012-04-23T20:15:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-18115>

### Ray — 2012-04-23T20:23:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-18118>

Like continuum fitness says, once the ice has absorbed its 144 btu per pound in the process of melting, it is sitting at 32\*F. It can still mix with 50\*F water and "soak up" more heat. How much more? If you assume there is a large enough supply of 50\*+ water the now melted ice will go from 32 to 50 (or 18 more BTU). It is not exact, but close enough.

### quelsen — 2012-04-23T20:32:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-ice.947/post-18124>

Term

How many BTU's are requires to raise the temperature of 500 GALLONS of water from 70Â°F to 160Â°F?

Definition

Convert GALLONS to pounds by multiplying 500 by 8.33 (which equals 4165), then multiply the BTU's from 70Â°F to 160Â°F (which is 90 BTU's) by 4165 and the total BTU's is 374,850 BTU's.

[http://www.flashcardmachine.com/physics-thermodynamics.html](http://http://www.flashcardmachine.com/physics-thermodynamics.html)

### Ray — 2012-04-23T21:02:52+0300

# Thread: Frozen Planet
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-planet.955/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-04-18T01:17:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-planet.955/post-16030>

Is anybody else watching this series on Discovery? We Tivo'ed a few and just started watching the last couple of evenings (minus the yellow glasses - I had to see the northern lights in full spectrum). I was floored by how well adapted the Arctic-dwelling nomads are, living in bitter cold conditions, harnessing reindeer to haul their portable shacks as they move every couple of weeks so the reindeer have new places to graze (through the snow and ice). And the Inuit - scaling cliffs to steal eggs, going under the ice and harvesting mussels with bare hands, hunting walrus and fully using the whole animals. The photography is stunning. It's perfect timing to be watching now while I'm trying to get cold adapted.

If you haven't seen it yet, you can catch up with the FP marathon on Sunday.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-18T01:42:08+0300

Stunningly beautiful.

# Thread: Near death experience while on Paleo/Leptin Reset
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ineedanLR — 2012-04-18T02:05:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16057>

**_Less than 24 hours ago, I felt and looked like I was about to die. I gave my wife instructions on disposal of my body and prepared for the worst.

Today, I feel better, not good....but better. This is the story of the factors that allowed me to recover........

The latest chapter in my story began 1 month ago, when I went to the ER, they found an out of whack thyroid hormone (my tsh was 22). They found 2 different kidney type infections (I only have 1 marginally performing kidney).

My blood sugar was 500 (I'm diabetic, but haven't checked my blood sugar for years). My weight was 466, a combination of the weight and my arthritis in my legs really limits the amount of exercise I was getting. My doctor started me on insulin, although he knew almost nothing about diabetes and I've been ramping up insulin amounts slowly, but without a real/proper medical supervision (I was never insulin dependent in the past, so the insulin injections were new to me.

I had felt like crap for years.

When I got home from the ER, I realised I needed to lose weight and bring my blood sugar down, quickly!!!!!

I immediately reduced my carb intake, dropped sugar completely and ate mostly veggies. During this time, I started research diet/health plans and found Paleo, this forum, Dr. Kruse and the gaps diet.

I hadn't eaten right for years, so I changed to Paleo/Leptin Reset as it was clear I needed the reset protocol. I went full tilt Paleo/Leptin Reset about 2 weeks ago, after taking some time to check out the science of the plan (virtually 2 weeks of complete 24/7 internet based research from many independent sources).

After starating Leptin LR/Paleo, I felt even worse than before the ER trip!!! My sleep instantly deteriorated, my stomach felt like someone kicked me, it churned causing me enormous discomfort. My head felt like it was going to explode. The gas was world class, I couldn't stop passing gas. I was sick round the clock, even waking up in the night due to the stomach discomfort. Last evening my condition took a turn for the worse, my discomfort was the worst it had been and I suddenly turned white as a ghost. My family was very worried. I knew my blood sugar was not the issue, I had been checking it constantly and it was 146 last evening.

I had tried dropping all kinds of high fiber veggies, thinking they might make gas. I dropped a bunch of foods, hoping for relief, but nothing made any difference.

So, last night **_as I was preparing to die, **I stopped the insulin and took a 2 ounce shot of orange juice**_ out of desperation**. Within 2 hours, I felt much better. I slept better and woke up with only a minor headache and some lingering gas and much milder stomach cramps. The amount of carbs I took in the orange juice was only 6 carbs, so it just shouldn't have made much difference. As the day went on today, I felt even better._**

Based on reading Robb Wolf's The Paleo Solution, I believe I was suffering from withdrawal symptoms as a result of dumping the sugar in my diet. See notes 1 and 2 below for further info on the addictive power of sugar. It's really scary that sugar withdrawal can be so debilitating, but the brain is powerful and I don't think my brain wanted to have it's sugar connection severed. It's also not clear to me whether the sugar withdrawal issues were imaginary or whether the symptoms were real physiological type issues.

I'd appreciate having opinions from other regarding whether I might have suffered sugar withdrawal issues as well as any advice on how to break the sugar addiction.

TY

Art

**Note 1:

Rob Wolf's, The Paleo Solution has a short blurb about the addictiveness of sugar, and says it's harder to break than an opiate addiction. He indicates the same brain receptors are responsible for both opiate responses and sugar responses. See page 97.

Note 2:**

In the video by Doctor Robert H. Lustig, MD, UCSF Professor of Pediatrics in the Division of Endocrinology....the video shows the real time brain scan of an individual who drinks a small sip of soda while they are inside the MRI scanner. The scan shows instant activity in the same brain areas as an opiate addict would have. Video is at [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dBnniua6-oM\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;dBnniua6-oM\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dBnniua6-oM\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;dBnniua6-oM)

### shilohman — 2012-04-18T03:10:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16089>

You sure had a scary experience, woah!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-18T03:26:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16097>

sounds you like had a lot going on all at once if you ask me......why did you make all these changes all at once? TSH of 22 and 2 infections of your kidney all at once......and a T2D......jesus your lucky you did not kick off. You need to bring change just slow down the adaptation of it!!!! Welcome.......I think. LOL

### Glamorama — 2012-04-18T04:26:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16113>

Wow sounds scary! I'm thinking your body has gone through major detox/withdrawal and pure old shock! I know I felt like \*\*\*\*e for the first week of Paleo eating, and I was by any means in far better health than you when I started, so I give you credit for doing this- its awesome that you're taking this step- but yes, consider the years of food abuse you put your body through... its not gonna happen without a few discomforts along the way. I hope it eases for you soon!

### Jim — 2012-04-18T07:35:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16157>

The best book I have seen for lay people to better understand diabetes is Dr Bernstein's Diabetes Solution. He does a great job is explaining everything. Getting control of your blood sugar is something you should be able to do with minimal supervision.

[http://www.diabetes-book.com/](http://http://www.diabetes-book.com/)

Another great website is Jenny Ruhl's Blood Sugar 101

[http://bloodsugar101.com/how.php](http://http://bloodsugar101.com/how.php)

Good luck!

### janagram — 2012-04-18T09:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16167>

I've heard of a "carb flu" and maybe had it the other night...nausea, headache, etc. You may be having the carb flu. And why wouldn't you? Also, sugar is a carb.....but sugar is also sugar, an addictive substance, so there are many things tormenting you. Wishing you the best of luck and may it get easier!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-18T18:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16225>

Jeebus!

Keep us posted, would love to watch progress and compare this to prior attempts at better health. Also, know that when you start CT - more detoxing to come. It's enjoyable, as it's clearly something working!

I don't think sugar detox alone can cause the severity of symptoms you've laid out here, but you have so many confounders - including an infection which is probably hard to get a handle on for any doc because of your size. Doubtful they know the difference in how that would present in such a big guy with what you've got going on.

One thing to note, your body does need time to adjust to being a fat burner. It's not and toggle switch that moves easily from A to B. Lower carbs a little slower, more each day. Switch to more useful carbs etc. For a guy your size, starting at 75 grams or even 100 isn't crazy. The nice thing is this slower approach gives your brain time to accept while your biology is changing.

Lastly, move. NOT EXERCISE in the Jillian Michaels/biggest loser sense. Slow walks and movement. Get bright sun in the day and dark at night.

### maggie — 2012-04-18T19:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16257>

> **Jim;16227 wrote:** The best book I have seen for lay people to better understand diabetes is Dr Bernstein's Diabetes Solution. He does a great job is explaining everything. Getting control of your blood sugar is something you should be able to do with minimal supervision.
>
>
>
> [http://www.diabetes-book.com/](http://http://www.diabetes-book.com/)
>
>
>
> Another great website is Jenny Ruhl's Blood Sugar 101
>
> [http://bloodsugar101.com/how.php](http://http://bloodsugar101.com/how.php)
>
>
>
> Good luck!

I agree with these two suggestions and have used both. Dr B also has a forum for diabetics who follow his low carb solution that might have some useful input. Remember that when your body is used to running at very high blood glucose levels, lower levels even when normal can feel hypo and be very uncomfortable. I really urge you to read Dr. B's book and the people on his forum, especially since you're using insulin now.

And congratulations on changing your life! You made a very courageous decision and I wish you the very best.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-04-18T20:36:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16286>

First, let me congratulate you on trying to fix your health issues. But to borrow from St. Teresa of Avila and her comments about spiritual directors - "He who has himself as a spiritual director has an arse for a spiritual director". It is true that we know our own hearts or bodies best because we are living in them. That said, the fact that your body is not in the best of shape happened at your own hands. Part of fixing it is realizing you need to a better job and knowing ALL the issues that may be related. And that is something you may not be able to do by yourself. For example as I started reading your description of what happened, I wondered "hemochromatosis??"

So I would first recommend that you find a doctor to partner with you. Second, I would make your own personal medical history and read, read, read. It will be maddening to see how much you need to read because just about every symptom can be related to many dysfunctions.

Next I would sit down and make a food log. Write down what you ate in a day - as best as you can remember - BEFORE you started the LRx. Put it in FitDay or some other online macronutrient/calorie calculator and make some notes. Then, apply the right amount of change. Now this is NOT in contradiction to the lectures Jack has given against "moderation". Your mind is full in. You are committed. You are acting with intensity. BUT you are knowledgeable now of the environment and all the risk factors you need to mitigate as you move to optimal. What is a crucible for a 18 year old healthy person moving to optimal is not the same crucible to refine an older T2D with a single kidney and a ton of weight to lose. Can you still refine gold? Yes, but you have got to do a heck of a lot more tinkering with the crucible.

So now what?

Take that list of what you used to eat. Take a similar assessment of what you were eating when you started the LRx and adjust. For example if you were eating 350 g of carbs a day and taking X amount of insulin and then went to 50g of carbs and Y amount of insulin, you need to find something in between that is sustainable - It might even be more carbs than ideal for a few days and then titrate it down by no more than a small % over the course of a week.

And certainly, you don't want to be doing LRx or CT or anything that dumps all the crap out of your body when you already have an infection. That just overstresses the system.

Now if you are having trouble accepting that your crucible isn't at the same temp or with the same ingredients as the next person, well then you have some contemplation to do as well. You WILL get there - if you do it right. And there are still great things you can change even if you can't go keto levels of carbs overnight. Motivate yourself with things like

-   banishing artificial sweetners
-   nixing grains
-   finding a new food you have never had before - (mine was Swiss Chard about 4years ago)
    drinking more water
    and all the other parts of the protocol - particularly the sleep/light control
    getting lab tests that help you assess where you are AND track progress

And yes, just 6 grams of mainlined carbs in OJ can make a difference, the body is that sensitive.

Yep, this was a tough love post. Do it, but do it right. Now go hunt down a doctor!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-18T21:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16323>

To respond directly to you on breaking the sugar addiction. No easy way. It's just hard and takes time. There is more at play in the broken brain category for you. One doesn't get to be your size without MANY factors contributing. Controlled weaning makes sense, but I think for most true addicts the cold-turkey thing is relevant. Here is the good news - it does get easier!! Your brain will rework itself! Also, artificial sweeteners are not a long term strategy.

There can be a time in your future when you are well, that you can seasonally add things in. . . the irony is once you've done what's needed to fix you, you won't want it.

Also, when someone who's always been healthy and/or thin naturally thinks they know what you need - run. They do not. Most of the time, they have zero framework to understand what you are dealing with. It took me over 40 years to realize I wasn't the wrong one. My exception to this is if they have actual people they have helped improved. Very VERY rare is that.

The concepts called "hunger" and "need to eat" are not felt/experienced the same way across all people. There are not enough words to describe these concepts. Until then, just accept you are evolved to rein supreme in the next big disaster/ice age and you should look to do what works for you.

### ineedanLR — 2012-04-19T23:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-16746>

Just a quick update and a few comments about all your posts. Thanks so much for commenting.

I hade been reducing my caloric intake and eating healthier for the past 3 months (2 months before I ended up in the ER). I had never needed insulin before, so injecting insulin was a new experience, and I'm not even sure if I'm doing it right (qty or technique).

My doctor is no help.

So, when I got home from the ER, I realised I **needed to get more aggressive about recovering, or else I wouldn't be around to much longer. This is the reason why so many changes were made at the same time-I felt my physical conditioning and a lifetime of poor eating habits mandated a more timely change to healthy eating. **So, the trip to the ER and the new medical info received as a result of the labs is the reason why so many changes were made so quickly.**

So, here's the update.
**

After my shot glass of OJ, my swelling/bulging head returned to more 'normal', not good, just much improved. I was able to sleep much better that night, not good, not normal, but I didn't wake up feeling I desperately needed to sleep again. On the night I took the shot of OJ, I also took only 30 units of insulin, I had been taking 64 units per day before that.

The extreme kicked in the gut feeling improved slightly, although the gas was still flowing in amounts that might have ended the energy crisis.......

But, the next day (yesterday), it all came back....... My doctor never returned my calls. I felt like I was gonna die again. Last night, my wife found ketosis with a google search, and it sure seems like it's my problem, especially the odd aftertaste when I burp. It's also consistent with fast weight loss and detox I'm going through now.

I have lost a ton of weight in the last 3 weeks, so perhaps ketosis is the issue. It's possible that the isue wasn't sugar withdrawal at all.

I've added some carbs to my meals (mostly low glycemic index cantaloupe), stopped taking insulin completely and plan scale down the aggressive leptin reset process (less protein, slightly more carbs for the BAB). I'm feeling like I need to detox a little more slowly. I am definitely detoxing, swelling in my arthritic legs has never been better and I am losing allot of weight very quickly!!!!

Will post an update as soon as my doctor decides to return my calls.

Art

### vlynnb — 2012-04-23T01:17:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-17922>

I hope you are feeling better but I am really worried about you. I understand that your situation motivated you to make a lot of changes at once but your situation is complicated and serious and definitely life threatening. Please take Dr. Kruse advice and get a Dr. that will respond to you, immediately. You have too many conditions that should be addressed with the best medical advice immediately, for instance your kidney problems and your blood sugar. Also please keep educating yourself so you don't make anymore near fatal mistakes. Keep us informed of your progress.

### MJ* — 2012-04-24T15:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-18337>

I'm feeling like I need to detox a little more slowly. I am definitely detoxing, swelling in my arthritic legs has never been better and I am losing allot of weight very quickly!!!!

Art I applaud your taking your health on! First, you need a new doctor, shop around!!! Second, I understand your sense of urgency, but you may feel a little better if you let your body adapt a little more slowly to these changes you want to make for yourself. I suffered carb flu for sure, but not nearly as badly as you described. I slowed down a little and that helped me stay in the game. You did not get here overnight. What are you eating? Give us a list of what you ate the last three days. So much gas does not sound normal at all...if you are eating clean you should probably be experiencing less gas.

MJ

### ineedanLR — 2012-05-07T18:40:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-21945>

> **MJ\*;18410 wrote:** I suffered carb flu for sure, but not nearly as badly as you described. I slowed down a little and that helped me stay in the game. You did not get here overnight.
>
> MJ

Thanks MJ.

I will post an update here, in a few moments.

But, what is 'carb flu'?

My condition took a turn for the worse when I started Paleo, although I was already not feeling well in general. The change to Paleo has to be a big jolt to the system, so I didn't expect the process to be without a few bumps in the road.

There's no way I can blame Paleo, I think the timing must have been coincidental. Or there are/were underlying factors that led me to my current state of sickness.

Is carb flu related to the withdrawal of sugar? If so, I believe I had it already. If carb flu is the bodies normal reaction to the transition to low carbs, where can I learn more about it?

TY.

Art

### ineedanLR — 2012-05-07T19:16:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-death-experience-while-on-paleo-leptin-reset.956/post-21960>

My primary care doctor finally surfaced, and suggested that I might have simple acid reflux related to the abrupt change in diet. He suggested I try Zantec or Prylosec (but not both). I tried Prylosec, but it gave me a rash and itchiness. I tried Zantec, it did nothing.

After another week of suffering, he finally authorized a trip to the ER for me.

At the ER, they found severely elevated lipase levels, although there was an atypical lack of swelling in the pancreas (as imaged by a CT scan). The diagnosis was pancreatitis. My kidney function was also poor, although it improved as the lipase levels declined towards normalcy. After 1 week in the hospital, my lipase levels did not quite reach the normal levels and my kidney function returned to near normal.

The hospital doctors also believed I had acid reflux, and put me on Protonix and took away all food for 5.5 days and kept me on an IV. After 5.5 days, they put me on solid foods, but the stomach pain and churning returned, it was just as bad as the day I showed up in the ER. They released me on Day 7, knowing that my acid reflux was just as bad as it had ever been.

After I got home, I slowly realized that the more food I ate, the worse I felt. And, the more insulin I took, the worse I felt. I am down to eating one extremely small meal per day, 300 to 500 calories. Last night I had a slightly bigger Dinner than 'normal' consisting of 6 small chunks cantaloupe, 3 oz chicken, 1/3 cup walnuts, 6 broccoli florets. I felt much worse than I had been feeling and even woke up feeling worse-normally I wake up feeling ok-ish initially.

I stopped taking insulin. I am drinking less than 3 cups of water per day, drinking water makes me feel worse too.

I am trying to arrange appointments with an endocrinologist, a gastroenterologist and my kidney doctor.

I'll post updates here.

Art

# Thread: Marketing comments for the Monkeys!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marketing-comments-for-the-monkeys.957/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-04-18T03:10:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marketing-comments-for-the-monkeys.957/post-16088>

I am making this comment because I really appreciate Jack and want the best for his business and the world at large.

62\. avatar CoolingWeb Says:

April 17th, 2012 at 11:32 am

If I may,

Nested comments add ons just like the one on MDA and such will do just fine. Many use WordPress like you do. The fancy Javascript one that your web master is trying to work will always give trouble across since it is never works correctly across all browsers and platforms.

Why not get the one with out any fancy stuff. They are solid and tested since they have been in use by so many already?

The less fancy the more we will use it. the more we will use it.

It is important not to have your replies to comments hidden until we click "replies". It is not productive to have to click on every comment just to see if there is a reply worth reading. Scanning comments by just scrolling is very useful and optimal.

I do user experience research in m y other life. I just would like to contribute in any small way I can to the mission.

Best Regards,

Cooling

# Thread: Optimal Hormonal Levels
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-hormonal-levels.959/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### indigogirl — 2012-04-18T05:57:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-hormonal-levels.959/post-16135>

Does anyone know where I can learn what the optimal levels for specific hormones are?

My GYN tests my hormone levels through blood work. It occurred to me that when she tells me where I am in a range, and that my levels are good, that her good may not be optimal!

### Destiny — 2012-04-18T13:06:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-hormonal-levels.959/post-16174>

> **indigogirl;16205 wrote:** Does anyone know where I can learn what the optimal levels for specific hormones are?
>
>
>
> My GYN tests my hormone levels through blood work. It occurred to me that when she tells me where I am in a range, and that my levels are good, that her good may not be optimal!

According to the book Matural Hormonal Balance by Uzzi Reiss, the test results is a good start but you need to include your symptoms of deficiency. He explains that each of us has an individual requirement for hormones. Some need higher levels of hormones other lower. Basically, he is saying you might be in the range but not doing well.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-04-18T19:28:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-hormonal-levels.959/post-16244>

> **indigogirl;16205 wrote:** Does anyone know where I can learn what the optimal levels for specific hormones are?
>
>
>
> My GYN tests my hormone levels through blood work. It occurred to me that when she tells me where I am in a range, and that my levels are good, that her good may not be optimal!

I've not been able to find real ratios either, so hoping someone will enlighten us. I have the Reiss book and he does not give exact #s, or even a numerical range, relying more on symptoms. Helpful, but I'd like to have a numerical range to use along with my blood tests and symptoms together.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-18T19:42:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-hormonal-levels.959/post-16255>

I got the following list from a post JanSz did on MDA. It was an attachment that looks like it came from a book but I don't know where. It's optimal levels not some random range levels from the lab. I believe this is a good starting point to what you are looking for:

FEMALES

Estrogen 250-437 pg/mL

Progesterone 6-28 ng/mL

Testosterone 60-76 ng/mL

DHEA-s 250-380 ug/dL

Pregnenolone 200-230

MALES

Estrogen

### janagram — 2012-04-19T02:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-hormonal-levels.959/post-16391>

Thanks for this...but there must be a difference according to one's age...but, good start.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-19T02:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-hormonal-levels.959/post-16404>

I guess it depends on how you look at it. I want to be optimal.... not optimal for my age.
